- Description: No `@Retryable` annotation found anywhere. External service calls (payment gateway, Redis, etc.) have no retry strategy. A single network hiccup causes permanent failure.
- Status: PARTIAL_SOURCE_FIXED / PAYMENT_GATEWAY_RETRY_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED / E2E_PENDING
- Expected fix direction: Add `@Retryable` to PaymentService calls, Redis operations, and external API calls with exponential backoff.
- Fix evidence (2026-06-10 06:02 UTC): The PaymentService `GENERIC_API` payment gateway create/refresh calls now retry transient HTTP/network failures with bounded exponential backoff and the same `Idempotency-Key` header/payload. Retryable statuses are 5xx, 408, 425, and 429; 4xx client errors such as 400 do not retry and surface a sanitized `HTTP <status>` error. `PaymentFlowServiceTest` covers transient 500 retry success and 400 no-retry/no-provider-detail leakage. Remaining scope: this does not yet close the broad "all services / Redis / every external API" wording.

### F3456: HIGH — Frontend swallows all API errors with console.error only

- Area: Frontend / API client, ErrorBoundary
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: `api/index.ts` catches errors and does `console.error`. `ErrorBoundary.tsx` catches render errors and does `console.error`. No user-facing error notifications, no error reporting, no retry logic. Silent failures leave users confused.
- Status: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED / E2E_PENDING 2026-06-11 18:17 UTC
- Resolution: `api/index.ts` now reports terminal API failures through `reportNonBlockingError('api.response', ...)` only after the existing transient retry, anonymous retry, and auth-refresh paths have been exhausted. It also dispatches `shop:api-error` with safe method/path/status/retry-count and a generic user-facing message, avoiding raw Axios errors, response payloads, query strings, tokens, or secrets in UI notification payloads.
- Error reporting: Added `frontend/src/utils/nonBlockingError.ts`, which masks sensitive values, rate-limits and deduplicates client reports, posts to `/errors` or configured client-error URL without credentials, and installs global `window.error` / `unhandledrejection` reporting. `ErrorBoundary.tsx` now reports render crashes via the shared reporter instead of console-only handling.
- Regression guard: `FrontendApiErrorHandlingContractTest` verifies retry-before-terminal-reporting, UI notification event dispatch, render/global error reporting, and reporter security controls. `frontend/src/utils/nonBlockingError.test.ts` covers sanitized remote dispatch, dedupe, global listener reporting, and non-error object summaries.
- Verification: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=FrontendApiErrorHandlingContractTest test` passed in the current worktree; `CI=true npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/utils/nonBlockingError.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand --testTimeout=30000` passed; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` remains blocked by the pre-existing `frontend/src/api/index.test.ts:437` `config.headers` possibly undefined error. A clean staged-state Maven run is still blocked by the known clean-HEAD review-image compile gap (`ReviewImageUrlCodec` is referenced by tracked source but remains untracked in this workspace).

### F3402: MEDIUM — Product list smart shopping guide actions overflow and are covered on tablet

- Area: Frontend / Product list responsive smart shopping guide
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4187`, static build, unauthenticated `/products?q=dog&sort=price_desc`; viewports `768x1024`, `1024x768`, `430x932`, and `740x360`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: At the `768x1024` tablet breakpoint, the product list enters a mixed layout state: the storefront fixed bottom navigation is still active, but the product list itself uses the AntD `md` two-column layout with the left filters/sidebar and a narrowed `592px` content column. The `Smart shopping guide` card keeps the full action cluster in that narrow content area, so the hero recommendation button extends off the right side of the page and the lower action is covered by the bottom nav. In the captured viewport the user sees truncated controls like `View ...`, `B...`, and a partially hidden `Quick-add picks`, making the recommended product and sorting actions hard to understand and unreliable to tap.
- Status: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED / E2E_PENDING 2026-06-11 18:42 UTC
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2116-public-tablet-codex/products-tablet768-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2116-public-tablet-codex/products-desktop1024-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2116-public-tablet-codex/products-large-mobile430-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2116-public-tablet-codex/products-landscape740-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2116-public-tablet-codex/report.json` records `.product-list__smartBar` at `rect=166,874,568,122` with `scrollWidth=731` and `clientWidth=566`, `.product-list__smartActions` at `rect=587,887,132,96` with `scrollWidth=310` and `clientWidth=132`, and `.product-list__smartPick` at `rect=587,887,310,44`, ending at `right=897` in a `768px` viewport. The same report records `.shop-nav__bottomBar` fixed at `rect=8,952,752,62`, overlapping the `Quick-add picks` button at `rect=587,939,62,44`.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/ProductList.tsx` renders the smart guide actions as one `Space` with the long `View pick + product name`, `Best deals`, and `Quick-add picks` buttons. `frontend/src/pages/ProductList.css` sets the base `.product-list__smartBar` to two grid columns and caps `.product-list__smartPick` at `max-width: 310px`; the main mobile refinements that convert the smart bar/actions to single-column/full-width run under `@media (max-width: 767px)`, while the global storefront bottom bar remains visible through the `768px` tablet viewport. That leaves a one-pixel tablet breakpoint where the product list still has a desktop/sidebar structure but mobile bottom navigation covers content.
- Resolution: Extended the smart guide action layout through the 780px bottom-nav breakpoint. At `max-width: 780px`, `.product-list__smartBar` becomes one column, `.product-list__smartActions` uses bounded two-column grid tracks, long hero/quick-add actions span the full row, and button text can wrap instead of becoming one-letter ellipses. At the exact mixed tablet band `768px-780px`, `.shop-app-shell--product-list .shop-nav__bottomBar` is hidden so the product list no longer combines desktop/tablet content with a fixed mobile bottom bar covering the smart guide.
- Regression guard: `ProductListSmartGuideResponsiveContractTest` asserts the 780px smart guide grid, long-action full-row spans, wrapped action labels, and the 768-780 product-list bottom-nav override.
- Verification: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=ProductListSmartGuideResponsiveContractTest test` passed in the current worktree. Browser E2E remains pending for `768x1024`, `780x1024`, `430x932`, `740x360`, and `1024x768`.

### F3403: MEDIUM — Product detail tablet breakpoint hides title, price, and purchase controls below the first viewport

- Area: Frontend / Product detail tablet responsive layout
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4187`, static build, unauthenticated `/products/1`; viewports `768x1024`, `430x932`, and `1024x768`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: At the exact `768x1024` tablet breakpoint, the product detail page switches to the mobile one-column layout but keeps a full-width square gallery. The sticky storefront header consumes the top 258px, then the gallery card runs from `y=258` to `y=1084`. The product title, brand, price, stock, and add-to-cart/buy controls do not start until below the visible viewport, so the first screen shows navigation plus a giant product image only. This is a product-discovery and conversion problem: a tablet user has no first-screen confirmation of product name, price, availability, or purchase path even though both smaller mobile and larger desktop layouts expose key purchase context earlier.
- Status: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED / E2E_PENDING 2026-06-11 18:59 UTC
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2116-public-tablet-codex/product-detail-tablet768-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2030-product-detail-focused-codex/product-detail-tablet768-scroll0.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2116-public-tablet-codex/product-detail-large-mobile430-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2116-public-tablet-codex/product-detail-desktop1024-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2030-product-detail-focused-codex/report.json` records the `768x1024` header at `rect=0,0,768,258`, `.product-gallery-card` at `rect=0,258,744,826`, `.product-detail-main-image` at `rect=0,258,744,744`, and thumbnails at `rect=0,1002,744,82`. The summary card starts at `rect=0,1092,744,1777`; `.product-title-block` starts at `y=1118`, `.product-price-panel` at `y=1207`, and `.product-mobile-buybar` at `y=1410`, all below the 1024px viewport. The same focused report records `430x932` with title at `y=596` and price at `y=685`, and `1024x768` with desktop summary/title/price visible beside the gallery in the first viewport.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx` renders the gallery and purchase summary as separate AntD columns (`<Col span={12}>` for the gallery, then `<Col span={12}>` for `.product-summary-card`). `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.css` applies `@media (max-width: 768px)` rules that force all `.product-detail-page .ant-col` elements to `flex: 0 0 100% !important; max-width: 100% !important`, and sets `.product-detail-main-image` to `aspect-ratio: 1 / 1`. The desktop/sticky summary treatment starts above that breakpoint, so `768px` lands in a one-column tablet state with a 744px-tall image before any commercial information.
- Expected fix direction: Add a dedicated tablet detail layout instead of treating `768px` like a phone. Options include keeping gallery and summary in two columns at 768px, capping the gallery height/aspect ratio for tablet portrait, or moving a compact title/price/purchase strip above or alongside the gallery. Verify `768x1024`, `820x1180`, `430x932`, `740x360`, and `1024x768` so the first viewport always includes product identity plus price or purchase affordance without recreating F2720's mobile buy-bar overlap.
- Resolution: Added an explicit web tablet portrait band for `768px-900px` widths with `min-height: 700px`. The detail row is restored to two bounded grid columns, AntD mobile `100%` column overrides are neutralized inside that row, the gallery image is capped to `clamp(280px, 36dvh, 360px)` instead of a full-width square, and the summary card is non-sticky in this band so title and price start beside the gallery. The compact mobile buybar is shown in-flow for tablet portrait with only Add to cart / Buy now actions, while duplicate desktop `.product-actions` are hidden in the same band to avoid overlapping or repeated purchase controls.
- Regression guard: `ProductDetailTabletResponsiveContractTest` asserts the F3403 tablet portrait media band, two-column commercial grid, capped gallery height, static in-flow buybar, hidden secondary buybar tools, and hidden duplicate desktop action row.
- Verification: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=ProductDetailTabletResponsiveContractTest test` passed in the current worktree. Browser E2E remains pending for `768x1024`, `820x1180`, `430x932`, `740x360`, and `1024x768`.

### F3404: MEDIUM — Home guest conversion cards truncate primary account/order actions on tablet

- Area: Frontend / Home unauthenticated conversion actions
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4187`, static build, unauthenticated `/`, `shop-language=en`, `currency=USD`; viewports `768x1024`, `1024x768`, and `430x932`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: The home page guest conversion band stays in a three-column card row at the `768x1024` tablet breakpoint while each card also reserves separate icon, body, and action columns. That leaves the first card body only 85px wide and the third card body only 67px wide. The Register card's explanatory text is reduced to `Track orders,` and the order card's main title renders as `Track ...` instead of `Track order`, even though the same cards become readable when stacked on `430x932` mobile. This weakens the first logged-out account/order conversion row on a common tablet size and creates an inconsistent breakpoint where the card tap targets work but their labels are not reliably scannable.
- Status: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED / E2E_PENDING 2026-06-11 19:09 UTC
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2038-home-conversion-focused-codex/tablet768-conversion-scroll1250.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2038-home-conversion-focused-codex/desktop1024-conversion-scroll1050.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2038-home-conversion-focused-codex/mobile430-conversion-scroll1000.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2038-home-conversion-focused-codex/report.json`. The focused report records the `768x1024` conversion band at `rect=16,360,736,74` with three `237px` grid tracks. The Register body is `rect=87,375,85,44` with `scrollWidth=700` and `clientWidth=85`, and its description is `scrollWidth=700` / `clientWidth=85`. The Track order title is `scrollWidth=82` / `clientWidth=67`, so it visibly truncates to `Track ...` in the screenshot. The same report records the `430x932` mobile case as a single-column `396px` grid where `Track order` has `clientWidth=327` and no title overflow, confirming the defect is the tablet three-column state rather than the content itself.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx:1186` renders the logged-out `.shopee-conversion-band` as three `button.shopee-conversion-band__card` entries, each with a fixed icon span, `.shopee-conversion-band__body`, and a separate action label. `frontend/src/pages/Home.css:637` keeps the band at `grid-template-columns: repeat(3, minmax(0, 1fr))`, while `frontend/src/pages/Home.css:644` sets every card to `grid-template-columns: auto minmax(0, 1fr) auto`. The single-column conversion-band rule only appears inside the `@media (max-width: 600px)` mobile block, so `768px` tablet portrait keeps the compressed three-card layout.
- Expected fix direction: Add a tablet breakpoint for the guest conversion band before the body column falls below readable width. Either switch to one or two columns through the bottom-nav/tablet range, move the action label below the body sooner, or let the title/action wrap without forcing a 67px text track. Verify `600px`, `768x1024`, `820x1180`, `1024x768`, and `430x932` so `Register`, `Log in`, and `Track order` remain fully readable and the secondary copy is intentionally clamped rather than abruptly cut.
- Resolution: Added a web-only `601px-900px` tablet breakpoint for `.shopee-conversion-band`. The band uses two bounded columns instead of three squeezed cards, each card moves the action label below the body to remove the narrow third text track, and the Track order card spans the full tablet row with a wider action track. Primary titles and action labels are allowed to wrap/show fully, while secondary copy is explicitly two-line clamped.
- Regression guard: `HomeConversionBandResponsiveContractTest` asserts the tablet breakpoint, two-column conversion grid, icon/body/action grid areas, readable non-ellipsized title/action labels, two-line secondary clamp, and full-row Track order tablet card.
- Verification: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=HomeConversionBandResponsiveContractTest test` passed in the current worktree. Browser E2E remains pending for `600px`, `768x1024`, `820x1180`, `1024x768`, and `430x932`.

### F3410: MEDIUM — Loaded mobile product comparison hides decision metrics behind an undiscoverable horizontal rail

- Area: Frontend / Product comparison loaded-state decision helper
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4187/compare`, static build, unauthenticated, `shop-product-compare=[1,2,3]`, `shop-language=en`, `currency=USD`; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `768x1024`, and `1024x768`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: After products are loaded into the comparison page, the `Decision helper` renders four business-critical metric cards (`Ready to buy`, `Best value`, `Top rated`, `Low stock`) in a horizontal mobile rail. On `390x844`, only the first two cards and a cropped sliver of `Top rated` are visible; `Low stock` is completely off-screen. On `320x568` and `360x740`, the same rail exposes only two cards by default. The rail hides scrollbars and sits inside an outer `.product-compare__decision` card with `overflow-x/y: hidden`, so users get no clear continuation cue and may miss rating/stock risk information before acting on the recommendation. This is not a duplicate of F2714: F2714 covers the top action buttons truncating, while this defect affects the loaded comparison decision metrics after compare data exists.
- Status: FIXED (2026-06-08 15:03 UTC — Maintainer targeted fix)
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2045-compare-coupons-codex/compare-mobile390-decision-focused.png`; `compare-compact360-decision-focused.png`; `compare-small320-decision-focused.png`; `compare-tablet768-decision-focused.png`; `compare-desktop1024.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2045-compare-coupons-codex/compare-decision-focused-report.json`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2045-compare-coupons-codex/report.json`. The focused report records `390x844` with `.product-compare__decisionGrid` at `rect=22,197,322,87`, `scrollWidth=509`, and `clientWidth=322`; item 2 (`0.0 Top rated`) starts at `x=276` and ends at `x=392` in a 390px viewport, while item 3 (`0 Low stock`) starts at `x=399`, fully off-screen. At `360x740`, the grid has `scrollWidth=477` / `clientWidth=292`, item 2 ends at `x=368` and item 3 starts at `x=375`; at `320x568`, item 2 starts at `x=260` and item 3 starts at `x=375`. The same report records `768x1024` and `1024x768` with all four cards visible, confirming the defect is the mobile rail treatment.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/ProductCompare.tsx:517` renders the decision helper as one `.product-compare__decisionGrid` containing the four metric cards. `frontend/src/pages/ProductCompare.css:747` switches `.product-compare__decisionGrid` to `display: flex; overflow-x: auto` under the small-screen density rules and gives each `.product-compare__decisionItem` a fixed `flex: 0 0 116px`. Later mobile rules at `frontend/src/pages/ProductCompare.css:941` hide the scrollbar and add only a subtle mask, while `frontend/src/pages/ProductCompare.css:1005` clips page overflow. The outer decision card remains clipped in the focused metrics (`overflowX=hidden`, `overflowY=hidden`), so the continuation state is not visually obvious.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/ProductCompare.css` keeps the mobile decision metrics in `grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr))`, removes the decision grid from hidden-scrollbar/mask/horizontal-overflow rail groups, and drops the fixed flex-basis/scroll-snap treatment from each decision metric card. `frontend/src/pages/ProductCompare.test.ts` guards the responsive CSS so the decision grid cannot regress to flex scrolling, hidden overflow, masks, pan-x touch handling, or fixed snap cards.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/ProductCompare.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Make the decision metrics fully readable by default on mobile instead of relying on hidden horizontal panning. A two-by-two grid is the clearest fit for four equal metrics at 320-430px; alternatively show an explicit carousel affordance and keep all risk-bearing metrics reachable without clipping. Verify `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `430x932`, `768x1024`, and `1024x768` with 3 compared products so `Top rated` and `Low stock` are visible or unmistakably scrollable without overlapping the bottom nav.

### F3411: MEDIUM — Mobile checkout fixed pay bar clips the primary payment CTA

- Area: Frontend / Checkout loaded-state mobile payment bar
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4187/checkout`, static build, unauthenticated guest checkout with three cart items preloaded and selected; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `768x1024`, `740x360`, and `1024x768`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: In the loaded guest checkout state, the fixed mobile pay bar puts the payable amount and the long `Create order and pay $6.85` button into a single two-column row. At `320x568` the CTA shows only the middle/right part of the label (`eate order and pay $6.`), and at `360x740` the left edge is visibly clipped while the amount also runs into the button's right edge. The `390x844` state barely fits, leaving no margin for longer currencies, translations, or higher payable amounts. This is payment-critical because the final submit action is always pinned to the bottom of the mobile checkout and its label communicates the order/payment commitment.
- Status: FIXED (2026-06-08 15:12 UTC — Maintainer targeted fix)
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2054-cart-checkout-loaded-codex/checkout-small320-bottom.png`; `checkout-compact360-bottom.png`; `checkout-mobile390-bottom.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2054-cart-checkout-loaded-codex/report.json`. The report records `.checkout-page__mobilePayBar` as fixed and visible only on small mobile: at `320x568` it is `rect=8,505,304,63` with the CTA button constrained to `rect=125,516,175,44`; at `360x740` it is `rect=8,677,344,63` with the CTA button `rect=139,688,201,44`; at `390x844` it is `rect=8,781,374,63` with the CTA button `rect=163,792,207,44`. The same bottom captures record the tablet/landscape/desktop pay bar as hidden, confirming this is the small-mobile fixed-bar layout rather than the main checkout submit button.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:2427` renders `.checkout-page__mobilePayBar` with a payable amount span and a submit `<Button>` using the full `renderSubmitWithAmount()` label. `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.css:1388` enables the bar under `@media (max-width: 560px)` as `position: fixed` and `display: grid`; `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.css:1395` sets `grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 0.9fr) minmax(150px, 1.1fr)`, while `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.css:1410` only allows the button to wrap with `white-space: normal`. The grid still leaves only 175-201px for the payment CTA on common phone widths.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.css` now applies a final `max-width: 430px` override that changes `.checkout-page__mobilePayBar` to one column, puts the payable summary above the action, makes the submit button full width, restores normal wrapping for the button label, and increases page bottom padding/scroll padding to match the taller fixed rail. `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.test.tsx` guards this source contract.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/Checkout.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅ with existing React/Router/Ant Design warning noise; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Give the mobile pay bar a stable layout that does not squeeze the final payment action into a narrow column. The safest mobile pattern is a two-row bar with the payable amount above and a full-width CTA below on `<=390px`, or a responsive grid that moves to one column when the button label cannot fit. Preserve safe-area bottom padding, keep the main form submit hidden only when the fixed bar is fully readable, and verify `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `430x932`, and `560px` widths with longer amounts/translations.

### F3412: MEDIUM — Android WebView checkout payment bar floats above hidden bottom navigation space

- Area: Frontend / Android WebView checkout fixed payment rail
- Environment: Simulated Capacitor Android WebView (`ShopTestAndroidApp` user agent plus `window.Capacitor.isNativePlatform() === true`), unauthenticated guest checkout with three cart items preloaded and selected; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, and `390x844`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: In the Android WebView checkout flow, the route correctly hides the global bottom navigation, but the fixed checkout payment rail is still offset upward by a bottom-navigation-height variable. The result is a floating white payment bar sitting well above the bottom of the viewport: at `320x568` it appears at `y=413..484`, at `360x740` it appears at `y=588..656`, and at `390x844` it appears at `y=692..760`. Because no bottom nav is visible underneath it, the rail covers checkout summary/readiness content while leaving a large unused band below. This is distinct from F3411's text-fitting issue: even if the CTA text is fixed, the WebView-specific placement still makes the payment rail look detached and obstructs the checkout review surface.
- Status: FIXED (2026-06-08 15:12 UTC — Maintainer targeted fix)
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2102-native-webview-codex/checkout-small320-viewport.png`; `checkout-compact360-viewport.png`; `checkout-mobile390-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2102-native-webview-codex/report-valid.json`; `report-supplement.json`. The valid report records `.shop-app-shell--checkout-flow` for all three checkout captures and no visible `.shop-nav__bottomBar`. It records `.checkout-page__mobilePayBar` as `position=fixed zIndex=955`: `rect=10,413,300,71` at `320x568`, `rect=10,588,340,68` at `360x740`, and `rect=10,692,370,68` at `390x844`. Hit testing at the bottom center reaches normal checkout content rather than the pay bar, confirming the pay bar is floating above the hidden bottom-nav zone instead of being pinned to the safe-area bottom.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css:1826` and `frontend/src/mobile-app.css:5093` hide `.shop-nav__bottomBar` for `.shop-app-shell--checkout-flow`. `frontend/src/mobile-app.css:5111` initially positions `.checkout-page__mobilePayBar` with only the floating action gap/safe-area bottom, but the later WebView rule at `frontend/src/mobile-app.css:9503` overrides it to `bottom: calc(var(--shop-mobile-bottom-nav-height, 74px) + 12px + env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px))`. That later rule applies the bottom-nav clearance even on the checkout route where the bottom nav has already been hidden.
- Fix: `frontend/src/mobile-app.css` now adds a later checkout-flow override that pins `.checkout-page__mobilePayBar` to `max(var(--shop-mobile-floating-action-gap, 10px), env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px))` instead of `--shop-mobile-bottom-nav-height`, while preserving safe left/right insets, route-specific content padding, and the narrow-phone one-column CTA layout. `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.test.tsx` guards that the last checkout pay-bar bottom rule no longer contains the bottom-nav height variable.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/Checkout.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅ with existing React/Router/Ant Design warning noise; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime Android WebView screenshots/device validation are queued for E2E.
- Expected fix direction: Split checkout-flow fixed-rail placement from routes that keep the global bottom nav. For `.shop-app-shell--checkout-flow`, pin `.checkout-page__mobilePayBar` to the safe-area bottom/floating action gap only, and reserve content padding for that rail height rather than `--shop-mobile-bottom-nav-height`. Keep routes with a visible bottom nav using the bottom-nav offset. Verify Android WebView `320x568`, `360x740`, and `390x844` checkout with empty/missing payment method and selected payment method states, and re-check F3411 so the CTA text remains readable after the placement fix.

### F3413: MEDIUM — Register empty-submit scroll hides the first validation errors on 320px mobile

- Area: Frontend / Register mobile validation state
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4187/register`, static build, unauthenticated, empty Register form submitted; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, and `740x360`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: On the `320x568` mobile Register page, submitting the empty form scrolls the form to a position where the first invalid controls are already above the visible viewport. The screen starts at the Email/Phone fields and the visible errors are `Enter your email` and `Enter your phone`, while the first actionable errors for `Enter phone, username or email` and password are hidden above the sticky storefront header. The same empty-submit flow is correctly positioned at `360x740` and `390x844`, so this is a narrow-phone validation/scroll offset defect rather than a generic form validation failure. It can make a new customer fix lower fields while missing the first required username/password errors.
- Status: FIXED (2026-06-08 15:21 UTC — Maintainer targeted fix)
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2116-auth-validation-codex/register-small320-errors-viewport.png`; `register-small320-errors-full.png`; `register-compact360-errors-viewport.png`; `register-mobile390-errors-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2116-auth-validation-codex/report.json`. The report records the `320x568` storefront header as sticky at `rect=0,0,320,159`, while `.register-page` is shifted to `rect=0,-226,320,690` and the register card to `rect=10,-214,300,662`. The first invalid username form item is fully off-screen at `rect=25,-89,270,75`, with its error message at `rect=25,-29,270,15`; the password item starts at `y=-14` and its input is partially hidden. At `360x740`, the first invalid item is visible at `rect=25,296,310,75`, and at `390x844` it is visible at `rect=25,303,340,75`, confirming the bad state is specific to the 320px narrow viewport.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/Register.tsx:252` renders the AntD `<Form>` with `form={form}`, `name="register"`, `onFinish={onFinish}`, and `size="large"` but no explicit failed-submit handler or `scrollToFirstError` offset that accounts for the sticky storefront header. The first required field is the username `Form.Item` at `frontend/src/pages/Register.tsx:258`, followed by password and confirm-password items. Mobile CSS in `frontend/src/pages/Register.css:961` tightens the 320px page padding, while `frontend/src/pages/Register.css:993` makes the card header sticky inside the registration card; no corresponding `scroll-margin-top` or form-error offset is provided for `.ant-form-item` / `.ant-form-item-has-error`.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/Register.tsx` now wires `onFinishFailed` to a two-frame validation scroll helper that targets the first `.register-page__card .ant-form-item-has-error`, scrolls the card container if needed, scrolls the page with a 176px mobile chrome offset, and focuses the invalid control with `preventScroll`. `frontend/src/pages/Register.css` adds mobile `scroll-padding-top` plus invalid form-item `scroll-margin-top`/`scroll-margin-bottom`, and `frontend/src/pages/Register.test.tsx` guards the source/CSS contract.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/Register.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Make register validation focus/scroll land with the first invalid field and its error visible below the sticky storefront header on narrow phones. Add a failed-submit scroll target with a header-aware offset, `scroll-margin-top` on invalid form items, or remove the nested/sticky card-header behavior that causes the first errors to be hidden at 320px. Verify empty-submit and partially filled invalid-submit states at `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, and short landscape so the first invalid field, error text, and submit button remain reachable without recreating F2855's broader landscape header issue.

### F3414: MEDIUM — Product size guide modal clips fit instructions in mobile landscape

- Area: Frontend / Product Detail size-guide modal
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4187/products/1`, static build, unauthenticated, Product Detail `Size guide` modal opened; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, and `768x1024`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: Opening `Pet size guide` in `740x360` mobile landscape leaves the modal body only 168px tall while the guidance content needs 322px. The user can read the Neck card and the top of Chest, but the rest of Chest and the entire Back length instruction are hidden behind the sticky footer and `Got it` button. The same modal is readable in `360x740` and `390x844` portrait, and the product page's broader landscape first-viewport density is already covered by F2855; this defect is specifically the short-height modal layout for sizing guidance. It is purchase-critical for apparel/collar products because users open the guide before selecting size/options.
- Status: FIXED (2026-06-08 15:42 UTC — Maintainer targeted fix)
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2130-product-interactions-codex/product-size-guide-landscape740-viewport.png`; `product-size-guide-landscape740-full.png`; `product-size-guide-small320-viewport.png`; `product-size-guide-compact360-viewport.png`; `product-size-guide-mobile390-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2130-product-interactions-codex/report.json`. The report records the `740x360` modal at `rect=110,34,520,344`, extending to `bottom=378` in a 360px viewport. Its `.ant-modal-wrap` has `scrollHeight=388` / `clientHeight=360`, and `.ant-modal-body` is `rect=134,102,472,168` with `scrollHeight=322` / `clientHeight=168`, so nearly half the guide content is below the visible body while the footer remains sticky. By contrast, the `360x740` and `390x844` size-guide captures report no modal body overflow, confirming the failure is the short landscape height.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx:1486` and `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx:1568` open the same `Size guide` action from the option header and size calculator. `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx:2213` renders the guide as an AntD `<Modal>` with `className="profile-mobile-safe-modal product-detail__sizeGuideModal"`, and `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx:2220` renders three `.pet-size-guide` instruction cards. `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.css:947` only lays those cards out as a vertical grid with 12px gaps. Global mobile modal rules in `frontend/src/App.css:2847` and `frontend/src/App.css:2858` constrain every non-confirm modal to `max-height: calc(100dvh - 28px - safe-area)` with hidden overflow, while `frontend/src/App.css:2873` makes the body scrollable and `frontend/src/App.css:2805` makes the footer sticky. There is no `product-detail__sizeGuideModal` short-height override to switch the guide to a denser/two-column layout or make the content visibly scrollable above the footer.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.css` now adds a `max-width: 860px` / `max-height: 430px` override for `.product-detail__sizeGuideModal`: tighter top/body/footer padding, a non-sticky footer, scrollable body padding, two-column `.pet-size-guide` cards, compact text, and a full-width Back-length card. `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetailSizeGuide.test.ts` guards that short-height modal contract.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/ProductDetailSizeGuide.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Add a short-height landscape treatment for the product size-guide modal. Options include making the guide cards a two-column/compact grid at `max-height: 430px`, turning the modal into a full-height scroll sheet with a clearly scrollable body and footer that does not cover content, or moving the `Got it` action into the header for short landscape. Verify `740x360`, `844x390`, `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, and `768x1024` so Neck, Chest, Back length, close, and Got it are all visible or unmistakably scrollable without overlapping the product buy bar behind the modal.

### F3415: MEDIUM — Product-list quick-add modal leaves background purchase rails visible above the mask

- Area: Frontend / Product List quick-add option modal
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4187/products?q=feeder&sort=quick-add-desc`, static build, unauthenticated, option-required product `BrightBite Puzzle Treat Spinner` opened through the product-card `Choose` quick-add action; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, and `768x1024`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: Opening the option-required quick-add modal does not visually suppress the product-list fixed purchase rails underneath it. On `320x568`, the modal correctly shows Size/Color selectors and a disabled modal `Add to cart`, but the background product-list conversion bar remains visible directly underneath with its own orange `Add to cart` button, and the global bottom navigation remains visible below that. The modal wrapper intercepts taps, so this is not a live click-through bug, but the layered purchase state is visually ambiguous: customers see a disabled add-to-cart action in the modal and an enabled-looking add-to-cart rail behind it. This is distinct from F2754, which covers the quick-preview modal, and F2755, which covers the filter drawer; this affects the direct purchase option-selection modal.
- Status: FIXED (2026-06-08 15:58 UTC; source fix + regression guard added)
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2148-product-list-quickadd-options-codex/quickadd-options-options-open-small320-viewport.png`; `quickadd-options-options-open-compact360-viewport.png`; `quickadd-options-options-open-mobile390-viewport.png`; `quickadd-options-select-open-small320-viewport.png`; `quickadd-options-options-open-tablet768-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2148-product-list-quickadd-options-codex/report.json`. The report records `320x568` with `.ant-modal-mask` at `z=1000`, `.ant-modal-wrap` at `z=1500`, and the quick-add modal at `rect=10,34,300,431`. Behind it, `.product-list__mobileConversionBar` remains visible at `rect=8,424,304,58` with `z=1241`, while `.shop-nav__bottomBar` remains visible at `rect=6,496,304,62` with `z=1250`. The modal footer is `rect=34,316,252,117`, so the visible background conversion bar starts only 9px below the modal card and shows a second `Add to cart` rail. The same report records `360x740` with the conversion bar visible at `y=596..654` and bottom nav at `y=668..730`, and `390x844` with the conversion bar visible at `y=696..754` and bottom nav at `y=772..834`. Hit tests at the bottom and conversion-bar centers return `.ant-modal-wrap`, confirming taps are blocked but the visual layering is still wrong.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/ProductList.tsx:1012` opens quick add by setting `quickAddProduct`, while `frontend/src/pages/ProductList.tsx:2502` renders the option-selection `<Modal>` with `className="profile-mobile-safe-modal product-list__quickAddModal"`. The Size/Color selects are rendered in `renderQuickAddOptions()` at `frontend/src/pages/ProductList.tsx:1025`, and their dropdowns mount to `document.body` at `frontend/src/pages/ProductList.tsx:1047`. The product-list fixed conversion rail remains part of the page while the modal is open, and `frontend/src/pages/ProductList.css:3609` only sizes the quick-add modal; it does not hide or lower `.product-list__mobileConversionBar` when `.product-list__quickAddModal` is open. Existing fixed rail rules keep `.product-list__mobileConversionBar` and `.shop-nav__bottomBar` visually above the AntD mask, while the modal wrap sits higher for pointer capture.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/ProductList.tsx` now adds the `product-list--quickAddOpen` page state while quick add is active, applies `rootClassName="product-list__quickAddModalRoot"` to the quick-add modal portal, and gives body-mounted option Select dropdowns the `product-list__quickAddPopup` class. `frontend/src/pages/ProductList.css` hides `.product-list__mobileConversionBar` while the quick-add modal is open, raises the quick-add modal mask/wrap above product-list and native app rails, and keeps quick-add Select popups above the modal layer.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/ProductList.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅ with existing React/Router act/deprecation warning noise; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Treat quick add as a blocking purchase modal. When `quickAddProduct` is set, hide the product-list mobile conversion bar and global bottom navigation, or raise the modal mask above those rails so the background purchase controls are fully dimmed/obscured. Preserve the modal wrapper's tap capture, keep body-mounted Select dropdowns above the modal content, and verify option-required and no-option quick-add states at `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `430x932`, and `768x1024` without regressing F2754/F2755 overlay behavior.

### F3442: MEDIUM — Stock Alerts mobile action bar is covered by the global bottom navigation

- Area: Frontend / Stock Alerts loaded-state mobile recovery action
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4187/stock-alerts`, static build, unauthenticated, `shop-stock-alerts` preloaded with four alerts and `/api/products/by-ids` mocked to return one low-stock direct-add product, one option-required ready product, one normal ready product, and one sold-out product; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, and `768x1024`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: When saved stock alerts include ready-to-buy products, the page renders a fixed mobile next-action bar with the primary `Add ready items` CTA. On portrait phones the global storefront bottom navigation is fixed above it and visually covers almost the entire bar. At `390x844`, only a thin red edge of the Stock Alerts action remains visible behind the nav; at `320x568`, the bottom nav hides the action row before the user can read the CTA. The DOM still contains both controls, but the higher z-index global nav is the top hit target, so the page's primary restock recovery action looks unavailable and is not reliably tappable. This is not a duplicate of F2858/F2721/F2758/F3412/F3415 because it is the `/stock-alerts` page's own fixed recovery bar conflicting with the shared storefront bottom navigation.
- Status: FIXED (2026-06-08 16:11 UTC; source fix + regression guard added)
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2206-stock-alerts-ready-codex/phone390-stock-alerts-ready-viewport.png`; `small320-stock-alerts-ready-viewport.png`; `phone360-stock-alerts-ready-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2206-stock-alerts-ready-codex/report.json`. The report records `.stock-alerts__mobileAction` and `.shop-nav__bottomBar` overlapping by `374x55` at `390x844` (`mobileAction rect=0,779,390,65`; bottom nav `rect=8,772,374,62`), `344x55` at `360x740`, and `304x55` at `320x568`. Bottom-center hit testing returns `NAV.shop-nav__bottomBar` above the `Add ready items` button and `.stock-alerts__mobileAction`, confirming the nav is visually and interactively on top.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/StockAlerts.tsx:319` renders `.stock-alerts__mobileAction` whenever alerts exist, and `frontend/src/pages/StockAlerts.tsx:331` renders its CTA button from the computed next action. `frontend/src/pages/StockAlerts.css:365` fixes `.stock-alerts__mobileAction` to `bottom: 0` with `z-index: 1000`, while `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css:1825` enables the mobile bottom nav at `max-width: 780px` and `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css:1832` fixes `.shop-nav__bottomBar` with `z-index: 1250`. The route-specific bottom-nav hide list in `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css:1826` covers product detail, checkout, and auth flows but not stock alerts. Later `frontend/src/pages/StockAlerts.css:950` adds page bottom padding, but that does not offset the fixed `.stock-alerts__mobileAction` above the global nav.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/StockAlerts.tsx` now includes `stock-alerts-page` on the route root so shared app page rules can target the current page. `frontend/src/pages/StockAlerts.css` adds a final F3442 mobile rule that offsets `.stock-alerts__mobileAction` above `--shop-mobile-bottom-nav-height`, reserves matching page bottom/scroll padding, keeps Android WebView stacking below the nav once separated, and makes the action static in short landscape so the CTA remains readable without covering the viewport.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/StockAlerts.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Coordinate the two fixed mobile surfaces on `/stock-alerts`. Either hide the global bottom navigation while the Stock Alerts mobile action bar is visible, move `.stock-alerts__mobileAction` above `--shop-mobile-bottom-nav-height` with matching content padding, or merge the restock CTA into a single bottom surface that does not compete with navigation. Verify ready, option-required, waiting-only, and empty alert states at `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `430x932`, `740x360`, and `768x1024` so the restock action and bottom navigation are both readable and tappable without recreating existing fixed-rail collisions.

### F3443: MEDIUM — Wishlist mobile action bar is covered by the global bottom navigation

- Area: Frontend / Wishlist loaded-state mobile conversion action
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4191/wishlist`, current-source CRA dev server, authenticated profile and wishlist APIs mocked with four saved items: direct-add low-stock, option-required, direct-add normal, and unavailable; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, and `768x1024`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: When the wishlist contains ready-to-cart favorites, the page renders a fixed mobile next-action bar with the primary `Add all to cart` CTA. On portrait phones the global storefront bottom navigation sits on top of the same bottom area, leaving only a sliver or upper metadata of the Wishlist action visible while the CTA is hidden behind the nav. At `390x844`, the user can see the next-action copy and tags but the actual orange `Add all to cart` button is covered by the six-tab nav; at `320x568`, the nav covers the lower action surface before the user reaches the list. Because the bottom nav has the higher stacking order and appears first in hit testing, the page's loaded-state conversion action looks unavailable even though the DOM still contains it. This is not a duplicate of F3442 because it is `/wishlist`'s own loaded-state conversion rail, with option-required and unavailable-item logic, conflicting with the shared mobile navigation.
- Status: FIXED (2026-06-08 16:15 UTC; source fix + regression guard added)
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2232-wishlist-loaded-codex/phone390-wishlist-loaded-viewport.png`; `small320-wishlist-loaded-viewport.png`; `phone360-wishlist-loaded-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2232-wishlist-loaded-codex/report.json`. The report records `.wishlist-page__mobileAction` and `.shop-nav__bottomBar` overlapping by `374x62` at `390x844` (`mobileAction rect=0,766.5,390,77.5`; bottom nav `rect=8,772,374,62`), `344x62` at `360x740`, and `304x62` at `320x568`. Both controls are fixed and interactive, but the Wishlist action uses `z-index: 1000` while the bottom nav uses `z-index: 1250`. Bottom-point hit stacks return `NAV.shop-nav__bottomBar` or its `Cart` link above `.wishlist-page__mobileAction` and the `Add all to cart` button, confirming the navigation is visually and interactively on top.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/Wishlist.tsx:546` renders `.wishlist-page__mobileAction` whenever wishlist items exist, and `frontend/src/pages/Wishlist.tsx:552` renders the computed next-action button. `frontend/src/pages/Wishlist.css:504` fixes `.wishlist-page__mobileAction` to `bottom: 0` with `z-index: 1000`, while `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css:1825` enables the mobile bottom nav at `max-width: 780px` and `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css:1832` fixes `.shop-nav__bottomBar` with `z-index: 1250`. The route-specific bottom-nav hide list in `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css:1826` only covers product detail, checkout, and auth flows, not wishlist. Later `frontend/src/pages/Wishlist.css:1210` adds page bottom padding and `frontend/src/pages/Wishlist.css:1333` protects the mobile-action button height, but neither offsets the fixed action rail above the global nav.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/Wishlist.tsx` now marks loaded wishlist views with `wishlist-page--withMobileAction` while empty views remain outside the fixed-rail spacing contract. `frontend/src/pages/Wishlist.css` adds a final F3443 mobile rule that offsets `.wishlist-page__mobileAction` above `--shop-mobile-bottom-nav-height`, reserves matching bottom/scroll padding, keeps Android WebView stacking below the nav once separated, and makes the action static in short landscape so the CTA remains readable without covering the viewport.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/Wishlist.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅ with existing React/Router act/deprecation warning noise; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Coordinate the two fixed mobile surfaces on `/wishlist`. Either hide the global bottom navigation while the Wishlist mobile action bar is visible, move `.wishlist-page__mobileAction` above `--shop-mobile-bottom-nav-height` with matching content padding, or merge the wishlist next action into a single bottom surface that does not compete with navigation. Verify ready-to-cart, option-required, unavailable-only, empty, and unauthenticated redirect states at `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `430x932`, `740x360`, and `768x1024` so the wishlist conversion action and bottom navigation are both readable and tappable without recreating existing fixed-rail collisions.

### F3515: MEDIUM — Order tracking mobile result hides return/support actions off-screen

- Area: Frontend / Order Tracking shipped result actions
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4192/track-order`, current-source CRA dev server, guest order lookup mocked for shipped order `MX-20260607-TRACK01` with tracking number, `returnable=true`, and return deadline; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, and `768x1024`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: The shipped/returnable result state renders three high-value actions in the next-action panel: `Confirm receipt`, `Request return`, and `Contact support`. On portrait phones only `Confirm receipt` is visible; `Request return` and `Contact support` are pushed entirely outside the horizontal rail with no visible scrollbar, so users can easily miss the return and support paths. At `320x568`, `360x740`, and `390x844`, the later actions have `visibleWidth=0`; at `740x360` and `768x1024` the same actions stack visibly, confirming this is a mobile portrait rail layout defect rather than missing business logic. This is separate from F3410's compare metric rail and from F3442/F3443 fixed bottom action conflicts because it affects the in-card order recovery actions after a successful tracking lookup.
- Status: FIXED (2026-06-08 16:19 UTC; source fix + regression guard added)
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2303-order-tracking-shipped-result-codex/phone390-order-tracking-shipped-full.png`; `small320-order-tracking-shipped-full.png`; `phone360-order-tracking-shipped-full.png`; `phone390-order-tracking-shipped-actions.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2303-order-tracking-shipped-result-codex/report.json`. The report records `.order-tracking-page__nextActionButtons` at `320x568` with `clientWidth=260` and `scrollWidth=778`; `Confirm receipt` is fully visible at `visibleWidth=252`, while `Request return` and `Contact support` both have `visibleWidth=0`. At `360x740`, the rail is `clientWidth=300` / `scrollWidth=898` and the two later actions again have `visibleWidth=0`. At `390x844`, the rail is `clientWidth=328` / `scrollWidth=982`, with `Confirm receipt` visible at `visibleWidth=320` and both later actions at `visibleWidth=0`. The same report records the `740x360` and `768x1024` layouts with all three actions fully visible.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/OrderTracking.tsx:624` renders `.order-tracking-page__nextActionButtons` for non-payment tracked orders. `frontend/src/pages/OrderTracking.tsx:625` renders `Confirm receipt` for shipped orders, `frontend/src/pages/OrderTracking.tsx:630` renders the return action when `order.returnable` is true, and `frontend/src/pages/OrderTracking.tsx:640` renders `Contact support`. In mobile CSS, `frontend/src/pages/OrderTracking.css:804` includes `.order-tracking-page__nextActionButtons` in horizontal overflow rails, `frontend/src/pages/OrderTracking.css:814` forces the action buttons to `display:flex` with `flex-wrap: nowrap`, `frontend/src/pages/OrderTracking.css:822` prevents AntD space items from shrinking, and `frontend/src/pages/OrderTracking.css:827` caps each button at `max-width: min(220px, 76vw)`. The narrower mobile rule at `frontend/src/pages/OrderTracking.css:1091` hides the scrollbar and applies a right-edge mask to the same button rail, so the overflowed actions are not obvious.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/OrderTracking.css` now adds a final F3515 phone-width override for `.order-tracking-page__nextActionButtons`: the next-action panel stacks into a visible one-column grid, horizontal overflow and the right-edge mask are removed, `Space` items take full width, and button text can wrap instead of clipping. This keeps the assurance action rails unchanged while making shipped/returnable recovery actions visible.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/OrderTracking.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅ with existing React/Router act/deprecation warning noise; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Do not hide order recovery actions behind an undiscoverable horizontal strip on portrait phones. Stack the shipped/returnable next-action buttons vertically, allow a two-row wrap with stable tap targets, or expose the secondary actions through a visible segmented/menu treatment. Verify shipped returnable, shipped not-returnable, pending payment, return-approved, completed, restricted-login, and support-only states at `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `430x932`, `740x360`, and `768x1024` so every available action is readable and tappable without horizontal discovery.

### F3516: MEDIUM — Profile mobile hides account actions and order filters behind no-scrollbar rails

- Area: Frontend / Profile loaded-state mobile navigation and action rails
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4193/profile?tab=orders`, current-source CRA dev server, authenticated customer profile mocked with four order states (`PENDING_PAYMENT`, `SHIPPED`, `COMPLETED` with `returnable=true`, `RETURN_APPROVED`), two saved addresses, and two pet profiles; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, and `768x1024`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: On portrait phones, Profile converts several primary account controls into hidden-scrollbar horizontal rails. The account action center shows `Pending payment` and `In transit`, but hides `After-sales` and `Pet profile ready` off-screen; the primary Profile section rail hides the `Pets` tab completely and leaves `Personal information` partially clipped; the order-status rail hides `Shipped`, `Completed`, `Returnable`, `After-sales`, and `Cancelled` filters at the default scroll position. These are not secondary decorative chips: they are the user's route into after-sale work, pet profile completion, and order filtering. The current right-edge fade is too subtle and the scrollbars are suppressed, so the page looks as if those actions do not exist.
- Status: FIXED (2026-06-08 16:24 UTC; source fix + regression guard added)
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2321-profile-loaded-codex/phone390-profile-orders-top.png`; `small320-profile-orders-top.png`; `phone360-profile-orders-top.png`; `phone390-profile-orders-full.png`; `phone390-profile-addresses.png`; `phone390-profile-pets.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2321-profile-loaded-codex/report.json`. The report records `.profile-action-center__cards` at `320x568` with `clientWidth=286` and `scrollWidth=609`; `After-sales` and `50% Pet profile ready` both have `visibleWidth=0`. At `360x740`, `Pet profile ready` remains `visibleWidth=0` and `After-sales` is only `26.625px` of a `126.90625px` card. At `390x844`, `Pet profile ready` is still `visibleWidth=0` and `After-sales` is only `54.625px` of `126.90625px`. The same report records `.profile-mobile-entry` at `320/360/390` with `Pets (2)` at `visibleWidth=0` and `Personal information` only partially visible. The `.profile-orders__tabs` rail is `clientWidth=308/348/378` versus `scrollWidth=910`; `Shipped 1`, `Completed 1`, `Returnable 1`, `After-sales 1`, and `Cancelled` have `visibleWidth=0` at all three portrait phone widths.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx:1244` renders the account action center, and `frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx:1252` renders four action cards including pending pay, in transit, after-sale, and profile completion. `frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx:1284` renders the mobile Profile tab rail with Orders, Addresses, Personal information, and Pets. `frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx:1492` renders the order-status filter rail. The mobile CSS at `frontend/src/pages/Profile.css:1688` changes `.profile-action-center__cards` into an `overflow-x:auto` flex rail with hidden scrollbars and a right mask, while `frontend/src/pages/Profile.css:1704` gives each card a fixed flex basis. `frontend/src/pages/Profile.css:1715` makes `.profile-mobile-entry` an overflow-x rail with hidden scrollbars and fixed-width pills, and `frontend/src/pages/Profile.css:1794` applies the same hidden-scrollbar/masked treatment to `.profile-orders__tabs`. Later rules at `frontend/src/pages/Profile.css:1955` and `frontend/src/pages/Profile.css:2313` keep these rails masked, horizontally scrollable, and scrollbar-free.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/Profile.css` now adds a final F3516 phone-width override for the primary Profile controls. `.profile-action-center__cards`, `.profile-mobile-entry`, and `.profile-orders__tabs` no longer use hidden horizontal overflow, scroll snapping, suppressed scrollbars, or edge masks at phone widths; the four account action cards and four section entries render as 2-column grids, and order filters wrap into visible grid rows with stable 44px tap targets.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/Profile.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Keep Profile's high-value mobile controls visible without relying on hidden horizontal discovery. Use a two-row grid for the four account action cards, render the four Profile section entries as a stable 2x2 segmented control or full-width tab stack, and wrap or group order filters so all active order states are discoverable at `320/360/390/430` widths. Verify default Orders, Addresses, Info, Pets, pending-payment, shipped, completed returnable, return-approved, empty-order, and Spanish labels across `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `430x932`, `740x360`, and `768x1024`.

### F3517: MEDIUM — Security Audit Logs mobile sticky filters slide under the admin header

- Area: Frontend / Admin Security Audit Logs mobile filtering
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4194/admin/audit-logs`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile/permissions mocked with populated audit-log and summary responses; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: The Security Audit Logs filter card becomes sticky on portrait phones so operators can refine the table while reviewing audit rows, but the sticky offset is based on a single-row header height while the mobile admin header wraps into a taller two-row surface. When the page is scrolled to the audit table, the filter card is positioned partly above the viewport and behind the higher-z-index admin header. The `Action`, `Result`, and `Resource` filters disappear; only the lower operator/date/search/export controls remain visible. Clicking the visible date range field in this stuck state focuses the input but opens the calendar dropdown outside the viewport, so operators cannot set a time range without manually scrolling away from the table. This is separate from F2761's admin summary/status rail issue because the broken surface is the audit table's filtering workflow, not the summary metrics.
- Status: FIXED (2026-06-08 16:27 UTC; source fix + regression guard added)
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2330-admin-audit-logs-codex/small320-sticky-toolbar.png`; `phone360-sticky-toolbar.png`; `phone390-sticky-toolbar.png`; `phone390-range-open.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2330-admin-audit-logs-codex/report.json`. The report records the sticky `.audit-log-page__toolbar` at `320x568` with `rect.y=-24.48`, `height=363`, and `bottom=338.52`; at `360x740` with `rect.y=-24.38`, `height=363`, and `bottom=338.63`; and at `390x844` with `rect.y=-23.56`, `height=363`, and `bottom=339.44`. In those screenshots, the mobile admin header covers the top of the card, leaving the first filters hidden. After clicking the date range at the same scroll position, the report records the `.ant-picker-dropdown` off-screen with negative vertical coordinates (`320x568: y=-2235 bottom=-1918.33`, `360x740: y=-2216 bottom=-1809.89`, `390x844: y=-2186 bottom=-1744.69`), and `phone390-range-open.png` shows focus on the field with no calendar visible.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/SecurityAuditLogManagement.tsx:1608` renders the filter card as `.audit-log-page__toolbar`, with the action/result/resource selects at `frontend/src/pages/SecurityAuditLogManagement.tsx:1610`, `1622`, and `1637`, the `RangePicker` at `frontend/src/pages/SecurityAuditLogManagement.tsx:1659`, and the table immediately below at `frontend/src/pages/SecurityAuditLogManagement.tsx:1671`. `frontend/src/pages/SecurityAuditLogManagement.css:623` adds mobile bottom clearance, then `frontend/src/pages/SecurityAuditLogManagement.css:629-637` makes the toolbar `position: sticky` with `top: 64px`/`58px` and `z-index: 8`. The shared admin header is also sticky and higher in the stack: `frontend/src/components/AdminLayout.css:177-185` allows the header to wrap with auto height on narrow widths, `frontend/src/components/AdminLayout.css:238-241` sets `.admin-layout__header` to `position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 60`, and `frontend/src/components/AdminLayout.tsx:287-319` renders the mobile menu, Back to store, Submit bug, and Log out controls that wrap into the two-row header seen in the screenshots.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/SecurityAuditLogManagement.css` now adds a final F3517 mobile override for `.audit-log-page__toolbar`: at admin mobile widths it uses `position: static !important`, `top: auto !important`, and `z-index: auto !important`, so the full filter card scrolls naturally below the variable-height admin header instead of sticking under it. `frontend/src/pages/SecurityAuditLogManagement.tsx` gives the RangePicker popup a page-specific `audit-log-page__rangePopup` class while keeping the body portal, and the page CSS constrains that mobile picker panel with viewport max-width/max-height and vertical range panels.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/SecurityAuditLogManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Coordinate the audit toolbar with the actual mobile admin header height instead of a fixed 58/64px assumption. Either remove sticky positioning for this large filter card on mobile, collapse filters into a drawer/sheet, or expose a shared admin header offset CSS variable so the toolbar sticks below the full wrapped header with enough viewport left for the table. Verify `Action`, `Result`, `Resource`, operator, date range, search, export, and date-picker popup visibility at `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `430x932`, `740x360`, and `768x1024`, including the scrolled-to-table state.

### F3518: MEDIUM — Permission Management desktop role editor hides Save/Cancel below the viewport

- Area: Frontend / Admin Permission Management role editor modal
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4195/admin/permissions`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile/permissions mocked with built-in and custom role rows; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: Opening `New role` on the desktop `1366x900` admin page creates a 720px-wide modal containing the role fields and the full allowed-page/action permission checklist. Because desktop styles do not cap the modal content height or make the body scroll independently, the modal expands to `1020px` high and pushes the footer to `y=1032`, below the 900px viewport. The `Cancel` and `OK` buttons have `visibleHeight=0`, and scrolling the modal body has no effect because the body height equals its content height. Operators can inspect many permissions but cannot submit or cancel from the visible dialog without browser/page-level workarounds. Mobile and short-landscape states use the mobile max-height/sticky-footer rules and keep the footer visible, so this is a desktop/tablet breakpoint gap rather than the existing mobile touch-target issue.
- Status: FIXED (2026-06-08 16:38 UTC; source fix + regression guard added)
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2337-admin-permissions-codex/desktop1366-new-role-modal-top.png`; `desktop1366-new-role-modal-bottom.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2337-admin-permissions-codex/report.json`. The report records `desktop1366` with modal root `rect={x:323,y:100,width:720,height:1020,bottom:1120}`, modal body `rect={x:347,y:152,width:672,height:862,bottom:1014}`, and footer `rect={x:347,y:1032,width:672,height:44,bottom:1076}`. Both footer buttons are outside the viewport after the attempted body scroll: `Cancel visibleHeight=0` and `OK visibleHeight=0`. Control viewports confirm the footer is visible under the mobile rules: `740x360`, `768x1024`, `390x844`, `360x740`, and `320x568` all record footer button `visibleHeight=44`.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/PermissionManagement.tsx:195-205` renders the role editor as an Ant Design `Modal` with `width={720}` and class `permission-management-page__modal`; `frontend/src/pages/PermissionManagement.tsx:207-230` renders three form fields plus `ADMIN_PAGE_PERMISSIONS.map(...)` as the full checkbox list inside the modal body. `frontend/src/pages/PermissionManagement.css:89-91` only sets desktop modal border radius and `overflow:hidden`, while the max-height, body scrolling, flex column, and sticky footer protections at `frontend/src/pages/PermissionManagement.css:147-158` and `frontend/src/pages/PermissionManagement.css:240-260` are scoped to `@media (max-width: 720px)`. Desktop therefore receives the growing content but none of the viewport containment applied to mobile.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/PermissionManagement.css` now applies the modal containment contract before the mobile breakpoint rules, so desktop/tablet role editors get `max-height: calc(100dvh - safe margins)`, flex-column `.ant-modal-content`, an independently scrollable `.ant-modal-body`, and a visible sticky `.ant-modal-footer` with Save/Cancel outside the body scroll region. The existing mobile-specific max-height/footer/touch rules still override where needed under `720px`.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/PermissionManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Give the role editor a viewport-bounded modal layout at all breakpoints, not only under 720px. Use `max-height: calc(100dvh - safe margins)`, a flex column modal content, independently scrollable `.ant-modal-body`, and a visible sticky footer for desktop/tablet as well. Also consider grouping the long permission checklist by domain so the desktop dialog is scannable. Verify new-role and edit-role states with the current full permission list at `1366x900`, `1024x768`, `768x1024`, `740x360`, `390x844`, and `320x568`, ensuring Save/Cancel remain visible and keyboard focus cannot land on controls hidden below the viewport.

### F3519: MEDIUM — Notification Broadcast mobile readiness rail hides the conversion-hook check

- Area: Frontend / Admin Notification Broadcast readiness checklist
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4196/admin/notifications`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `notifications:broadcast` permission plus mocked support unread-count and broadcast APIs; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: The broadcast page tells admins to check four readiness signals before sending: title, useful content, next-step link, and conversion hook. On portrait phones, the checklist becomes a hidden-scrollbar horizontal rail, and the fourth `Conversion hook` tag is completely outside the initial viewport at `320x568` and `360x740`; at `390x844`, only a sliver of the tag is visible. This makes the quality gate look like a three-check process and hides the conversion-hook requirement before the admin reaches the broadcast composer. Landscape, tablet, and desktop layouts show all four checks, so the defect is the mobile portrait rail treatment rather than missing readiness logic.
- Status: FIXED (2026-06-08 16:43 UTC; source fix + regression guard added)
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2345-admin-notifications-codex/phone360-empty-top.png`; `small320-empty-top.png`; `phone390-empty-top.png`; `phone360-template-top.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2345-admin-notifications-codex/report.json`. The report records `.notification-readiness__checks` at `320x568` with `clientWidth=268` and `scrollWidth=511`; `Clear title` and `Useful content` are fully visible, `Next-step link` is partially visible at `77.97px` of `115.69px`, and `Conversion hook` has `visibleWidth=0` of `135.02px`. At `360x740`, the rail is `clientWidth=308` / `scrollWidth=511`, and `Conversion hook` remains `visibleWidth=0`. At `390x844`, the rail is `clientWidth=334` / `scrollWidth=511`, and `Conversion hook` is only `22.28px` of `135.02px`. The same report records `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900` with all four checks fully visible.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/NotificationManagement.tsx:114-138` renders `.notification-readiness`, the `readinessSignals.readyCount`, and all four readiness tags including `frontend/src/pages/NotificationManagement.tsx:134-135` for `checkHook`. The mobile CSS at `frontend/src/pages/NotificationManagement.css:262-285` changes `.notification-readiness__checks` into a nowrap horizontal scrolling rail under `560px`, while `frontend/src/pages/NotificationManagement.css:287-293` keeps each tag as `flex: 0 0 auto`. Later `frontend/src/pages/NotificationManagement.css:341-348` suppresses the rail scrollbar with `scrollbar-width: none` and hidden WebKit scrollbars, leaving no visible affordance that the fourth readiness gate exists off-screen.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/NotificationManagement.css` now adds a final F3519 phone-width override for `.notification-readiness__checks`: the readiness checks render as a visible two-column grid, horizontal overflow and scroll snapping are removed, hidden-scrollbar treatment is undone, and each AntD tag takes the full grid cell width with wrapping text and a stable 44px minimum target.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/NotificationManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Keep all four broadcast readiness signals visible by default on portrait phones. Prefer a stable 2x2 grid or wrapping tag group for the readiness checks; if horizontal overflow remains, add an obvious affordance and avoid hiding required checks at the default scroll position. Verify empty compose, template inserted, confirmation popover, and long localized label states at `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `430x932`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900` so title/content/link/conversion-hook status is always readable before sending.

### F3520: HIGH — Config Center mobile properties editor is clipped by a fixed-width card

- Area: Frontend / Admin Config Center properties editor
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4197/admin/config-center`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `config-center`, `config-center:apply`, and `config-center:publish` permissions plus a populated Nacos snapshot containing long runtime property keys, masked sensitive values, warnings, parsed properties, and effective runtime values; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: On portrait phones, the Config Center's primary `Properties content` editor is wider than both the admin content area and the viewport. The form grid/card expands to 490px at `320x568` and `360x740`, and to 526px at `390x844`; the textarea itself is 460px/496px wide. The page-level horizontal overflow is clipped, and the document reports no horizontal scroll recovery, so the right side of production configuration lines is simply cut off. Long keys such as traffic-rate-limit, IP-blacklist path prefixes, and masked secret values cannot be read or edited reliably before Apply/Publish. This is separate from the older ConfigCenter confirmation, permission, touch-target, i18n, and masked-secret workflow issues because the failure is the visible editing surface itself.
- Status: FIXED (2026-06-08 16:51 UTC; source fix + regression guard added)
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2355-admin-config-center-codex/phone360-editor-visible.png`; `small320-editor-visible.png`; `phone390-editor-visible.png`; `phone360-full.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2355-admin-config-center-codex/report.json`. The report records `320x568` with page width `290`, `.config-center__grid` width `490`, editor card width `490`, and `.config-center__editor` `x=28 width=460 right=488` while the document remains `clientWidth=320 scrollWidth=320`. At `360x740`, page width is `330`, the grid/card stay `490`, and the editor is again `x=28 width=460 right=488` while the document is `360/360`. At `390x844`, page width is `360`, grid/card width is `526`, and the editor is `x=30 width=496 right=526` while the document is `390/390`. The screenshots show the textarea content hard-cut at the right edge with no visible horizontal scroll affordance.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/ConfigCenter.tsx:259-304` renders the publish-target card and the `Properties content` editor inside the same `.config-center__grid`; `frontend/src/pages/ConfigCenter.tsx:295-301` renders the textarea as `.config-center__editor`. `frontend/src/pages/ConfigCenter.css:8-12` sets the page to `overflow-x: clip`, `frontend/src/pages/ConfigCenter.css:67-70` gives the grid fixed minimum tracks, and `frontend/src/pages/ConfigCenter.css:87-92` styles the editor without a mobile width reset. The mobile rules at `frontend/src/pages/ConfigCenter.css:136-188` reduce editor height/font size but do not constrain the grid/card to the portrait viewport, while the later containment rules at `frontend/src/pages/ConfigCenter.css:388-405` omit `.config-center__editor` and do not stop the editor card from retaining the oversized width seen in the screenshots.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/ConfigCenter.css` now adds a final F3520 mobile override for the primary editor. `.config-center__grid` is forced to a single `minmax(0, 1fr)` column; `.config-center__editorCard`, the card body, `.config-center__contentItem`, the AntD Form.Item/control wrappers, and `textarea.config-center__editor` are all constrained to `width: 100%`, `min-width: 0`, and `max-width: 100%`. The textarea remains vertically resizable but owns its own horizontal/vertical overflow and wraps long property lines, so the page/card no longer expands into clipped viewport space.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/ConfigCenter.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Make the Config Center edit surface fit the visible admin content width on phones. Collapse `.config-center__grid` to a true single column with `minmax(0, 1fr)`, force the card/body/editor to `width: 100%` and `max-width: 100%`, and avoid page-level clipping that hides required text with no recovery path. If long property lines need horizontal panning, provide it inside the textarea/editor itself with a visible affordance, not by letting the whole card overflow. Verify loaded snapshot, sensitive-key warning, edit-focused, apply-confirmation, publish-confirmation, parsed table, and effective-values states at `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `430x932`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and desktop.

### F3521: MEDIUM — Announcement editor mobile date picker opens off-screen above the modal

- Area: Frontend / Admin Announcement editor scheduling controls
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4199/admin/announcements`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `announcements:write` and `announcements:delete` permissions plus active, scheduled, expired, inactive, and linked announcement data; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: Opening `New announcement` on portrait phones and tapping `Starts at` renders the Ant Design date/time picker through a body-level portal above the editor modal, but the popup is positioned partly outside the viewport. At `320x568`, only the lower `109px` of a `295px` picker remains visible; at `360x740`, the picker starts at `y=-79` and loses the month header/top rows; at `390x844`, the picker still starts at `y=-7`, visibly clipped at the top edge. This hides the month navigation and part of the day grid needed to schedule storefront announcements, while desktop and tablet show the date picker fully. This is separate from the existing announcement touch-target and validation issues because the affected control is present and tappable, but its calendar overlay is not usable after it opens.
- Status: FIXED (2026-06-08 16:58 UTC; source fix + regression guard added)
