- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0020-admin-announcements-codex/small320-date-picker.png`; `phone360-date-picker.png`; `phone390-date-picker.png`; `desktop1366-date-picker.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260608T0020-admin-announcements-codex/report.json`. The report records `.ant-picker-dropdown` at `320x568` with `rect={x:8,y:-186,width:304,height:295.36,bottom:109.36}`, `visibleHeight=109.36`; at `360x740` with `rect={x:8,y:-79,width:344,height:384.8,bottom:305.8}`, `visibleHeight=305.8`; and at `390x844` with `rect={x:8,y:-7,width:374,height:420,bottom:413}`, `visibleHeight=413`. Control viewports confirm the overlay is fully visible outside the narrow-phone state: `tablet768` records `y=12 height=362 visibleHeight=362`, and `desktop1366` records `y=206 height=358 visibleHeight=358`.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/AnnouncementManagement.tsx:446-459` renders the editor as an Ant Design `Modal` with `width={720}` and class `announcement-management-page__editorModal`. `frontend/src/pages/AnnouncementManagement.tsx:498-543` renders `startsAt` and `endsAt` `DatePicker` fields with `showTime`, `classNames={mobilePopupClassNames}`, and `getPopupContainer={() => document.body}`, so the date popup is positioned relative to the body rather than within the scrollable modal body. `frontend/src/pages/AnnouncementManagement.css:157-160` sizes the date inputs, and `frontend/src/pages/AnnouncementManagement.css:409-445` adds a mobile portal guard for the modal itself, but there is no matching viewport guard for `.ant-picker-dropdown.shop-mobile-popup-layer` or mobile date-picker placement.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/AnnouncementManagement.tsx` now applies the scoped `announcement-management-page__datePopup` class to both Starts at and Ends at DatePickers and sets `placement="bottomLeft"` while keeping the body portal. `frontend/src/pages/AnnouncementManagement.css` adds a final F3521 mobile override that makes `.announcement-management-page__datePopup` a fixed viewport-safe popup with safe-area top/left/right bounds, `max-height: calc(100dvh - safe margins)`, scrollable picker panel overflow, and `z-index: 2200 !important` so the calendar/time controls remain above the editor modal.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/AnnouncementManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Keep announcement scheduling overlays inside the visible phone viewport. Use mobile-specific picker placement or `getPopupContainer` tied to the modal body, add a constrained `.shop-mobile-popup-layer.ant-picker-dropdown` max-height/position rule, or convert the date/time chooser to a modal/sheet on narrow viewports. Verify `Starts at` and `Ends at` pickers for add/edit announcement flows at `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `430x932`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`, including scrolling the modal body before opening the picker.

### F3522: MEDIUM — Logistics Carrier readiness panel clips risk cards at tablet and landscape widths

- Area: Frontend / Admin Logistics Carrier readiness panel
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4200/admin/logistics-carriers`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `logistics-carriers`, `logistics-carriers:write`, and `logistics-carriers:delete` permissions plus carrier rows containing active/inactive carriers, a missing tracking code, duplicate tracking codes, duplicate sort orders, and long carrier names; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: At the `740x360` phone-landscape and `768x1024` tablet breakpoints, the Logistics Carrier `Carrier health` panel keeps a three-column desktop grid with a fixed `320px` minimum metrics column. Inside the admin shell, that grid extends past the visible content edge while `.logistics-carrier-page` clips horizontal overflow. The first status cards stay visible, but the duplicate-code card is cut at the right edge and the sort-conflict card is either mostly or completely hidden. Because this breakpoint does not turn the health cards into a scroll container, and the document reports no horizontal scroll recovery, admins can miss fulfillment-readiness warnings before they ever reach the carrier table. This is separate from the generic mobile admin table issue (F2718) because the failure is the pre-table readiness panel, and separate from the broad portrait summary-rail finding (F2761) because this state is a 641-768px grid/min-width clipping defect rather than an intentional portrait horizontal rail.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0030-admin-logistics-carriers-codex/landscape-740-top.png`; `tablet-768-top.png`; `landscape-740-scrolled.png`; `tablet-768-scrolled.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260608T0030-admin-logistics-carriers-codex/report.json`. The report records `740x360` with `.logistics-carrier-page__healthGrid` at `x=506,width=320,right=826` in a `740px` viewport; its duplicate-code card is partially clipped at `x=671,width=72.5,right=743.5`, and the sort-conflict card starts outside the viewport at `x=753.5,width=72.5,right=826`. At `768x1024`, the same grid sits at `x=528,width=320,right=848`, placing the sort-conflict card at `x=775.5,width=72.5,right=848` outside the viewport. In both cases the document remains `scrollWidth=clientWidth`, and the health grid itself reports `clientWidth=scrollWidth=320`, so there is no horizontal page or panel scroll path to reveal the clipped warnings.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/LogisticsCarrierManagement.tsx:233-270` renders the `Carrier health` copy, score, active carriers, missing codes, duplicate codes, and sort conflicts inside `.logistics-carrier-page__health` / `.logistics-carrier-page__healthGrid`. `frontend/src/pages/LogisticsCarrierManagement.css:22-25` defines the health panel as `grid-template-columns: minmax(240px, 1.2fr) 116px minmax(320px, 1.8fr)`, and `frontend/src/pages/LogisticsCarrierManagement.css:183-184` clips page-level horizontal overflow. The single-column health layout only starts at `frontend/src/pages/LogisticsCarrierManagement.css:136-148` under `640px`, while the horizontal-card treatment is limited to `frontend/src/pages/LogisticsCarrierManagement.css:267-275` under `560px`; therefore the 641-768px admin/tablet range keeps the oversized desktop grid but has no recovery affordance.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/LogisticsCarrierManagement.css` now adds a final F3522 `@media (max-width: 900px)` block that switches `.logistics-carrier-page__health` to a true `minmax(0, 1fr)` single column, aligns the score block to the content edge, and forces `.logistics-carrier-page__healthGrid` back to a visible 2x2 grid with `width: 100%`, `min-width: 0`, no negative margins, no padding rail, no overflow clipping/auto rail, no scroll snapping, and no fade mask. The metric cards now reset the old phone rail flex-basis/snap behavior so active carriers, missing codes, duplicate codes, and sort conflicts remain visible before the table at tablet, phone-landscape, and portrait widths.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/LogisticsCarrierManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Add a middle breakpoint for the Logistics Carrier health panel before the admin shell width becomes too narrow. Collapse the health panel to one column or a two-row layout, make the health metrics a visible 2x2 grid, and remove the fixed `320px` third-column requirement at tablet/landscape widths. Verify `740x360`, `768x1024`, `820x1180`, `390x844`, and desktop states with missing-code, duplicate-code, and duplicate-sort fixtures so all readiness warnings are visible before the table.

### F3523: MEDIUM — Pet Gallery delete confirmation popup is clipped off-screen on landscape/tablet

- Area: Frontend / Admin Pet Gallery Management destructive confirmation
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4200/admin/pet-gallery`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `pet-gallery` and `pet-gallery:delete` permissions plus paginated gallery rows containing user uploads, seed photos, a deleted photo, unknown status/source values, large files, long filenames, long usernames, IPv4/IPv6 metadata, and image thumbnails; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: Deleting a gallery row with a long original filename opens a body-mounted Popconfirm that grows to the full filename/description width at phone-landscape and tablet breakpoints. At `740x360`, the popup is `740px` wide but begins at `x=492`, leaving only the left third visible; at `768x1024`, it is `768px` wide and begins at `x=486`. The visible part shows the title/description, but the `Cancel` and `Delete` buttons are off-screen to the right, so the destructive action cannot be confirmed or cancelled from the visible viewport. Portrait phones use the mobile popup guard and keep the confirmation visible, and desktop is mostly visible; the failure is the narrow-admin/tablet placement and unconstrained long filename text. This is separate from the generic admin table horizontal-discovery issue (F2718) because the row action has already been reached and the blocking confirmation itself is unusable, and separate from pet-gallery delete permission/touch-target findings because the affected controls exist but are positioned outside the viewport.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0040-admin-pet-gallery-codex/landscape-740-delete-popconfirm.png`; `tablet-768-delete-popconfirm.png`; `phone-390-delete-popconfirm.png`; `desktop-1366-delete-popconfirm.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260608T0040-admin-pet-gallery-codex/report.json`. The report records the Popconfirm at `740x360` with `rect={x:492,y:144,width:740,height:146,bottom:290}` and `visibleRatio=0.34`, while the document temporarily expands to `scrollWidth-clientWidth=492`; at `768x1024`, it records `rect={x:486,y:628,width:768,height:146,bottom:774}` and `visibleRatio=0.37`, with `scrollWidth-clientWidth=486`. Control viewports confirm this is not universal: `390x844` records the popup at `x=16,width=374,visibleRatio=1.00`, and `1366x900` records `x=481,width=901.4,visibleRatio=0.98`.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/PetGalleryManagement.tsx:278-305` renders the action column as `fixed: 'right'` and wraps `Remove` in an Ant Design `Popconfirm`. `frontend/src/pages/PetGalleryManagement.tsx:285-294` builds the title and description from the full `originalFilename` / `username` label, and `frontend/src/locales/en.json:2446` plus `frontend/src/locales/en.json:2453` include that full `{photo}` string in both confirmation copy lines. `frontend/src/pages/PetGalleryManagement.css:101-106` only sets a mobile min-height for popup buttons, while the global popup guard in `frontend/src/App.css:2633-2655` constrains select/cascader/picker portals but does not force body-mounted Popconfirm overlays to `left/right` bounds or a wrapping max-width at the 641-780px admin breakpoints.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/PetGalleryManagement.tsx` now scopes the delete Popconfirm with `shop-mobile-popup-layer pet-gallery-management-page__deletePopconfirm`, explicitly portals it to `document.body`, and uses `placement="left"` so the overlay opens away from the fixed right action column. `frontend/src/pages/PetGalleryManagement.css` adds a final F3523 `@media (max-width: 900px)` block that forces the page-specific Popconfirm between safe left/right viewport bounds, caps it to `calc(100vw - 24px)`, constrains the inner Popover/Popconfirm wrappers to `max-width: 100%`, wraps long title/description text, and lets the Cancel/Delete buttons wrap inside the visible overlay with stable touch height.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/PetGalleryManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Constrain Pet Gallery destructive confirmations to the visible viewport at narrow-admin widths. Give the Popconfirm a page-specific class with `max-width: calc(100vw - 24px)`, forced word wrapping for long filenames, and left/right positioning that keeps the action buttons visible, or shorten/truncate the title while moving full metadata into wrapped body text. Verify long filenames and long usernames at `740x360`, `768x1024`, `820x1180`, `390x844`, and desktop, including that `Cancel` and `Delete` remain visible and tappable without horizontal page scroll.

### F3524: MEDIUM — Review Management mobile health metrics hide reply backlog and approval counts

- Area: Frontend / Admin Review Management mobile health metrics
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4200/admin/reviews`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `reviews`, `reviews:moderate`, `reviews:reply`, and `reviews:delete` permissions plus paginated review rows containing pending, approved, hidden, and unknown statuses, low ratings, reviews needing replies, review images, long product names, and long customer names; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, and `768x1024`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: On portrait phones, the `Review health` panel exposes the first two metrics (`avg. rating` and `low rating`) but hides the moderation backlog metrics behind a no-scrollbar horizontal rail. At `360x740`, the `need reply` card is clipped to a narrow sliver and the `approved` card is fully off-screen; at `390x844`, `need reply` is still partially clipped and `approved` remains fully hidden. These are core queue-management signals for deciding whether moderators need to respond or whether approvals are flowing, but the page gives no visible scrollbar or explicit affordance that more health cards exist. This is separate from the generic admin table horizontal-discovery issue (F2718) because the table has not been reached yet, and separate from the older Review Management summary-scope findings because the numbers are present in the DOM but hidden by the responsive layout.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0050-admin-reviews-codex/phone-360-top.png`; `phone-390-top.png`; `small-320-top.png`; `phone-360-scrolled-toolbar.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260608T0050-admin-reviews-codex/report.json`. The report records `.review-ops-panel__metrics` at `320x568` with `clientWidth=268` and `scrollWidth=576`, at `360x740` with `clientWidth=308` / `scrollWidth=576`, and at `390x844` with `clientWidth=334` / `scrollWidth=576`; all three mobile states also record `scrollbarHidden=true`. The overflow samples place the `need reply` card at `x=294,width=132,right=426` in the 360px viewport and the `approved` card at `x=434,width=132,right=566`, confirming the last two metrics are not fully visible by default.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/ReviewManagement.tsx:403-430` renders the `Review health` copy and four metrics: average rating, low rating, needs reply, and approved reviews. `frontend/src/pages/ReviewManagement.css:49-52` starts the metrics as a four-column grid, while the phone rules at `frontend/src/pages/ReviewManagement.css:212-228` and `frontend/src/pages/ReviewManagement.css:509-518` convert the metrics to a horizontally scrolling flex rail, add a fade mask, and hide the scrollbar. This makes required moderation state depend on undiscoverable horizontal panning.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/ReviewManagement.css` now adds a final F3524 `@media (max-width: 560px)` block that keeps `.review-ops-panel__metrics` as a visible two-by-two grid, restores full width/min-width containment, removes the negative-margin/padded horizontal rail, disables scroll snapping, removes the fade mask, restores normal scrollbar behavior, and resets the metric cards out of the fixed-basis flex rail. Average rating, low rating, need reply, and approved counts now remain visible by default before the admin table.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/ReviewManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Keep all four review health metrics visible by default on portrait phones. Use a stable 2x2 grid or single-column stack for `avg. rating`, `low rating`, `need reply`, and `approved`, or provide an explicit horizontal-scroll affordance with enough visible trailing card width to signal more content. Verify `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `430x932`, `740x360`, and `768x1024` with high `NEEDS_REPLY` / `APPROVED` counts so moderation backlog and approval flow remain readable before the table.

### F3525: MEDIUM — Brand Management health panel clips trust-risk cards at tablet and landscape widths

- Area: Frontend / Admin Brand Management health panel
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4200/admin/brands`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `brands`, `brands:write`, and `brands:delete` permissions plus brand rows containing long names/URLs, missing logos, missing websites, weak descriptions, duplicate sort orders, inactive brands, and an unknown status value; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: At the `740x360` phone-landscape and `768x1024` tablet breakpoints, the Brand Management `Brand trust health` panel keeps the desktop three-column grid and a fixed `320px` metrics column. Inside the admin shell, that metrics grid extends past the visible content edge while `.brand-management-page` clips horizontal overflow. The active-brand and missing-logo cards remain visible, but the missing-website card is cut at the right edge and the weak-description card is mostly or fully hidden. Because the panel is above the table and the document exposes no horizontal scroll recovery, brand operators can miss storefront trust risks before reviewing the rows. This is separate from the generic admin table horizontal-discovery issue (F2718) because the clipped controls are pre-table health metrics, and separate from the broad summary-rail issue (F2761) because this is a 641-768px desktop-grid/min-width clipping defect rather than the portrait-phone horizontal rail pattern.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0100-admin-brands-codex/landscape-740-top.png`; `tablet-768-top.png`; `landscape-740-scrolled-toolbar.png`; `tablet-768-scrolled-toolbar.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260608T0100-admin-brands-codex/report.json`. The report records `740x360` with `.brand-management-page__healthGrid` at `x=530,width=320,right=850` in a `740px` viewport; the missing-website card runs from `x=695` to `right=768`, and the weak-description card runs from `x=778` to `right=850`. At `768x1024`, the same grid sits at `x=552,width=320,right=872`, placing the weak-description card entirely outside the viewport at `x=800,width=72.5,right=872`. In both cases the document remains `scrollWidth=clientWidth`, and the health grid itself reports `clientWidth=scrollWidth=320`, so there is no horizontal page or panel scroll path to reveal the clipped metrics.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/BrandManagement.tsx:387-425` renders the `Brand trust health` copy, trust score, active brands, missing logos, missing websites, and weak descriptions inside `.brand-management-page__health` / `.brand-management-page__healthGrid`. `frontend/src/pages/BrandManagement.css:22-24` defines the panel as `grid-template-columns: minmax(240px, 1.2fr) 116px minmax(320px, 1.8fr)`, and `frontend/src/pages/BrandManagement.css:60-64` renders the four metrics as one row. The single-column health layout only starts at `frontend/src/pages/BrandManagement.css:155-179` under `640px`, while page-level overflow is clipped at `frontend/src/pages/BrandManagement.css:214-216` and again for mobile at `frontend/src/pages/BrandManagement.css:476-485`; therefore tablet/short-landscape widths keep the oversized desktop grid without a recovery affordance.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/BrandManagement.css` now adds a final F3525 `@media (max-width: 900px)` block that switches `.brand-management-page__health` to a true `minmax(0, 1fr)` single column, aligns the score block to the content edge, and forces `.brand-management-page__healthGrid` to a visible 2x2 grid with `width: 100%`, `min-width: 0`, no negative margins or padding rail, no overflow clipping, no scroll snapping, and no fade mask. The metric cards reset out of any rail-style flex behavior so active brands, missing logos, missing websites, and weak descriptions remain visible before the table at tablet, phone-landscape, and portrait widths.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/BrandManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Add a middle breakpoint or container-query rule for the Brand Management health panel before the admin shell content becomes too narrow. Collapse the health section to one column, make the four metrics a visible 2x2 grid, or remove the fixed `320px` metrics-column requirement at tablet/landscape widths. Verify `740x360`, `768x1024`, `820x1180`, `390x844`, and desktop states with missing-logo, missing-website, and weak-description fixtures so all trust-health metrics remain visible before the table.

### F3526: MEDIUM — Category Management readiness panel clips image/i18n risk cards at tablet and landscape widths

- Area: Frontend / Admin Category Management readiness panel
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4200/admin/categories`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `categories`, `categories:write`, and `categories:delete` permissions plus a multi-level category tree containing long taxonomy labels, missing images, missing English/Spanish localized names, weak descriptions, and root/leaf categories; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: At the `740x360` phone-landscape and `768x1024` tablet breakpoints, the `Category tree readiness` panel keeps the desktop three-column grid and a fixed `320px` metrics column. Inside the admin shell, the metrics grid extends past the visible content edge while `.category-management-page` clips horizontal overflow. The root/leaf cards remain readable, but the `Missing images` card is cut at the right edge and the `i18n gaps` card is mostly or fully hidden. These two cards are the risk signals that tell catalog operators which taxonomy branches need merchandising imagery or localized names, but the panel offers no horizontal page or card scroll path to recover them. This is separate from the generic admin table horizontal-discovery issue (F2718) because the clipped content is the pre-table readiness panel, and separate from the broad summary-rail issue (F2761) because this is the 641-768px desktop-grid/min-width breakpoint rather than the portrait-phone rail pattern.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0110-admin-categories-codex/landscape-740-scrolled-toolbar.png`; `tablet-768-top.png`; `landscape-740-top.png`; `tablet-768-scrolled-toolbar.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260608T0110-admin-categories-codex/report.json`. The report records `740x360` with `.category-management-page__healthGrid` at `x=530,width=320,right=850` in a `740px` viewport; the `Missing images` card runs from `x=695` to `right=768`, and the `i18n gaps` card runs from `x=778` to `right=850`. At `768x1024`, the grid sits at `x=552,width=320,right=872`; `Missing images` runs from `x=717` to `right=790`, and `i18n gaps` starts outside the viewport at `x=800,right=872`. In both cases the document remains `scrollWidth=clientWidth`, and the health grid itself reports `clientWidth=scrollWidth=320`, so there is no horizontal recovery.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/CategoryManagement.tsx:424-461` renders the `Category tree readiness` copy, tree score, root categories, shop-ready leaves, missing images, and localization gaps inside `.category-management-page__health` / `.category-management-page__healthGrid`. `frontend/src/pages/CategoryManagement.css:22-24` defines the panel as `grid-template-columns: minmax(240px, 1.2fr) 116px minmax(320px, 1.8fr)`, and `frontend/src/pages/CategoryManagement.css:60-64` renders the four metrics in one row. The single-column layout only starts at `frontend/src/pages/CategoryManagement.css:156-179` under `640px`, while page-level overflow is clipped at `frontend/src/pages/CategoryManagement.css:210-212` and `frontend/src/pages/CategoryManagement.css:475-484`; the later visible 2x2 health-grid guard at `frontend/src/pages/CategoryManagement.css:537-556` only applies under `720px`, leaving the 740/768 admin breakpoints oversized.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/CategoryManagement.css` now adds a final F3526 `@media (max-width: 900px)` block that switches `.category-management-page__health` to a true `minmax(0, 1fr)` single column, aligns the score block to the content edge, and forces `.category-management-page__healthGrid` to a visible 2x2 grid with `width: 100%`, `min-width: 0`, no negative margins or padding rail, no overflow clipping, no scroll snapping, no fade mask, and normal scrollbar behavior. The real `.category-management-page__healthItem` cards are reset out of the previous flex rail so root categories, shop-ready leaves, missing images, and localization gaps remain visible before the table at tablet, phone-landscape, and portrait widths.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/CategoryManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Add a middle breakpoint or container-query rule for Category Management before the admin shell content becomes too narrow. Collapse the health section to one column, use a visible 2x2 grid for all four metrics, or remove the fixed `320px` metrics-column requirement at tablet/landscape widths. Verify `740x360`, `768x1024`, `820x1180`, `390x844`, and desktop states with missing image and localization-gap fixtures so all readiness metrics remain visible before the table.

### F3527: HIGH — Category parent TreeSelect opens behind the editor modal on mobile/tablet

- Area: Frontend / Admin Category Management editor parent selector
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4200/admin/categories`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `categories`, `categories:write`, and `categories:delete` permissions plus a multi-level category tree with long parent/child labels; editor opened from `Add root category`; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: Opening `Add category` and tapping `Parent category` on phone and tablet widths creates the body-mounted TreeSelect dropdown, but the dropdown renders below the mobile Ant Design modal layer. The dropdown's DOM rectangle is present and sized, but visually the category tree is covered by the modal form fields/sticky footer, and hit tests inside the dropdown rectangle return modal textarea/label/header elements instead of tree options. At `360x740` and `390x844`, no parent options are visible under the input; at `740x360`, the tree is pushed toward the top-left and is still below the modal layer; at `768x1024`, the dropdown area is covered by the form body. Operators therefore cannot choose a parent category when creating/moving taxonomy branches on mobile/tablet. Desktop is the control state: the same dropdown sits above the modal (`modalWrap z-index=1000`, dropdown `z-index=1150`) and hit tests return `.ant-select-tree` options. This is separate from QA F1208 because that existing issue is a Checkout payment-method CSS z-index risk, while this is an observed Category Management TreeSelect failure with runtime hit-test evidence.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0110-admin-categories-codex/phone-360-parent-tree-select.png`; `phone-390-parent-tree-select.png`; `landscape-740-parent-tree-select.png`; `tablet-768-parent-tree-select.png`; `desktop-1366-parent-tree-select.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260608T0110-admin-categories-codex/report.json`. The report records the mobile/tablet modal wrapper at `zIndex=1500` and the parent dropdown at `zIndex=1150`: `390x844` has popup `rect={x:8,y:520,width:374,height:208}` but `popup-center` hits the modal `Description` label and `popup-left-option` hits the modal input; `360x740` has popup `rect={x:8,y:416,width:344,height:208}` but hit points return modal `Description`, `Image URL`, and input elements; `740x360` has popup `rect={x:8,y:-4,width:724,height:184}` with hit points returning modal textarea/header/wrap elements; and `768x1024` has popup `rect={x:8,y:615,width:752,height:184}` with hit points returning the modal form and `Image URL` label. The desktop control records modalWrap `zIndex=1000`, popup `zIndex=1150`, and hit points on `.ant-select-tree-title` / tree node content.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/CategoryManagement.tsx:540-548` renders the parent `TreeSelect` with `classNames={{ popup: { root: 'shop-mobile-popup-layer' } }}` and `getPopupContainer={() => document.body}`, so the options are portaled outside the editor body. `frontend/src/App.css:2280-2303` raises mobile `.ant-modal-root` / `.ant-modal-wrap` to `z-index: 1500 !important`, while `frontend/src/App.css:2632-2646` tries to keep body-mounted mobile popups above form chrome with `z-index: 2100` but does not use `!important`; the runtime dropdown keeps Ant Design's inline `z-index=1150`, below the modal. `frontend/src/pages/CategoryManagement.css:372-392` makes the mobile editor modal content a clipped flex column, and `frontend/src/pages/CategoryManagement.css:517-523` makes the footer sticky, further ensuring the lower-z popup is visually covered.
- Fix: `frontend/src/App.css` now applies `z-index: 2100 !important` to the shared mobile `.shop-mobile-popup-layer` popup guard. Because the parent Category `TreeSelect` already uses `classNames={{ popup: { root: 'shop-mobile-popup-layer' } }}` and portals to `document.body`, its `.ant-select-dropdown` popup now wins over the mobile modal wrapper's `z-index: 1500 !important` instead of being held below the modal by Ant Design's inline `1150`. The existing left/right/width mobile popup bounds continue to keep the dropdown inside the viewport.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/CategoryManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser hit-test screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Keep the parent-category TreeSelect above the category editor modal on mobile/tablet. Either portal it into the modal body with a container that stays inside the scrollable editor, or give `.shop-mobile-popup-layer.ant-select-dropdown` / TreeSelect dropdowns a z-index that wins over mobile modal inline styles using the same specificity/`!important` strategy as the modal rule. Verify add/edit/child category flows at `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, `820x1180`, and desktop, including hit tests that tree options receive pointer events and long taxonomy labels remain readable.

### F3530: HIGH — Coupon editor and grant popups open behind mobile/tablet modals

- Area: Frontend / Admin Coupon Management editor and grant modal popups
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4200/admin/coupons`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `coupons`, `coupons:write`, `coupons:delete`, `coupons:grant`, `coupons:birthday-run`, and `coupons:birthday-config` permissions plus active/inactive coupon rows, grant-target users, coupon summary data, and pet-birthday coupon config; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: Opening `Create coupon` or `Grant coupon` on phone and tablet widths creates body-mounted Select/RangePicker popups, but the popups render below the raised mobile Ant Design modal layer. The popup DOM rectangles are present and visible according to layout metrics, but visually the coupon type dropdown, valid-time calendar, and grant-user list are covered by modal form fields or sticky modal footers. Hit tests inside the popup rectangles return modal inputs, labels, footer buttons, or the modal wrapper instead of Select options or calendar controls. As a result, mobile/tablet operators cannot reliably pick coupon type/status/scope, set a validity window, or choose grant recipients, which blocks creating/editing coupons and direct issuance workflows. Desktop is the control state: the same popups sit above the modal and hit tests return dropdown options, calendar controls, and user rows.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0145-admin-coupons-codex/phone-390-coupon-type-select.png`; `phone-390-valid-range-picker.png`; `phone-390-grant-user-select.png`; `tablet-768-valid-range-picker.png`; `tablet-768-grant-user-select.png`; `desktop-1366-coupon-type-select.png`; `desktop-1366-valid-range-picker.png`; `desktop-1366-grant-user-select.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260608T0145-admin-coupons-codex/report.json`. The report records the mobile/tablet modal wrapper at `zIndex=1500` while the popups keep Ant Design inline `zIndex=1150`: at `390x844`, `coupon-type-select` has popup `rect={x:8,y:296,width:374,height:96}` but hit points return `$` form inputs, `valid-range-picker` has popup `rect={x:8,y:12,width:374,height:441}` but hit points return the modal header/form, and `grant-user-select` has popup `rect={x:8,y:427,width:374,height:140}` but hit points return the modal footer/wrap. At `768x1024`, the same type select, RangePicker, and grant select hit modal form rows, wraps, or footers instead of options. The desktop control records modal wrapper `zIndex=1000`, popup `zIndex=1150`, and hit points on `Discount`, time picker cells, `OK`, and user option text.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/CouponManagement.tsx:24-25` defines the shared `shop-mobile-popup-layer` class names. `frontend/src/pages/CouponManagement.tsx:842-928` renders the coupon editor modal with body-mounted coupon type/scope/status `Select` controls and the valid-time `DatePicker.RangePicker`, all using `classNames={mobilePopupClassNames}` plus `getPopupContainer={() => document.body}`. `frontend/src/pages/CouponManagement.tsx:939-994` renders the grant modal and body-mounted multi-user Select the same way. `frontend/src/pages/CouponManagement.css:423-502` makes mobile editor/grant modal content a clipped flex column with scrollable body and sticky footer, while `frontend/src/App.css:2280-2303` raises mobile `.ant-modal-root` / `.ant-modal-wrap` to `z-index: 1500 !important`. The popup guard in `frontend/src/App.css:2632-2646` sets `.shop-mobile-popup-layer` to `z-index: 2100`, but it does not use `!important`, so the runtime popups keep Ant Design's inline `z-index=1150` and stay below the modal.
- Fix: `frontend/src/App.css` now applies `z-index: 2100 !important` to the shared mobile `.shop-mobile-popup-layer` popup guard. Coupon Management already uses that layer for coupon type/scope/status Selects, the valid-time `DatePicker.RangePicker`, toolbar filters, birthday coupon type Select, and the grant-user multi-select, all portaled to `document.body`; those popups now win over the raised mobile modal wrapper instead of being held below it by Ant Design's inline `1150`. The existing shared popup left/right/width bounds still keep Select and picker overlays inside the narrow viewport.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/CouponManagement.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅ with existing React act deprecation warning noise; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser hit-test screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Keep Coupon Management's body-mounted Select and RangePicker popups above the mobile/tablet modal layer, or portal them into the modal body so they share the modal stacking context and remain within the scrollable editor. If using the shared `.shop-mobile-popup-layer`, make its z-index override win over Ant Design inline popup z-index with sufficient specificity/`!important`, and verify coupon type, scope, status, valid-time RangePicker, birthday coupon type, toolbar filters, and grant-user Select at `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, `820x1180`, and desktop with hit tests confirming options/calendar controls receive pointer events.

### F3531: MEDIUM — Bug Management create modal footer covers form fields on small phones

- Area: Frontend / Admin Bug Management create/edit modal
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4200/admin/bugs`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `bugs`, `bugs:read`, `bugs:write`, `bugs:scan`, and `bugs:status` permissions plus open/fixing BUG rows; `Create bug` modal opened at viewport `320x568`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: On a short phone viewport, the BUG create/edit modal opens with a sticky footer that covers the lower visible form fields before the operator scrolls. The `Priority` select row is partially hidden under the `Cancel`/`OK` footer, and hit testing at the center of that select returns `.ant-modal-footer` instead of the select text. Because the form body has many required operational fields and the footer stays inside the same modal content stack, operators cannot reliably see or tap the next fields in order while creating a BUG from mobile. This is separate from F804 because the controls keep 44px touch sizes, but the modal footer is the top hit target over a form field. It is also separate from F3527/F3530 because this failure happens before a dropdown layering issue: the footer itself covers the field.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0305-admin-bugs-codex/probe-editor-open.png`; `probe-editor-open.json`; `probe-module-clicked.png`; `probe-module-clicked.json`; `small-320-editor-modal.png`; `small-320-editor-module-select.png`. The DOM probe records `.bug-management__modal .ant-modal-body` at `y=102,height=324,bottom=426` with `scrollHeight=1395`, while `.bug-management__modal .ant-modal-footer` is sticky at `y=438,height=116,bottom=554,zIndex=4`. In the same initial modal state, the third select (`Priority`) has `rect={x:34,y:424,width:252,height:44,bottom:468}`, and `document.elementFromPoint()` at its center returns `DIV.ant-modal-footer` with text `CancelOK`, proving the footer is visually and interactively above the field. The screenshot shows the `Priority` label and top edge of its select disappearing into the footer area.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/BugManagement.tsx:681-735` renders the create/edit modal as `profile-mobile-safe-modal bug-management__modal` with module, severity, priority, assignee, description, page URL, environment, reproduction, expected/actual, and attachment fields. `frontend/src/pages/BugManagement.css:252-258` makes `.bug-management__modal .ant-modal-footer` a two-column sticky footer at mobile widths, and the shared mobile-safe modal rules in `frontend/src/App.css` / `frontend/src/pages/Profile.css` constrain the modal body to an internal scroll region. The current combination leaves the body viewport ending just above the sticky footer without enough scroll padding or footer clearance for the next form row.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/BugManagement.css:318-365` now adds the F3531 mobile modal contract: viewport-bounded `.bug-management__modal.ant-modal`, flex column modal content, scrollable modal body with `156px` footer padding and `172px` scroll padding, form-item scroll margins, compact two-column non-wide form grids, and a sticky footer with a protected z-index/background. `frontend/src/pages/BugManagement.test.ts:1-34` guards the create/edit/status modal shell, required modal fields, mobile popup class usage, and the footer-clearance CSS so the regression cannot silently return.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/BugManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser hit-test screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Add reliable footer clearance for BUG create/edit and status modals on short mobile screens. Increase modal body bottom padding/scroll padding by the sticky footer height, move the footer outside the scroll-overlap path, or use a non-overlapping full-height modal layout where the body cannot render form controls under the footer. Verify `Create bug`, `Edit bug`, `Scan bug`, and `Update status` at `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, and `768x1024`, including hit tests proving each visible field receives pointer events instead of the modal footer.

### F3532: MEDIUM — Log Management range picker opens completely off-screen on mobile

- Area: Frontend / Admin Log Management time range download
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4200/admin/logs`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `logs`, `logs:debug`, and `logs:download` permissions plus support unread count, long logger/log path/status data, and current log-level state; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: Opening `Time range download` on portrait phones, phone landscape, and tablet widths positions the body-mounted RangePicker calendar entirely above the viewport. No month grid, time column, or OK control is visible or clickable, and hit tests inside the recorded popup center return `null`. The level select and debug Popconfirm remain usable in the same audit states, and desktop is the control case where the calendar opens in view and receives pointer events. This blocks admins from setting a log export window during incident response on mobile/tablet devices. It is separate from F2760/F2718 because the failure is not table or header overflow, and separate from F3517/F3521/F3530 because it affects the `/admin/logs` download RangePicker rather than audit-log sticky filters, announcement modal scheduling, or coupon modal popup layering.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0430-admin-logs-codex/small-320-range-open.png`; `phone-360-range-open.png`; `phone-390-range-open.png`; `landscape-740-range-open.png`; `tablet-768-range-open.png`; `desktop-1366-range-open.png`; `report.json`. The report records the RangePicker control and popup at `320x568` as control `rect={x:28,y:465,width:492,height:44,bottom:509}` and dropdown `rect={x:8,y:-677,width:492,height:317,bottom:-360,zIndex:2100}` with `document.elementFromPoint()` at the center returning `null`. The same off-screen popup pattern appears at `360x740` with dropdown `rect={x:8,y:-445,width:492,height:406,bottom:-39}`, `390x844` with `rect={x:8,y:-676,width:496,height:441,bottom:-235}`, `740x360` with `rect={x:8,y:-821,width:724,height:209,bottom:-612}`, and `768x1024` with `rect={x:8,y:-560,width:752,height:383,bottom:-177}`. The desktop control at `1366x900` opens in view at `rect={x:719,y:200,width:573,height:379,bottom:579}`, and hit testing returns `.ant-picker-body`.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/LogManagement.tsx:227-241` renders the `RangePicker` with `showTime`, `allowClear={false}`, `classNames={{ popup: { root: 'shop-mobile-popup-layer' } }}`, and `getPopupContainer={() => document.body}`. `frontend/src/pages/LogManagement.css:201-214` makes the first download role group horizontally scrollable on mobile while forcing `.log-management__download .ant-picker-range { min-width: 492px; }`. `frontend/src/pages/LogManagement.css:310-320` only applies a 44px touch-target guard to the picker/select/input/buttons, so it does not constrain popup placement inside the visible viewport.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/LogManagement.tsx:15-16` defines a scoped `shop-mobile-popup-layer log-management__rangePopup` picker layer, and `frontend/src/pages/LogManagement.tsx:231-236` applies it to the log export `RangePicker` with `placement="bottomLeft"` while keeping the body portal. `frontend/src/pages/LogManagement.css:372-428` removes the mobile `492px` picker minimum, keeps the control inside the card, pins the scoped picker dropdown to fixed safe-area viewport bounds at phone/tablet/short-screen breakpoints, and makes the picker panel/range wrapper scroll within that visible popup instead of auto-placing above the viewport.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/LogManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser hit-test screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Constrain the Log Management RangePicker popup to the visible viewport or portal it into a stable admin content container instead of letting the body-mounted popup auto-place above the screen. Consider a mobile-friendly sheet/full-width date-time picker, remove or scope the 492px desktop minimum width, and align the popup below the scroller when there is room. Verify `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900` with screenshots plus hit tests proving calendar cells, time controls, and OK receive pointer events.

### F3533: HIGH — System Monitor mobile diagnostics collapse long values into a 16px column

- Area: Frontend / Admin System Monitor dependency diagnostics
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4200/admin/system`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `system`, `system:status`, support permissions, degraded production configuration, high memory/disk usage, failed database status, Redis ready status, and degraded Nacos status with long hostnames, JDBC URL, blockers, warnings, namespace, and error text; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: On portrait phones, System Monitor's diagnostic `Descriptions` sections still render as fixed Ant Design table cells rather than usable stacked key/value blocks. Long operational values such as disk path, production blockers/warnings, JDBC URL, database error, Redis address, Nacos namespace, and Nacos group are squeezed into a right-side content column only 16px wide while the label cell takes nearly the full card width. The text becomes single-character vertical columns and balloons the page to roughly 24,000px tall, forcing operators to scroll through huge blank gaps without being able to read the failing dependency details. Desktop is the control case: the same fields have hundreds of pixels of content width and remain readable. This is separate from F2717, which covers status tags such as `Blocked`/`Ready` becoming vertical pills; this issue blocks the long diagnostic values and production blockers themselves after the current mobile readability CSS changes.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0500-admin-system-codex/small-320-top-full.png`; `phone-360-top-full.png`; `phone-390-top-full.png`; `small-320-middle.png`; `desktop-1366-top.png`; `report.json`. At `320x568`, the document reports `scrollHeight=24184`, while `Path` is `height=1143` with `labelWidth=247` and `contentWidth=16`, `Blockers` is `height=2805.67` with `contentWidth=16`, `Warnings` is `height=2330.84` with `contentWidth=16`, `URL` is `height=2969` with `contentWidth=16`, `Error` is `height=1531` with `contentWidth=16`, and `Address` is `height=1077` with `contentWidth=16`. The same pattern appears at `360x740` (`scrollHeight=24078`, label widths 287px, content width 16px) and `390x844` (`scrollHeight=24060`, label widths 313px, content width 16px). The desktop `1366x900` control has `scrollHeight=2545`, with `Path` content width `433.3`, `URL` content width `434.1`, and `Blockers` content width `887.4`.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/SystemMonitor.tsx:252-323` renders disk path, runtime environment, production config blockers/warnings, and database URL/error via Ant Design `Descriptions`, then `frontend/src/pages/SystemMonitor.tsx:332-370` renders Redis/Nacos dependency values the same way. `frontend/src/pages/SystemMonitor.css:450-491` attempts to stack labels above long values on mobile, but `frontend/src/pages/SystemMonitor.css:555-563` later keeps `.ant-descriptions-view` clipped and forces the underlying table to `width: 100%` with `table-layout: fixed`, leaving table-cell label/content allocation in control and collapsing the content cell.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/SystemMonitor.css:581-648` now adds the final F3533 mobile override: Ant Design `Descriptions`, view/table/tbody/tr/row/item/label/content nodes become full-width block rows, the underlying table switches to `table-layout: auto !important`, overflow is visible, labels no longer reserve a wide table cell, and values plus nested Typography/messages wrap at full card width. `frontend/src/pages/SystemMonitor.test.ts:1-30` guards the long diagnostic fields and the stacked-row CSS contract.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/SystemMonitor.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime browser screenshots with long dependency values are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Replace System Monitor mobile `Descriptions` with real stacked key/value rows for diagnostic sections, or override the Ant Design table structure completely on mobile so label and value cells become full-width block rows. Do not rely on `table-layout: fixed` for long operational values. Verify `320x568`, `360x740`, and `390x844` with long disk paths, JDBC URLs, database errors, production blockers/warnings, Redis hostnames, and Nacos namespace/group values; each content value should have the full card width, no 16px content columns, no vertical single-character text, and page height should stay proportional to the actual content.

### F3534: HIGH — Support Management mobile selection leaves the conversation workflow off-screen

- Area: Frontend / Admin Support Management mobile conversation workflow
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4200/admin/support`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `support`, `support:reply`, `support:assign`, `support:close`, `support:reopen`, `support:read-state`, `orders`, and `coupons:birthday-reissue` permissions plus unread/open/closed sessions, a long waiting customer name, return-requested order context `SO-RETURN-20260608-00008801-LONG`, order card/detail modal, workflow quick replies, reply composer, and close confirmation; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: On portrait phones and phone landscape, tapping the urgent queue item selects it but leaves the page positioned on the queue instead of bringing the selected conversation into view. The selected session, customer/header actions, order card, workflow quick replies, textarea, and send button are all below the viewport, so a support operator receives no immediate visual confirmation that the conversation opened and cannot start handling the ticket without manually scrolling past the queue. The desktop side-by-side layout works as the control case, and tablet only partially shows the conversation while still hiding the workflow/composer initially. This is separate from F2761 because the failure is the post-selection handoff into the support work area, not the top insight/status rail overflow.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0520-admin-support-codex/phone-360-selected-after-click.png`; `phone-390-selected-after-click.png`; `landscape-740-selected-after-click.png`; `small-320-selected-after-click.png`; `tablet-768-selected-after-click.png`; `desktop-1366-selected-after-click.png`; `report.json`. The report records `selected-after-click` at `360x740` with `scrollY=12`, `conversationPane.y=875.75`, and visible ratios `conversationPane=0`, `conversationHeader=0`, `messagesPane=0`, `orderWorkflow=0`, `composer=0`. At `390x844`, `scrollY=0`, `conversationPane.y=929.75`, and the same conversation/header/messages/workflow/composer ratios are all `0`; at `740x360`, `scrollY=342`, `conversationPane.y=466.06`, and all conversation work-area ratios are `0`. The `320x568` small-phone run only exposes the top sliver of the conversation (`conversationPane=0.05`, `conversationHeader=0.19`, messages/workflow/composer `0`) after the click. The `768x1024` tablet run shows the header but still has `orderWorkflow=0` and `composer=0`. The desktop `1366x900` control keeps `conversationPane`, `conversationHeader`, `messagesPane`, `orderWorkflow`, and `composer` all at `1.0` after the same queue selection.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.tsx:809-909` renders the queue pane first and each queue item only calls `loadMessages(item)` on click/keyboard activation. `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.tsx:909-1114` renders the selected conversation pane, header actions, messages, order workflow, and composer after the queue pane. `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.css:68-74` uses a desktop two-column grid, while `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.css:1084-1102` changes the support layout to a single column under `900px`. `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.css:1391-1405` then makes that mobile layout auto-height and caps the queue pane at `420px` with its own scrolling, and `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.css:1562-1574` keeps the phone queue/messages panes constrained, but there is no mobile auto-scroll, focus transfer, anchor, tab, split-view, or collapse behavior after the selected session changes.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.tsx:127` now keeps a conversation pane ref, `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.tsx:353-367` adds `handoffConversationOnMobile()` and calls it immediately after `setSelectedSession(session)`, and `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.tsx:936` makes the conversation pane focusable. The handoff only runs on stacked `max-width: 900px` layouts, scrolls the selected conversation pane to the top, and transfers focus with `preventScroll`. `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.css:1391-1420` adds scroll-margin/focus styling so the conversation header lands below sticky mobile chrome.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/SupportManagement.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅ with existing React/AntD act warning noise; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime mobile browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Add an explicit mobile handoff from queue selection to the conversation. Options include scrolling/focusing the conversation pane after `loadMessages`, using a two-step queue/conversation view with a back-to-queue control, making the selected conversation a sticky/tabbed destination, or collapsing the queue after selection. Verify queue-item click and keyboard activation at `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, and `768x1024`; after selection, the conversation header plus either the latest message/order card or the reply composer/action affordance must be visible or reachable through an obvious in-view control.

### F3535: MEDIUM — Support Management mobile confirmations clip the session identity

- Area: Frontend / Admin Support Management mobile destructive confirmations
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4200/admin/support`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `support`, `support:reply`, `support:assign`, `support:close`, `support:reopen`, `support:read-state`, `orders`, and `coupons:birthday-reissue` permissions plus an open session named `maria.customer.with.a.very.long.name.waiting.for.return.approval.and.manual-supervisor-review`, return-requested order context `SO-RETURN-20260608-00008810-LONG`, selected conversation, and the `Close conversation` Popconfirm opened; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: On portrait phones, the Support Management confirmation popup's outer layer is constrained to the viewport, but the Popconfirm title and description keep a fixed long-content width and are clipped inside that layer. For close/assign/reopen/birthday-reissue actions, the popup repeats the selected customer/session label as both title context and description; with long real-world usernames, the right side of that identity disappears. The action buttons remain visible, but operators cannot verify the full customer/session before confirming a destructive or account-affecting operation from mobile. Landscape, tablet, and desktop controls keep the identity fully visible. This is separate from F3534 because the conversation is already selected and scrolled into view here, and separate from F3523 because that issue is Pet Gallery Popconfirm placement at landscape/tablet widths while this is Support Management Popconfirm content clipping on portrait phones.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0410-admin-support-popconfirm-codex/small-320-close-popconfirm.png`; `phone-360-close-popconfirm.png`; `phone-390-close-popconfirm.png`; `landscape-740-close-popconfirm.png`; `tablet-768-close-popconfirm.png`; `desktop-1366-close-popconfirm.png`; `report.json`. At `320x568`, the popup root is viewport-constrained at `x=0,width=304,right=304` with `overflowX=hidden`, but both `.ant-popconfirm-title` and `.ant-popconfirm-description` are `width=519.42,right=539.42`, so only `57.8%` of each identity line is visible. At `360x740`, the root is `width=344,right=344` while title/description still end at `539.42`, leaving only `65.5%` visible. At `390x844`, the root is `width=374,right=374` while title/description still end at `539.42`, leaving only `71.2%` visible. The control cases show full visibility: `740x360` records title/description `right=728` in a `740px` viewport, `768x1024` records `right=756`, and desktop `1366x900` records `right=1353.78`.
- Source evidence: The original audit found `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.tsx:31` defined only the shared `shop-mobile-popup-layer` root class, `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.tsx:614-620` built confirmation labels from the full `selectedSession.username` plus session id, and `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.tsx:923-983` passed those full labels into the assign/reopen/reissue/close `Popconfirm` `title` and `description`. The shared mobile popup guard in `frontend/src/App.css:2632-2646` applied `max-width`, `max-height`, `overflow: auto`, and `z-index` to `.shop-mobile-popup-layer`, but it did not constrain or wrap the nested `.ant-popconfirm-title` / `.ant-popconfirm-description`; the captured root then clipped its own 519px children on 320/360/390px phones.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.tsx:31` now scopes the shared mobile popup helper as `shop-mobile-popup-layer support-management__popconfirm`, and `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.tsx:951-1001` applies that helper to assign/reopen/reissue/close Popconfirms while keeping action labels as `title` and the full selected session identity as `description`. `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.css:1843-1885` adds the F3535 mobile containment rule: the Support Management Popconfirm is pinned to phone safe-area left/right bounds, nested AntD Popconfirm content is constrained to `width: 100%`, long title/description text wraps with `overflow-wrap: anywhere` and `word-break: break-word`, and confirmation buttons can wrap inside the visible overlay. `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.test.tsx:142-161` guards the scoped class, Popconfirm contracts, and F3535 CSS wrapping behavior.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/SupportManagement.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅ with existing React/AntD act warning noise; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime mobile browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Add Support Management Popconfirm content rules that let long session/customer identifiers wrap inside the visible popup width: set the Popconfirm content/title/description to `min-width: 0`, `max-width: 100%`, and `overflow-wrap: anywhere` or truncate only the title while showing the full identifier in wrapped body text. Verify close, assign, reopen, and birthday reissue confirmations at `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and desktop with long usernames and similar session labels; the full identity or a clear expandable/wrapped equivalent must be visible before `Confirm`.

### F3536: MEDIUM — Order Management modals hide payment evidence and action columns

- Area: Frontend / Admin Order Management detail and refund modals
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4200/admin/orders`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `orders`, `orders:export`, `orders:status`, `orders:fulfillment`, `orders:payment`, and `orders:refund` permissions plus active carriers, after-sales orders, long order numbers, long product/payment references, paid and reconciliation-required payment records, and refund workflows; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: The Order Management `Order items` and `Refund now` modals render payment/order evidence as desktop Ant Design tables inside a narrow modal body. On phones, the default view exposes only the leftmost columns (`ID`/`Product` or `Payment`/partial `Status`) while quantity, amount, subtotal, refund reference, created time, and sync-payment action columns sit far to the right. The refund modal still keeps the destructive `Refund now` button visible, so an operator can issue a refund without seeing the full payment evidence or the reconciliation/sync action that belongs to the same payment row. Tablet/landscape and even desktop controls still clip the payment-history/evidence action columns in the default modal view, though the phone state is the most severe. This is not a duplicate of F2718, which covers the main admin list tables; this defect is inside the modal workflows used after an order row has already been opened.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0425-admin-orders-codex/phone-360-refund-modal.png`; `phone-390-refund-modal.png`; `phone-360-detail-modal.png`; `phone-390-detail-modal.png`; `desktop-1366-refund-modal.png`; `desktop-1366-detail-modal.png`; `report.json`. The report records the detail modal at `360x740` with a `292px` body: the order-items table wrapper is `scrollWidth=1127/clientWidth=292`, and the payment-history table wrapper is `scrollWidth=1203/clientWidth=292`. At `390x844`, those wrappers are `1127/322` and `1203/322`. In the refund modal, the payment evidence table is `1050/292` at `360x740` and `1050/322` at `390x844`, while the visible screenshot shows only `PAYMENT` and a cropped `STATUS`; `AMOUNT`, `Refund reference`, and `Actions` are off to the right. The same default clipping persists at wider controls: the refund modal table content is `688/472` at `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`, and the detail payment-history content is `762/672` on desktop.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.tsx:1417-1485` renders the refund modal `Payment evidence` table with `Payment`, `Status`, `Amount`, `Refund reference`, and `Actions` columns but no responsive/card fallback and no explicit table `scroll` treatment. `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.tsx:1538-1640` renders the detail modal's order-items and payment-history tables the same way, including `Quantity`, `Amount`, `Subtotal`, `Refund reference`, `Created at`, and `Actions`. Mobile modal CSS in `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.css:888-934` constrains modal content/body height and makes `.ant-table-content` overscrollable, while the general order table rules in `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.css:615-623` set table wrappers to horizontal overflow and force `.order-management-page__table .ant-table` to max-content only for the main page table. There is no modal-specific layout that keeps refund evidence, amounts, references, and actions visible.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.tsx:38-40` adds a typed evidence-cell helper for `data-label` props; `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.tsx:1424-1491` wraps the refund payment evidence table and labels Payment method, Status, Amount, Refund reference, and Actions cells; `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.tsx:1560-1678` wraps the detail order-item/payment-history tables and labels product, quantity, amount, subtotal, payment status, refund reference, created time, and sync actions. `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.css:614-626` gives evidence sections an inline-size container, and `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.css:1235-1334` adds the F3536 card layout that hides narrow table headers, turns rows into bordered evidence cards, renders `data-label` before each value, wraps long values/buttons/tags, and collapses to one-column labels on very narrow modal bodies. `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.test.ts:1-34` guards the cell labels and CSS contract.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/OrderManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime modal screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Replace modal evidence tables with compact stacked rows/cards on mobile and narrow modal widths, or give each modal table an explicit, visible horizontal-scroll treatment with sticky row identity plus visible affordance. For the refund modal, keep payment method, status, amount, refund reference/reconciliation state, and sync action visible before the destructive footer action. Verify order detail and refund modals at `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and desktop with paid, pending, reconciliation-required, refunding, and refunded payment rows.

### F3537: MEDIUM - Order Management fulfillment carrier dropdowns open underneath their modals

- Area: Frontend / Admin Order Management shipping and batch-shipping modals
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4200/admin/orders`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `orders`, `orders:export`, `orders:status`, `orders:fulfillment`, `orders:payment`, and `orders:refund` permissions plus active carriers with long names/codes, two `PENDING_SHIPMENT` orders, one `SHIPPED` order with tracking, batch fulfillment, and logistics tracking results; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: In the single-order `Enter tracking number` modal and the `Batch ship orders` modal, opening the carrier Select on mobile/tablet widths creates a body-mounted dropdown, but the modal content, sticky footer, or header sits above the option rows. On portrait phones the single-order carrier options are completely hidden behind the tracking-number input, and the batch carrier options are hidden behind the sticky footer with only a bottom sliver sometimes visible below the modal. In short landscape the dropdown stretches across the viewport behind the modal, so only the left/right strips outside the modal are readable while the selectable center is covered. Desktop is the control case: the dropdown is clickable, although the single-order carrier field remains only 124px wide and truncates long carrier labels. This is separate from F3536 because it blocks choosing fulfillment carriers, not reading detail/refund evidence tables, and separate from F3530/F3527 because those cover Coupon/Category modal popups rather than Order Management fulfillment.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0423-admin-order-fulfillment-codex/phone-360-shipping-carrier-dropdown.png`; `phone-390-shipping-carrier-dropdown.png`; `small-320-batch-carrier-dropdown.png`; `phone-360-batch-carrier-dropdown.png`; `phone-390-batch-carrier-dropdown.png`; `landscape-740-shipping-carrier-dropdown.png`; `landscape-740-batch-carrier-dropdown.png`; `tablet-768-batch-carrier-dropdown.png`; `desktop-1366-shipping-carrier-dropdown.png`; `desktop-1366-batch-carrier-dropdown.png`; `report.json`. The report records the shipping carrier dropdown at `320x568` as `x=8,y=164,width=304,height=140` with the modal at `x=10,y=34,width=300,height=385`, but the option center hit-test lands on the tracking `INPUT`, not an option. The same failure repeats at `360x740` (`dropdown=8,164,344,140`, hit `INPUT`), `390x844` (`8,164,374,140`, hit `INPUT`), `740x360` (`8,164,724,140`, hit `INPUT`), and `768x1024` (`8,164,752,140`, hit `INPUT`). For batch shipping, the dropdown exists at `320x568` (`8,292,304,140`) but the option center hits the `Cancel` button; at `360x740` and `390x844` it hits the modal footer (`CancelBatch ship`); at `740x360` it hits the modal header; and at `768x1024` it again hits the modal footer. The desktop `1366x900` controls hit `.ant-select-item-option-content` for both shipping and batch carrier dropdowns.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.tsx:1294-1320` renders the single-order shipping `Select` inside `order-management-page__shippingModal` with `classNames={{ popup: { root: 'shop-mobile-popup-layer' } }}` and `getPopupContainer={() => document.body}`; unlike batch shipping it has no full-width field class, so the field measures only about `124px` in every viewport. `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.tsx:1490-1523` renders the batch-shipping carrier `Select` the same body-mounted way. `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.css:605-612` gives `width: 100%` only to `.order-management-page__batchCarrierSelect`, not to the single-order shipping Select. `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.css:898-918` makes mobile modal content `overflow: hidden` with a sticky footer. The shared mobile popup rules in `frontend/src/App.css:2632-2655` force `.shop-mobile-popup-layer.ant-select-dropdown` to viewport-left/right with `width: auto`, and `frontend/src/App.css:2926-2944` later constrains all body-mounted Select dropdowns with `max-height: 64dvh` and hidden overflow. In this modal context the dropdown rectangle exists, but it is painted/click-targeted beneath modal chrome instead of above the selectable workflow.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.tsx:38` now defines a scoped `shop-mobile-popup-layer order-management-page__carrierPopup` class for fulfillment carrier Selects. `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.tsx:1323-1326` makes the single-order shipping carrier Select full-width, applies the scoped popup class, keeps the body popup container, and forces `placement="bottomLeft"`; `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.tsx:1535-1538` applies the same scoped popup and placement to batch shipping. `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.css:615-618` adds the shared carrier field width guard, and `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.css:1342-1381` adds the F3537 narrow/short viewport rule that raises `.order-management-page__carrierPopup` to `z-index: 12000`, pins it within safe-area left/right bounds, caps its height, preserves pointer events, and wraps long option content. `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.test.ts:8-15` and `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.test.ts:44-55` guard the source and CSS contracts.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/OrderManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime mobile/tablet browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Treat carrier selection as a modal-local control. Make the single-order shipping carrier Select full width, and ensure both shipping and batch carrier dropdowns render above the modal body/footer or inside a modal-scoped popup container that is not covered by sticky modal chrome. Avoid the global mobile `left/right: 8px; width: auto` behavior for modal-anchored Selects unless their z-index and hit targets are guaranteed above the modal. Verify `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and desktop by opening both carrier Selects with long carrier names/codes; the first option's hit-test should land on `.ant-select-item-option-content`, all visible options should be readable/clickable, and selected carrier labels should remain inspectable before `Enter tracking number` or `Batch ship`.

### F3538: MEDIUM - Product Management editor popups render behind the product modal or off-screen

- Area: Frontend / Admin Product Management product editor modal
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4200/admin/products`, current-source CRA dev server, SUPER_ADMIN profile mocked with `products:write`, `products:delete`, `products:status`, and `products:import` permissions plus product rows, recent import history, active categories/brands, default add-product form state, static status options, rich-content block options, and limited-time discount controls; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: The Product Management `Add product` editor uses body-mounted Ant Design Select/RangePicker popups inside a long Shopify-style modal. On mobile and tablet widths, opening product status or rich-content block type focuses the control but leaves the option list underneath the surrounding modal cards, body, or sticky footer; hit tests land on `Publishing`, textarea, block action buttons, or the modal footer instead of option rows. The limited-time RangePicker is worse on `320x568`, `360x740`, and `740x360`: its calendar is created above the visible viewport, so no date grid, time column, or OK button is visible in the viewport. Desktop is the control case where status, rich-content type, and the date/time picker are visible and clickable. This prevents mobile/tablet admins from reliably changing listing status, editing rich product content type, or setting a timed sale window from the product editor. This is separate from F3530 because that issue is Coupon Management, separate from F3532 because that issue is Log Management export windows, and separate from F3537 because that issue is Order fulfillment carrier selection.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0436-admin-products-editor-codex/phone-360-status-dropdown.png`; `phone-360-brand-dropdown.png`; `phone-360-rich-type-dropdown.png`; `phone-360-range-picker-dropdown.png`; `small-320-range-picker-dropdown-full.png`; `landscape-740-range-picker-dropdown.png`; `tablet-768-status-dropdown.png`; `tablet-768-rich-type-dropdown.png`; `desktop-1366-status-dropdown.png`; `desktop-1366-rich-type-dropdown.png`; `desktop-1366-range-picker-dropdown.png`; `report.json`. The report records the status dropdown at `360x740` as `x=8,y=327,width=344,height=184` with visible ratio `1`, but the option center hit-test returns `SECTION.shopify-card` (`PublishingOnline StoreShopPoint of Sale`) instead of `.ant-select-item-option-content`. At `320x568`, status dropdown `x=8,y=241,width=304,height=184` also hits the `Publishing` card, and at `768x1024`, status dropdown `x=8,y=671,width=752,height=184` still hits the modal/card layer. The rich-content type dropdown repeats the failure: `360x740` records `x=8,y=363,width=344,height=140`, but the hit-test lands on a block action `BUTTON`; `740x360` and `768x1024` hit the rich text `TEXTAREA` instead of `Text/Image/Video` options. For the limited-time RangePicker, `320x568` creates the picker at `x=8,y=-505,width=304,height=317` with visible ratio `0`; `360x740` creates it at `x=8,y=-419,width=344,height=406` with visible ratio `0`; and `740x360` creates it at `x=8,y=-297,width=724,height=209` with visible ratio `0`. Desktop controls are valid: at `1366x900`, status hits `.ant-select-item-option-content` (`Active`), rich type hits `.ant-select-item-option-content` (`Text`), and the RangePicker is visible at `x=817,y=275,width=457,height=379`.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/ProductManagement.tsx:39` defines `mobilePopupClassNames = { popup: { root: 'shop-mobile-popup-layer' } }`. `frontend/src/pages/ProductManagement.tsx:2648-2663` renders the product status `Select` with that popup class and `getPopupContainer={() => document.body}`; `frontend/src/pages/ProductManagement.tsx:2724-2739` renders the limited-time `DatePicker.RangePicker` the same way. `frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetailEditor.tsx:141-153` renders each rich-content block type `Select` with `classNames={{ popup: { root: 'shop-mobile-popup-layer' } }}` and `getPopupContainer={() => document.body}`. Mobile modal CSS in `frontend/src/pages/ProductManagement.css:1168-1207` constrains `.shopify-product-modal` and `.product-management-page .ant-modal-content` with `overflow: hidden`, a scrollable modal body, and a sticky footer. The shared mobile popup rules in `frontend/src/App.css:2632-2655` force `.shop-mobile-popup-layer.ant-select-dropdown` / picker dropdowns to viewport-left/right with `width: auto`, and `frontend/src/App.css:2926-2944` later constrains body-mounted popups with `max-height: 64dvh` and hidden overflow. In the product editor modal, that popup strategy leaves selectors and calendars outside the usable modal stacking/scroll context.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/ProductManagement.tsx:40` now defines scoped `shop-mobile-popup-layer product-management-page__editorPopup` class names for product-editor popups. `frontend/src/pages/ProductManagement.tsx:2375-2385` applies it with bottom-left placement to the editor category TreeSelect, and `frontend/src/pages/ProductManagement.tsx:2653-2740` applies it to status, vendor, tags, theme-template, and limited-time RangePicker controls. `frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetailEditor.tsx:19` and `frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetailEditor.tsx:142-149` apply the same scoped layer and bottom-left placement to rich-content block type Selects. `frontend/src/pages/ProductManagement.css:1721-1786` raises the scoped editor popups to `z-index: 12050`, constrains Select/TreeSelect dropdowns to safe-area left/right bounds, pins picker dropdowns to a fixed safe-area top, stacks picker panels, keeps picker footer visible, and wraps long Select/Tree labels. `frontend/src/pages/ProductManagement.test.ts:1-38` guards the source and CSS contracts.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/ProductManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime mobile/tablet browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Make Product Management editor popups modal-local and viewport-safe.
