Options include portaling editor Select/TreeSelect/RangePicker popups into the modal body/wrap, giving modal-scoped popups a z-index and placement that definitively sits above `.shopify-product-modal` content/footer, or replacing mobile date/time selection with an in-modal sheet. Verify `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and desktop by opening status, vendor/tag, rich block type, category, and limited-time RangePicker controls; option/calendar hit-tests should land on option rows, date cells, time columns, and OK controls, not modal cards, textareas, or footers.

### F3539: MEDIUM - Profile address and pet editor popups render behind mobile modal content

- Area: Frontend / Customer Profile address and pet editor modals
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4200/profile`, current-source CRA dev server, authenticated customer profile mocked with saved addresses, two pet profiles, pending/shipped orders, payment channels, and account-page APIs; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: The Profile address and pet editor modals use body-mounted Ant Design Cascader/Select/DatePicker popups. On mobile and tablet widths, opening the address region Cascader, pet type Select, pet birthday DatePicker, or pet size Select focuses the field but leaves the popup under the modal form, sticky footer, header, or modal wrapper. The popup rectangles are present and fully within the viewport, but the visible selectable area is covered: hit tests inside the option/calendar rows return form labels, inputs, modal content, modal footer, or modal wrap instead of Cascader items, Select options, or date cells. Desktop is the control case where the same controls hit actual option/date elements. This prevents customers from completing delivery regions or pet profiles from the account page on mobile/tablet, which blocks checkout readiness and personalized pet recommendations. This is separate from F3516 because that issue covers hidden Profile account/order rails, and separate from F3527/F3530/F3537/F3538 because those cover admin modal popups rather than customer Profile account forms.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0501-profile-modal-popups-codex/phone-360-address-region-cascader.png`; `phone-360-pet-type-select.png`; `phone-360-pet-birthday-picker.png`; `phone-360-pet-size-select.png`; `small-320-address-region-cascader.png`; `small-320-pet-size-select.png`; `landscape-740-pet-birthday-picker.png`; `tablet-768-pet-birthday-picker.png`; `desktop-1366-pet-type-select.png`; `desktop-1366-pet-birthday-picker.png`; `report.json`. The report records the address Cascader at `360x740` as `x=8,y=368,width=344,height=180,bottom=548,zIndex=1150` with visible ratio `1`, but the Cascader menu-item center hit returns the `Detailed address` form label; at `390x844` the same hit returns `Detailed address`, and at `768x1024` it still returns the modal form label. The pet type Select at `360x740` creates a dropdown `x=8,y=282,width=344,height=140,bottom=422,zIndex=1150`, but the option hit returns the `Breed` label; at `740x360` it returns the modal header. The birthday DatePicker at `390x844` creates a picker `x=8,y=12,width=374,height=420,bottom=432,zIndex=1150`, but date-cell hit testing lands on the `Pet name` label; at `740x360` and `768x1024`, the date-cell hit returns the modal wrap. The pet size Select at `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, and `768x1024` hits `.ant-modal-content` rather than option rows. Desktop controls are valid: at `1366x900`, the address Cascader hits `.ant-cascader-menu-item-content` (`中国`), pet type hits `.ant-select-item-option-content` (`Dog`), birthday hits `.ant-picker-cell-inner` (`1`), and size hits `.ant-select-item-option-content` (`Small`).
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx:1981-1990` renders the address region `Cascader` with `classNames={{ popup: { root: 'shop-mobile-popup-layer' } }}` and `getPopupContainer={() => document.body}`. `frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx:2018-2027` renders the pet type `Select` the same way, `frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx:2032-2033` renders the birthday `DatePicker` as a body-mounted `.shop-mobile-popup-layer`, and `frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx:2038-2048` renders the pet size `Select` with the same body portal. `frontend/src/pages/Profile.css:3037-3045` makes the address modal body an internal scroller, `frontend/src/pages/Profile.css:3056-3065` makes the address footer sticky, and `frontend/src/pages/Profile.css:3074-3090` makes profile modal bodies scroll while only constraining `.shop-mobile-popup-layer` size. `frontend/src/App.css:2288-2302` raises mobile modal roots/wraps to `z-index: 1500 !important`; `frontend/src/App.css:2632-2645` tries to assign `.shop-mobile-popup-layer` `z-index: 2100` without `!important`, while runtime popups keep Ant Design's inline `z-index=1150`; `frontend/src/App.css:2926-2948` later constrains all body-mounted Select/Cascader/Picker dropdowns but does not restore a winning stacking order.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx:28` defines scoped `shop-mobile-popup-layer profile-modal-popup` class names for Profile editor popups. `frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx:1983-1991` applies them with a body portal and bottom-left placement to the address region Cascader. `frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx:2021-2051` applies the same popup layer and placement to pet type, birthday, and pet size controls. `frontend/src/pages/Profile.css:3504-3560` raises the scoped popup layer to `z-index: 12050 !important`, constrains Select/Cascader dropdowns to safe-area viewport bounds, pins the DatePicker popup inside the visible viewport, keeps the picker footer visible, and wraps Select/Cascader option text. `frontend/src/pages/Profile.test.ts:8-24` guards the source and CSS contracts.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/Profile.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime mobile/tablet browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Make Profile editor popups modal-local and viewport-safe. Either portal the address Cascader, pet Selects, and birthday DatePicker into the active modal body/wrap, or give `.profile-mobile-safe-modal` / `.profile-address-modal` popups a stacking rule that definitively beats the mobile modal root and sticky footer, using the specificity needed to override Ant Design inline z-index. Verify `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and desktop by opening address region, pet type, birthday, and size controls; hit tests should land on Cascader menu items, Select option content, and picker day cells, and the Save/Cancel footer should not cover any selectable row.

### F3540: HIGH - Customer support order dropdown and order detail modal render under the support panel

- Area: Frontend / Customer support widget order context controls
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4200`, current-source CRA dev server, signed-in customer profile mocked with support sessions, chat messages containing an order card, two customer orders, order detail, and order-item APIs; support opened via `shop:open-support`; viewports `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: The customer support widget keeps its open panel above other overlays, but the order Select dropdown and the `View order` detail modal are still body-mounted Ant Design overlays with lower stacking levels. Opening `Send order` creates a visible order dropdown, yet its option rows sit below the support composer/panel; hit tests on the first option land on the message input, close/send actions, or support panel instead of `.ant-select-item-option-content`, so customers cannot choose an order to share. Opening `View order` creates the order-detail modal behind the same panel on phone and landscape sizes; the modal content and close button exist in the DOM, but their center points hit chat messages, composer fields, or the support header close button. This blocks the support workflow where a customer needs to attach or inspect order context while chatting. This is separate from F2859 because F2859 covers the support panel's short-landscape sizing before interacting with order controls, and separate from F3539 because F3539 covers Profile editor popups rather than the customer support widget.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260608T0515-customer-support-widget-codex/phone-360-order-select-open.png`; `phone-360-order-modal-open.png`; `small-320-order-select-open.png`; `small-320-order-modal-open.png`; `phone-390-order-select-open.png`; `phone-390-order-modal-open.png`; `landscape-740-order-select-open.png`; `landscape-740-order-modal-open.png`; `tablet-768-order-select-open.png`; `desktop-1366-order-select-open.png`; `report.json`. The report records `.customer-support-widget__panel` with `zIndex=2590` in every viewport, while `.support-order-select-popup` is only `zIndex=1305`; the first order option has visible ratio `1` but hit tests return the support textarea at `320x568`, the `Close conversation` button at `360x740`, the support textarea at `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900`. For `View order`, the mobile modal wrap is only `zIndex=1500` below the panel: at `320x568`, modal content `rect=10,34,300,540` has visible ratio `0.989`, but the modal center stack starts with `.customer-support-widget__bubble` and the modal close center hits `.customer-support-widget__headerClose`; at `360x740`, modal content `rect=10,34,340,614` has visible ratio `1`, but the modal center stack starts with the order card in the chat and the close center again hits the support header close; at `390x844`, modal center hits `.customer-support-widget__message--mine`; at `740x360`, the modal center hits the support textarea and the close center hits the support `Order attached` tag. Tablet and desktop are partial controls for the order-detail modal center, but their order Select dropdowns still hit the support composer instead of the option row.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.tsx:1191-1213` renders the support order `Select` with `classNames={{ popup: { root: 'shop-mobile-popup-layer support-order-select-popup' } }}` and `getPopupContainer={() => document.body}`. `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.tsx:1242-1248` renders the order detail as a regular body-mounted Ant Design `Modal` while the support panel remains open. `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.css:1107-1114` sizes `.support-order-select-popup` but does not give it a stacking level that beats the panel. `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.css:2490-2498` later sets the support button to `z-index: 2600` and the open panel to `z-index: 2590 !important`. `frontend/src/App.css:2288-2302` sets normal mobile modal roots/wraps to `z-index: 1500 !important`, and `frontend/src/styles/mobile-page-contrast.css:49-65` only raises AntD overlays to `--mobile-page-overlay-z: 9800` under `body.shop-mobile-app`, so the ordinary browser support widget path leaves order popups/modals below the open support panel.
- Fix: `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.tsx:30-34` defines a dedicated support-order overlay z-index and scoped order Select popup class/style helpers. `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.tsx:1215-1218` applies that popup class, explicit z-index style, body portal, and top-left placement to the support order Select. `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.tsx:1248-1250` gives the order detail Modal a raised `customer-support-widget__orderModalRoot` and matching `zIndex`. `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.css:2565-2620` raises the Select popup and modal mask/wrap/modal above the support panel, constrains the dropdown to safe-area viewport bounds, wraps option text, and keeps the modal aligned in mobile/short viewports. `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.test.tsx:8-26` guards the source and CSS contracts.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime mobile/tablet/desktop browser screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Treat support order controls as part of the active support overlay stack. Either portal the order Select dropdown and order detail modal into the support panel/wrap with a higher local z-index, temporarily lower/disable the panel stacking while the order overlay is open, or assign a dedicated support-order overlay z-index above `2590` for both `.support-order-select-popup` and `.customer-support-widget__orderModal` root/wrap/mask. Verify `320x568`, `360x740`, `390x844`, `740x360`, `768x1024`, and `1366x900` by opening `Send order` and `View order`; hit tests must land on Select option content, modal body content, and the modal close button rather than support panel elements.

### F3393: LOW — Spanish mobile bottom navigation clips the Products label at 360px

- Area: Frontend / Storefront mobile bottom navigation i18n
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190`, current-source CRA dev server, `shop-language=es`, `currency=MXN`, unauthenticated public storefront; viewports `360x740`, `390x844`, and `320x568`
- Severity: LOW
- Description: The fixed bottom navigation is not quite wide enough for the Spanish `Productos` label at the common `360x740` mobile viewport. The nav keeps six equal columns and 10px single-line labels, so the Products tab renders as `Produc...` while the other Spanish tab labels fit. At `390x844` the same label fits, and at `320x568` the CSS intentionally switches labels to visually hidden 1px text/icon-only navigation, so the defect is the intermediate 360px state where one core destination label is visibly clipped instead of either remaining readable or intentionally entering the icon-only mode. This is a low-severity polish/accessibility issue because the icon and link still work, but the primary product-browsing destination is less legible in Spanish.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2055-spanish-public-mobile-codex/bottom-nav-es-home-compact360-viewport.png`; `home-compact360-viewport.png`; `coupons-compact360-viewport.png`; `bottom-nav-es-home-mobile390-viewport.png`; `bottom-nav-es-home-small320-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2055-spanish-public-mobile-codex/bottom-nav-es-home-report.json` records the `360x740` bottom nav at `rect=6,668,344,62` and the `Productos` label at `rect=71,704,48,11` with `scrollWidth=49`, `clientWidth=48`, `textOverflow=ellipsis`, `whiteSpace=nowrap`, and `isEllipsized=true`. The same report records no ellipsized labels at `390x844`, and records the `320x568` labels as deliberate 1px visually hidden spans for all tabs.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx` renders the fixed bottom bar with six equal destinations (`Inicio`, `Productos`, `Cupones`, `Carrito`, `App`, `Cuenta`) using translated labels. `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css` sets `.shop-nav__bottomBar` to `grid-template-columns: repeat(6, minmax(0, 1fr))`, lowers item font size to `10px`, and forces bottom labels to `max-width: 100%; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap`. A later `@media (max-width: 340px)` visually hides all labels, but there is no Spanish-aware or 341-380px rule to keep `Productos` readable before that icon-only breakpoint.
- Fix: `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css:2841-2854` adds a Spanish-only `341px-380px` bottom-nav refinement that gives the Products column extra width, tightens the six-column gap, reduces only the Products label to `9.5px`, keeps `letter-spacing: 0`, and clips without ellipsis only for that label. The existing `max-width: 340px` icon-only fallback remains in place below the new rule. `frontend/src/components/Navbar.test.tsx:172-173` adds static source/CSS readers, and `frontend/src/components/Navbar.test.tsx:232-241` guards the Spanish bottom-bar class, F3393 CSS rule, and icon-only fallback.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/components/Navbar.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅ with existing React Router/AntD/act warning noise; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime Spanish/English/Chinese viewport screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Add an i18n-aware bottom-nav refinement for Spanish narrow phones: slightly widen/rebalance columns, reduce only the Spanish Products label font/letter spacing within readable limits, use a shorter localized tab label if product copy allows it, or switch the entire bottom nav to the existing icon-only treatment earlier for Spanish at widths where all labels cannot fit. Verify `360x740`, `375x812`, `390x844`, and `320x568` in Spanish, English, and Chinese so no single tab label visibly truncates while neighboring labels remain readable.

### F3358: MEDIUM — Storefront desktop utility nav collapses into two-line labels at tablet landscape widths

- Area: Frontend / Storefront navigation responsive breakpoints
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190`, current-source CRA dev server, unauthenticated home page; viewports `844x390`, `900x390`, and `1024x600`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: The storefront switches from the compact mobile header to the full desktop utility navigation immediately above the `780px` breakpoint, but the desktop row is too dense for common tablet/phone landscape widths. At `844x390`, the top row contains 12 utility controls across the width; 10 of the visible links/actions render as two-line labels (`Seller center`, `Pet finder`, `Pet gallery`, `Download Android app`, `Track order`, `Log in`, etc.), and many active targets are only 24-30px tall. The `Log in` link is squeezed to a 26px-wide column and breaks into two stacked words. At `900x390` and `1024x600`, the same utility row still renders 10 two-line items, so this is not a one-pixel breakpoint artifact. The result looks broken and makes the high-frequency account/support/order controls harder to scan and tap/click on landscape tablets and foldable/narrow desktop windows.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2032-nav-search-overlays-codex/home-baseline-landscape844-viewport.png`; `home-baseline-landscape900-viewport.png`; `home-baseline-tablet1024-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2032-nav-search-overlays-codex/report.json` records `844x390` with `headerHeight=254`, `headerViewportRatio=0.651`, `topItemCount=12`, `topItemsMultiline=10`, and visible under-44px controls including `Log in rect=806,38,26,30`, `Register rect=740,39,52,28`, `English rect=524,41,94,24`, and `USD $ rect=632,41,94,24`. The same report records `900x390` with `topItemsMultiline=10`, `headerViewportRatio=0.549`, `Log in rect=862,38,26,30`, and `1024x600` with `topItemsMultiline=10`. Mobile portrait controls at `390x844` and `320x568` did not show the top utility row, confirming the defect is in the desktop/tablet breakpoint path.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx` renders two `.shop-nav__links` groups in `.shop-nav__top` with seller, pet, coupon, Android download, deals, order, support, language, currency, register, and login actions. `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css` keeps `.shop-nav__top` at a fixed `height: 34px`, gives link/button targets only `min-height: 28px`, assigns language/currency selects `min-width: 94px`, and only hides the top row under `@media (max-width: 780px)`. There is no intermediate `781px-1024px` compact/tablet rule to move low-priority utilities into the More menu, prevent word wrapping, or increase the row height/touch targets.
- Fix: `frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx:1003-1014` adds seller/products, pet finder, pet gallery, deals, track order, coupons, support, compare, history, and alerts to the authenticated More menu; `frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx:1053-1068` adds the same tablet-hidden utilities to the guest More menu. `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css:2841-2935` adds a `781px-1024px` compact utility mode that hides the dense top row, keeps the main header on a compact three-column grid, exposes locale/More/account/cart controls with 44px targets, hides duplicate guest summary CTAs, and keeps category chips non-wrapping in a horizontal rail. `frontend/src/components/Navbar.test.tsx:244-258` guards the source and CSS contracts.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/components/Navbar.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅ with existing React Router/AntD/act warning noise; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime tablet-landscape screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Add a tablet-landscape navigation mode between the current mobile and desktop breakpoints. Move secondary utilities such as Seller center, Pet finder/gallery, Android download, Flash deals, or locale/currency into a compact More/utility menu, or allow the utility row to become a deliberate two-row layout with stable 44px targets and non-wrapping labels. Verify `844x390`, `900x390`, `1024x600`, `390x844`, and desktop `1366x900` so the desktop row stays readable, tappable, and does not consume the first viewport.

### F3357: MEDIUM — Storefront More menu clips utility actions in short landscape viewports

- Area: Frontend / Storefront navigation More dropdown responsive layout
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190`, current-source CRA dev server, unauthenticated home page, More menu opened from the storefront nav; viewports `740x360` and `844x390`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: Opening the storefront More menu on short landscape screens places a full-height utility dropdown below the already dense navigation stack without constraining it to the visible viewport. At `740x360`, the menu has 7 actions but only 5 are fully visible; `Browsing history` is partially clipped at the bottom edge and `Stock alerts` is fully off-screen. The same viewport also has the global mobile bottom navigation active, so the dropdown visually occupies the bottom-nav zone while its final actions extend below the page. At `844x390`, where the desktop-style nav is active and the bottom bar is absent, the menu still starts too low and clips the final 2 actions. Customers in landscape cannot reliably reach the history/stock-alert utility actions from the More menu.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T2010-more-menu-codex/home-more-menu-landscape740-viewport.png`; `home-more-menu-landscape740-full.png`; `home-more-menu-landscape844-viewport.png`; `home-more-menu-landscape844-full.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T2010-more-menu-codex/report.json` records `740x360` with `.ant-dropdown-menu` at `top=84 bottom=437 height=353`, `menuBottomBeyondViewportPx=77`, `itemCount=7`, `visibleItemCount=5`, `clippedItemCount=2`, `menuOverlapsBottomNav=true`, and `.shop-nav__bottomBar` at `top=288 bottom=350`. The same report records `844x390` with `.ant-dropdown-menu` at `top=157 bottom=482 height=325`, `menuBottomBeyondViewportPx=92`, `visibleItemCount=5`, and `clippedItemCount=2`. Control portrait checks at `320x568` and `390x844` recorded `visibleItemCount=7`, confirming the regression is tied to short landscape height rather than the menu content alone.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx` renders the account/guest More actions as Ant Design `Dropdown` overlays attached to `document.body` with `overlayClassName="shop-nav__dropdown-popup"`, but the dropdown had no explicit short-viewport placement or scroll constraint. `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css` gave `.shop-nav__dropdown-popup` only z-index/menu-row touch-target rules, including 48px minimum mobile rows, and did not set `max-height`, `overflow-y: auto`, or a short-height/landscape placement override. The same stylesheet enables the fixed `.shop-nav__bottomBar` at `max-width: 780px`, so `740x360` combined a tall dropdown with a bottom fixed rail.
- Fix: `frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx:882-883`, `frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx:949-950`, `frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx:996-997`, and `frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx:1047-1048` keep navigation dropdowns body-mounted with the scoped `shop-nav__dropdown-popup` class. `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css:604-637` adds the F3357 short-viewport dropdown constraint: fixed safe-area top placement, visible-viewport max-height, internal menu scrolling, overscroll containment, and a mobile-landscape cap that subtracts `--shop-mobile-bottom-nav-height`. `frontend/src/components/Navbar.test.tsx:261-273` guards the popup class, body portal, and short-viewport CSS contract.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/components/Navbar.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅ with existing React Router/AntD/act warning noise; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime short-landscape screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Constrain the More dropdown to the available viewport height on short screens, for example with a `max-height: calc(100dvh - safe top/bottom offsets)` and internal `overflow-y: auto`, or use a landscape-specific placement/sheet pattern that opens upward or centers the menu away from the bottom nav. Verify all 7 actions remain visible or clearly scrollable and tappable at `740x360`, `844x390`, `390x844`, and `320x568`, with no bottom-nav overlap hiding menu items.

### F2859: HIGH — Customer support panel is unusable in mobile landscape

- Area: Frontend / Customer support widget mobile landscape layout
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190`, current-source CRA dev server, support widget opened via `shop:open-support`, viewport `740x360`
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: Opening customer support in a short mobile landscape viewport produces a desktop-sized floating panel on the right instead of a usable landscape/mobile support sheet. At `740x360`, the panel is only 240px tall, the message viewport collapses to 32px, and the composer content extends below the panel. The visible panel shows the header, conversation brief, a thin empty message strip, and the top of the composer; the order selector, message field, `Close conversation`, and `Log in` actions are pushed into clipped/internal scroll space and are not visible or discoverable from the first open state. This blocks the main help workflow precisely on the landscape phone size where the fixed mobile navigation is already consuming vertical space.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1925-support-interactions-codex/support-open-landscape-viewport.png`; `support-open-landscape-full.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1925-support-interactions-codex/report.json` records `.customer-support-widget__panel` at `rect=286,28,430,240`, `.customer-support-widget__messages` at `rect=287,165,428,32`, `.customer-support-widget__composer` at `rect=287,197,428,158` with `scrollHeight=440`, and `.customer-support-widget__actions` at `rect=301,580,400,44`, below the viewport and outside the visible panel. The same report records `.shop-nav__bottomBar` active at `rect=8,288,724,62`, confirming this viewport is in the mobile navigation regime while the support widget is not using its mobile layout.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css` switches the global storefront bottom bar on below the mobile/tablet breakpoints, while `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.css` kept the desktop support panel base rule (`right: 24px; bottom: 92px; height: min(620px, calc(100vh - 120px))`) for `740px` wide landscape. The support widget's mobile overrides were scoped mainly to `@media (max-width: 720px)`, so a common `740x360` landscape phone landed between the nav and support-widget breakpoints.
- Fix: `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.tsx:31-82` defines the shared support compact breakpoint as `<=780px` plus short landscape up to `900x430`, and `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.tsx:137` / `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.tsx:300` use that helper for initial and resized mobile state. `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.css:2664-2906` adds the F2859 compact support sheet: visible backdrop, safe-area-bounded panel, grid rows with a real scrollable messages area, internally scrollable composer, 44px order/input/action controls, and a short-landscape trim that hides nonessential triage/quick-reply chrome while keeping order selection, message input, Close conversation, and Login/Send controls reachable. `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.test.tsx:29-48` guards the source breakpoint contract and compact-sheet CSS.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime mobile-landscape screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Align the support widget breakpoint with the navigation breakpoint or add a short-height landscape rule. The open support state should either become a compact full-width/top-bottom sheet or reserve enough visible height for header, messages, composer, text input, and primary actions. Verify `740x360`, `844x390`, `390x844`, and `360x740` with unauthenticated support open, ensuring the message area is meaningfully readable and the login/send controls are visible or clearly reachable without hidden clipped overflow.

### F2858: HIGH — Pet Gallery sticky toolbar collides with the global bottom navigation on mobile

- Area: Frontend / Pet Gallery mobile sticky actions
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190/pet-gallery`, current-source CRA dev server, viewports `390x844` and `360x740`
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: The Pet Gallery `Latest community moments` toolbar becomes sticky on mobile, but it is not offset from the global fixed bottom navigation. At `390x844`, the toolbar sticks near the bottom of the first viewport while the bottom nav covers the following `Gallery momentum` section. At `360x740`, the bottom nav directly overlays the toolbar's `Log in to upload` button, leaving the control visible behind navigation chrome and visually/tactually ambiguous. This is the page's primary refresh/upload control surface, so the collision blocks the main gallery workflow on short mobile screens.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1805-storefront-recheck-codex/pet-gallery-mobile-viewport.png`; `pet-gallery-compact-viewport.png`; `pet-gallery-mobile-full.png`; `pet-gallery-compact-full.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1805-storefront-recheck-codex/report.json` records `.shop-nav__bottomBar` as `position=fixed rect=8,772,374,62 z=1250` on mobile and `rect=6,668,344,62 z=1250` on compact. The same report records `.pet-gallery-toolbar` as `position=sticky rect=6,656,378,110 z=8` on mobile and `rect=5,656,350,162 z=8` on compact; top-at-point bottom-center hits the bottom nav with the toolbar/action content directly underneath.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/PetGallery.css` added mobile bottom padding to `.pet-gallery-page`, then set `.pet-gallery-toolbar { position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 8; }` under `@media (max-width: 620px)` and later turned the toolbar into a one-column action grid. `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css` keeps `.shop-nav__bottomBar` fixed above the app shell with a higher z-index, so the page-level sticky toolbar could not reserve or escape the bottom-nav area.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/PetGallery.tsx:313-328` keeps the `Latest community moments` toolbar with login/upload and refresh actions. `frontend/src/pages/PetGallery.css:1691-1734` adds the F2858 mobile override: bottom-nav-aware page padding, `position: sticky` with `top: auto` and a `bottom: calc(var(--shop-mobile-bottom-nav-height, 76px) + 12px + env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px))` offset, compact two-column toolbar actions, and bottom-nav-aware scroll margin for the Gallery momentum section. `frontend/src/pages/PetGallery.test.ts:7-21` guards the source and CSS contracts.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/PetGallery.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime mobile screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Either remove sticky positioning from the Pet Gallery toolbar on mobile, make it stick below the header rather than near the bottom content transition, or coordinate it with `--shop-mobile-bottom-nav-height` so it never sits under the global bottom bar. Verify `Latest community moments`, `Log in to upload`, `Refresh`, and the start of `Gallery momentum` are all readable and tappable at `390x844`, `360x740`, and `430px` widths.

### F2857: MEDIUM — Unauthenticated Wishlist redirect stacks duplicate login-required toasts

- Area: Frontend / Wishlist auth redirect and mobile notification layout
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190/wishlist`, unauthenticated current-source CRA dev server, viewports `390x844` and `360x740`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: Opening Wishlist while logged out redirects to `/login`, but two identical `Please log in first` AntD messages remain stacked at the top of the login page. On mobile this places duplicate warning boxes over the sticky storefront header/search area, so the first thing the customer sees after redirect is repeated system feedback instead of a single clear login prompt. The duplication also makes the auth redirect feel like two separate failures.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1805-storefront-recheck-codex/wishlist-mobile-viewport.png`; `wishlist-compact-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1805-storefront-recheck-codex/report.json` records both Wishlist mobile and compact runs ending at `http://127.0.0.1:4190/login`, with `bodyTextStart` containing `Please log in first` twice and the top-center hit stack on `.ant-message-notice-content` showing `Please log in first`.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/Wishlist.tsx` ran a mount effect that called `message.warning(t('messages.loginRequired'))` and immediately `navigate(buildLoginUrlFromWindow())` when no token exists. The CRA entry in `frontend/src/index.tsx` wraps the app in `React.StrictMode`, so development mounts can double-run this effect; the message call had no stable key/dedupe guard to collapse repeated auth-redirect warnings.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/Wishlist.tsx:19` defines `WISHLIST_LOGIN_REQUIRED_MESSAGE_KEY`, and `frontend/src/pages/Wishlist.tsx:115-123` uses `message.open({ key: WISHLIST_LOGIN_REQUIRED_MESSAGE_KEY, type: 'warning', content: t('messages.loginRequired') })` before redirecting, so repeated unauthenticated mount effects update one AntD notice instead of stacking duplicates. `frontend/src/pages/Wishlist.test.tsx:69-78` guards the stable-key contract and rejects the old unkeyed warning call.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/Wishlist.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅ with existing React/React Router warning noise; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime mobile redirect screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Gate the Wishlist auth redirect warning with a ref/session flag or use an AntD message key so repeated mount/effect execution updates the existing toast instead of creating another one. Verify unauthenticated `/wishlist` shows exactly one login-required message at `390x844`, `360x740`, and desktop, and that the message does not cover the login form or navigation controls.

### F2855: HIGH — Storefront landscape first viewport is consumed by fixed navigation

- Area: Frontend / Storefront mobile landscape navigation layout
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190`, current-source CRA dev server, viewport `740x360` mobile landscape; reproduced on `/`, `/products?q=feeder&sort=price_desc`, `/products/1`, and `/coupons`
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: In mobile landscape, the storefront header remains a full stacked mobile commerce header: announcement, brand/action row, category chip rail, search bar, and search suggestions. It occupies about 258px of a 360px-high viewport. On most public routes the fixed bottom navigation then occupies another 62px at the bottom, leaving only a thin sliver of actual page content visible. The home page first viewport shows only the bottom of the hero headline, product search lands with the content title hidden behind the bottom nav, coupons starts under the bottom nav, and product detail shows only a blank gallery edge/discount badge after the 258px header. A landscape phone user must scroll before seeing meaningful page content or primary shopping controls.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1805-storefront-recheck-codex/home-landscape-viewport.png`; `products-search-landscape-viewport.png`; `product-detail-landscape-viewport.png`; `coupons-landscape-viewport.png`; `home-landscape-full.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1805-storefront-recheck-codex/report.json` records `header.shop-nav shop-nav--en` as `position=sticky rect=0,0,740,258 z=1300`. On home/products/coupons it also records `.shop-nav__bottomBar` as `position=fixed rect=8,288,724,62 z=1250`, with bottom-center/top stacks hitting the bottom nav at `y=336`. Product detail suppresses the bottom bar, but the same report records its product grid starting at `y=258`, leaving only about 102px of the first viewport for content.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css` applied the same `@media (max-width: 780px)` mobile header composition to short landscape screens, including the announcement, `.shop-nav__mega`, `.shop-nav__search`, suggestion chips, and the fixed `.shop-nav__bottomBar`. The file had multiple mobile refinement blocks for these elements but no `max-height`/landscape-specific compact mode that reduced the header stack or coordinated first-viewport clearance with the bottom navigation.
- Fix: `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css:2666-2769` adds the F2855 short mobile landscape compact mode: the announcement, top utility row, mega/category rail, suggestion/search signal chrome, mobile tools, and bottom nav are hidden at `max-width: 780px` and `max-height: 430px`; the main header becomes a compact `brand search actions` row; the search control remains 44px tall; and the action cluster stays nowrap/scrollable with 44px auth/action targets. `frontend/src/components/Navbar.test.tsx:275-286` guards the compact short-landscape CSS contract.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/components/Navbar.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅ with existing React Router/AntD/act warning noise; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime storefront landscape screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: Add a landscape-specific storefront nav layout for short mobile viewports, such as collapsing suggestions/category chips behind menus, reducing the sticky header to a single compact row plus search affordance, or disabling the bottom bar on short landscape routes where primary page content is otherwise hidden. Verify home hero copy, product list controls/title, coupon hero, and product-detail gallery/title are meaningfully visible at `740x360` and `844x390`, with no bottom nav covering first-screen content.

### F2810: HIGH — Mobile admin dashboard sticky hero clips the commercial-readiness score

- Area: Frontend / Admin Dashboard mobile layout
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190/admin/dashboard`, authenticated `SUPER_ADMIN`, current-source CRA dev server, viewport `390x844`
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: The mobile admin dashboard keeps the hero summary as a sticky card, but the card's fixed/clipped height is too short for its contents and it sits above the following commercial-readiness section. In the first mobile viewport, the `92` go-live score starts immediately under the sticky hero and its top is visibly cut off, so the primary readiness number looks partially hidden. The hero itself also clips its own content (`DASHBOARD`, `Dashboard`, `Operations action center`, `Tracking coverage`), making the top dashboard summary feel broken before the operator reaches the rest of the page.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1752-admin-recheck-codex/admin-dashboard-mobile-viewport.png`; `admin-dashboard-mobile-full.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1752-admin-recheck-codex/report.json` records `.admin-dashboard__hero` at `x=15 y=230 w=360 h=160` with `scrollWidth=398 clientWidth=358` and `scrollHeight=198 clientHeight=158`, plus `overflow=hidden/hidden/hidden`. The same report records the following readiness/status rails starting directly below that clipped sticky hero.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboard.css` made `.admin-dashboard__hero` `position: sticky` under `@media (max-width: 720px)` with `z-index: 8`, while the shared dashboard polish set `.admin-dashboard__hero { overflow: hidden; }`. Later mobile rules turned `.admin-dashboard__heroMeta` into a horizontal rail with fixed `128px` items, but the hero container did not gain enough height or bottom clearance for the sticky state.
- Fix: `frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboard.css:1360-1392` adds the F2810 mobile override: narrow dashboards use a normal relative hero with `top: auto`, `height: auto`, `max-height: none`, visible overflow, and readiness spacing/scroll margin, while the decorative hero pseudo-element is disabled and the metric rail is allowed to show its full height. `frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboard.test.tsx:223-234` guards the CSS contract.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/AdminDashboard.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅ with existing React/React Router warning noise; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅. Runtime mobile admin screenshots are queued for E2E because this pass was a source/test fix, not a new Playwright capture run.
- Expected fix direction: On mobile, either avoid sticky positioning for the dashboard hero or let it expand to fit the two-line meta rail and reserve vertical clearance before the commercial-readiness section. Verify the full `92` readiness score, hero heading, and both hero metric cards are visible at `390x844`, `360x740`, and `430px` widths without overlap or clipping.

### F2788: CRITICAL — Admin bootstrap endpoint is publicly accessible without authentication
- **Status**: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `SecurityConfig.java:102`, `UserController.java:136-181`, `UserService.java:135-170`
- **Impact**: Current source does not expose `POST /admin/bootstrap/first-super-admin` or an `AdminUserBootstrapController`. The only anonymous bootstrap route is `POST /users/create-admin`, which is `permitAll()` solely to support first-admin bootstrap before any admin session exists; it is controller-gated by configured `admin.bootstrap-token`, required `X-Bootstrap-Token`, constant-time comparison, audit/IP failure logging, and `UserService.registerAdmin()` admin-count plus DB bootstrap-lock checks. Missing, blank, or invalid bootstrap tokens reject before `registerAdmin()` is called, and an existing admin still blocks the flow.
- **Evidence**: `SecurityConfig.java:102` permits only `POST /users/create-admin` for bootstrap while `/admin/**` remains admin-gated at `SecurityConfig.java:127` and `.anyRequest().authenticated()` remains at `SecurityConfig.java:128`. `UserController.java:136-181` requires `X-Bootstrap-Token`, reads configured `admin.bootstrap-token`, rejects blank configuration, and uses `constantTimeEquals(adminBootstrapToken, bootstrapToken.trim())`. `UserService.java:135-170` acquires the admin bootstrap lock, rejects when `countAdminUsers() > 0`, validates/normalizes credentials, creates an `ADMIN`, and releases the lock.
- **Regression guard**: `SecurityConfigCorsTest.java:96-122` now rejects the stale `/admin/bootstrap/first-super-admin` route and guards the narrow controller-gated `/users/create-admin` contract. `UserControllerAdminBootstrapTest.java:36-89` verifies existing-admin rejection, missing-token rejection before `registerAdmin()`, and not-configured rejection before `registerAdmin()`.
- **Verification**: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=SecurityConfigCorsTest,UserControllerAdminBootstrapTest,UserServiceTest test` ✅.
- **Severity**: CRITICAL
- **Dimension**: Security

### F2789: CRITICAL — Config center allows runtime modification of JWT secret, CORS origins, and session timeout
- **Status**: FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `ConfigCenterService.java:45-88`, `ConfigCenterService.java:429-437`, `ConfigCenterService.java:646-666`, `application.properties:278`, `GlobalApiExceptionHandler.java:37-64`
- **Impact**: Config Center now rejects protected runtime/security keys instead of allowing admins to hot-apply JWT, CORS/WebSocket origin, session, bootstrap, Stripe, Spring/server/management/MyBatis, or logging file/pattern changes through Nacos/runtime overrides. The current controller surface is `POST /admin/config-center/publish` and `POST /admin/config-center/apply`, not the stale `PUT/PATCH /admin/config/{group}/{key}` paths. `GlobalApiExceptionHandler` already preserves `ResponseStatusException` reasons and bad-request messages while masking unexpected 500 details.
- **Fix**: `ConfigCenterService.java:45-88` removes `app.cors.*` and `app.websocket.*` from the default allowlist and introduces `PROTECTED_CONFIG_KEY_PATTERN` covering current keys (`app.jwtSecret`, `app.cors.*`, `app.websocket.*`) plus stale/reported names (`security.jwt.*`, `security.cors.*`, `security.session.*`) and infrastructure keys. `ConfigCenterService.java:429-437` excludes protected keys from runtime application, and `ConfigCenterService.java:646-666` rejects and filters protected keys even when `admin.config-center.allowed-key-prefixes` is overridden. `application.properties:278` removes CORS/WebSocket origin prefixes from the default environment allowlist.
- **Regression guard**: `ConfigCenterServiceTest.java:80-109` verifies protected JWT/CORS/WebSocket/session keys are rejected, runtime apply is skipped, safe order config is not partially applied, and protected prefixes are not exposed in the response allowlist. `GlobalApiExceptionHandlerTest` remains green for preserved response-status/bad-request messages and masked unexpected 500s.
- **Verification**: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=ConfigCenterServiceTest,GlobalApiExceptionHandlerTest test` ✅.
- **Severity**: CRITICAL
- **Dimension**: Security

### F2790: HIGH — Guest order mutation endpoints accessible with only a guessable guestToken
- **Status**: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `SecurityConfig.java:87-94`, `OrderController.java:224-245`, `OrderController.java:316-340`, `OrderController.java:365-386`, `OrderController.java:414-438`, `OrderController.java:498-521`, `OrderService.java:1120-1149`, `PaymentController.java:269-289`, `PaymentController.java:302-317`, `RateLimitService.java:176-182`, `RateLimitService.java:314-323`, `RateLimitService.java:374-385`
- **Impact**: The reported `PATCH/POST /guest/orders/{guestToken}/...` paths are stale against current source. There is no current mutation endpoint that accepts only a 6-digit `guestToken`; current public guest mutations are `PUT /orders/guest/{id}/cancel`, `PUT /orders/guest/{id}/confirm`, `PUT /orders/guest/{id}/return`, `PUT /orders/guest/{id}/return-shipment`, plus `POST /payments` for payment creation.
- **Evidence**: `SecurityConfig.java:87-94` permits only current `/orders/guest/**` and payment routes at the security boundary. `OrderController.java:224-245`, `316-340`, `365-386`, and `414-438` pass guest request bodies through the shared resolver before cancelling, confirming, returning, or submitting return shipment data. `OrderController.java:498-521` accepts a guest operation only when `guestOrderAccessMatches(order, guestEmail, orderNo)` succeeds; otherwise it records a guest-order operation failure and returns forbidden. `OrderService.java:1120-1149` requires matching order number, guest-order classification, and normalized guest email, so registered-customer orders are not exposed through the guest path. `PaymentController.java:269-289` and `302-317` apply the same guest credential check before anonymous payment creation or payment operations. `RateLimitService.java:176-182`, `314-323`, and `374-385` configure and apply a dedicated `orders:guest-mutation` per-client limit across guest mutation endpoints.
- **Regression guard**: `OrderControllerGuestAfterSaleAccessTest.java:35-118` covers legitimate matching guest after-sale operations and rejects missing credentials before mutation. `PaymentControllerSimulationAccessTest.java:130-246` rejects anonymous registered-order and missing-credential payment attempts while allowing matching guest credentials. `OrderInputNormalizationServiceTest.java:111-121` rejects registered orders even when email and order number match. `RateLimitServiceTest.java:150-179` verifies guest mutation endpoints share the dedicated per-client mutation bucket across IDs.
- **Verification**: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=OrderControllerGuestAfterSaleAccessTest,PaymentControllerSimulationAccessTest,OrderInputNormalizationServiceTest,RateLimitServiceTest test` passed.
- **Severity**: HIGH
- **Dimension**: Security

### F2791: HIGH — JWT secret stored as plaintext in database runtime config
- **Status**: FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `JwtService.java:25-31`, `JwtService.java:115-130`, `ConfigCenterService.java:82-88`, `ConfigCenterService.java:646-666`
- **Impact**: The exact reported `RuntimeConfig.security.jwt.secret`, `resolveHmacSecret()`, `RuntimeConfig.java`, and `admin123456` database fallback are stale against current source, but the remaining risk was valid because the JWT signing secret was still read via `RuntimeConfigService`. `JwtService` now captures `${app.jwtSecret}` once at construction from startup deployment configuration, trims it, and uses the captured value for token generation and parsing; runtime/admin config changes cannot replace the signing key in a live process. Existing validation still rejects blank, placeholder, and shorter-than-32-character secrets.
- **Fix**: `JwtService.java:25-31` stores the startup-injected secret in a final field instead of resolving it through `RuntimeConfigService`. `JwtService.java:115-130` validates and signs with that captured secret. `ConfigCenterService.java:82-88` and `646-666` continue to reject and filter `app.jwt*` and `security.jwt.*` config keys so Config Center cannot publish or apply JWT signing material.
- **Regression guard**: `JwtServiceTest.java:98-123` verifies token signing/parsing succeeds with the construction-time secret without any `runtimeConfig.getString("app.jwtSecret", "")` call, and source-guards against stale `security.jwt.secret` or `admin123456` fallback strings. `ConfigCenterServiceTest.java:80-109` continues to reject protected JWT keys through the admin Config Center path.
- **Verification**: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=JwtServiceTest,ConfigCenterServiceTest test` passed.
- **Severity**: HIGH
- **Dimension**: Security

### F2792: HIGH — Stock reservation race condition allows overselling
- **Status**: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `OrderService.java:189-213`, `OrderService.java:272-297`, `OrderService.java:426-457`, `OrderService.java:469-510`, `OrderService.java:528-540`, `OrderService.java:627-640`, `ProductRepository.java:89-95`
- **Impact**: The reported `reserveStock()` line references are stale against current source. Active registered checkout and guest checkout enter through `@Transactional` service methods, pass `reserveStock=true`, normalize and sort product IDs, load products through `ProductRepository.findAllByIdForUpdate(...)`, validate current product/variant stock, and only then mutate stock with `reserveProductStock(...)` inside the same transaction. The in-memory decrement is applied to a pessimistically locked product row, so two concurrent checkouts for the same product cannot both read and reserve the same stock count.
- **Evidence**: `OrderService.java:189-213` and `272-297` show registered and guest checkout reach stock reservation from transactional entrypoints. `OrderService.java:426-457` and `469-510` reserve registered/guest items only after loading products for update and validating active status, selection, and stock. `OrderService.java:627-640` sorts product IDs and selects `findAllByIdForUpdate(...)` when reservation is requested. `ProductRepository.java:89-95` defines the pessimistic write-lock queries. `OrderService.java:528-540` performs the product/variant stock mutation on those locked entities.
- **Regression guard**: `OrderStockReservationServiceTest.java:29-44` verifies guest checkout reservation calls `findAllByIdForUpdate(List.of(7L))`, does not call unlocked `findAllById(List.of(7L))`, saves the product, and decrements stock from 3 to 1.
- **Verification**: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=OrderStockReservationServiceTest test` passed.
- **Severity**: HIGH
- **Dimension**: Data Integrity / Concurrency

### F2793: HIGH — N+1 user lookups in order list queries
- **Status**: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `OrderController.java:116-140`, `OrderController.java:171-189`, `AdminController.java:1048-1088`, `OrderService.java:933-946`, `OrderService.java:1170-1183`, `OrderService.java:1939-1949`, `OrderMapper.xml:44-73`, `OrderMapper.xml:239-278`
- **Impact**: The reported `AdminOrderController` path and controller-side `userMapper.selectById(...)` enrichment loop are stale against current source. Customer order lists use bounded `getOrdersByUserId(userId, page, size)`, admin `/admin/orders` and `/admin/orders/page` share the bounded page builder, and `OrderMapper` selects customer display fields with a single `LEFT JOIN users` per page. Current `enrichReturnInfo(...)` computes return metadata and guest status from already-selected order/customer columns; it does not query users per order.
- **Evidence**: `OrderController.java:116-140` returns customer order pages and `OrderController.java:171-189` caps admin-compatible `/orders` reads. `AdminController.java:1048-1088` builds paged admin order responses from `countAdminOrders(...)` and `searchAdminOrders(...)`. `OrderService.java:933-946` maps admin paging to `searchAdminOrders(..., offset, safeSize)` and `OrderService.java:1170-1183` maps customer paging to `findByUserIdPage(...)`. `OrderMapper.xml:44-73` defines `orderCustomerSelectColumns`; `OrderMapper.xml:239-278` uses those columns plus `LEFT JOIN users` and `LIMIT/OFFSET` for admin search/count paths.
- **Regression guard**: `OrderStatsServiceTest.java:111-132` source-guards the paged service/controller paths, the joined customer column mapper contract, `LIMIT/OFFSET`, and absence of `userMapper`/`userMapper.selectById` loops in order-list code.
- **Verification**: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=OrderStatsServiceTest,AdminControllerOrderPageTest,OrderControllerCustomerPaginationTest test` passed.
- **Severity**: HIGH
- **Dimension**: Performance

### F2794: MEDIUM — CORS uses private network `*` origins as default
- **Status**: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `SecurityConfig.java:142-167`, `CorsOriginProperties.java:15-55`, `CorsOriginProperties.java:63-82`, `SecurityConfigCorsTest.java:47-90`, `ApplicationProfileContractTest.java:17-43`, `ApplicationProfileContractTest.java:91-96`
- **Impact**: The reported private-LAN default and `allowPrivateNetwork(true)` CORS behavior is stale against current source. `SecurityConfig` builds an explicit header/method/origin allowlist and does not enable private-network CORS. `CorsOriginProperties` uses the deployed HTTPS storefront in production, filters production origins to HTTPS public non-wildcard hosts, and keeps non-production fallback to loopback browser origins only. Checked-in base/dev defaults exclude `10.*`, `172.*`, and `192.168.*` private-LAN wildcards; device testing can still explicitly opt into a concrete LAN origin outside production.
- **Evidence**: `SecurityConfig.java:142-167` sets allowed origin patterns from `CorsOriginProperties` without `allowPrivateNetwork(true)`. `CorsOriginProperties.java:15-55` defines production and loopback-only non-production fallbacks, and `CorsOriginProperties.java:63-82` rejects wildcard, local, private, non-HTTPS, credentialed, and malformed origins in production.
- **Regression guard**: `SecurityConfigCorsTest.java:47-90` verifies production filtering, non-production fallback without private-LAN wildcards, explicit non-production device-origin opt-in, and absence of `allowPrivateNetwork(true)`. `ApplicationProfileContractTest.java:17-43` and `91-96` guard checked-in base/dev profile CORS defaults against private-LAN wildcards.
- **Verification**: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=SecurityConfigCorsTest,ApplicationProfileContractTest test` passed.
- **Severity**: MEDIUM
- **Dimension**: Security

### F2795: MEDIUM — Login timing side-channel reveals valid email addresses
- **Status**: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `LoginController.java:44-130`, `CustomUserDetailsService.java:16-22`, `LoginControllerPasswordLoginTest.java`, `CustomUserDetailsServiceTest.java`
- **Impact**: The report's `loginOrRegisterAndBind()` and `userService.findByEmail(normalized)` paths are stale; current source has neither method/path. Current `/auth/login` normalizes the supplied identifier, optionally resolves it to the canonical username for real accounts, and still calls `authenticationManager.authenticate(...)` for syntactically valid unknown accounts instead of returning immediately. The active `CustomUserDetailsService` throws `UsernameNotFoundException` for missing users, and Spring Security's `DaoAuthenticationProvider` prepares and verifies a dummy `userNotFoundPassword` hash on that exception path before the controller returns the same generic invalid-credentials response.
- **Evidence**: `LoginController.java:62-90` builds an authentication token and delegates to `authenticationManager.authenticate(...)` after lookup/rate-limit/lockout checks; unknown accounts keep the normalized submitted login as the authentication principal. `CustomUserDetailsService.java:16-22` uses `findByUsernameOrPhoneOrEmail(...)` and throws `UsernameNotFoundException` when absent, which the default provider handles with missing-user timing mitigation.
- **Regression guard**: `LoginControllerPasswordLoginTest.unknownPasswordLoginStillDelegatesToAuthenticationManager` verifies an unknown email login still reaches `AuthenticationManager` with the normalized principal and records the same generic failure path. `CustomUserDetailsServiceTest.defaultDaoProviderRunsDummyPasswordMatchWhenLoginIsMissing` verifies `DaoAuthenticationProvider` encodes `userNotFoundPassword` and calls `PasswordEncoder.matches(...)` against the dummy hash for missing users.
- **Verification**: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=LoginControllerPasswordLoginTest,CustomUserDetailsServiceTest test` passed.
- **Severity**: MEDIUM
- **Dimension**: Security

### F2796: MEDIUM — Guest checkout has no rate limiting
- **Status**: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `SecurityConfig.java:89`, `RateLimitFilter.java:31-49`, `RateLimitService.java:176`, `RateLimitService.java:342-344`, `application.properties:51`, `RateLimitServiceTest.java:183-195`
- **Impact**: The reported `POST /guest/orders` path is stale; current source has no anonymous `/guest/orders` order-creation route. Active guest checkout is `POST /orders/checkout/guest`, and it is intentionally anonymous but not unthrottled: `SecurityConfig` permits that exact route, `RateLimitFilter` still invokes `RateLimitService` for all non-skipped requests, and `RateLimitService` maps the route to a dedicated `checkout:guest` per-client bucket with a 3600-second window. The default deployment/config-center value is `traffic.rate-limit.guest-checkout-per-hour=10`.
- **Evidence**: `OrderController.java:87-93` exposes only `/orders/checkout/guest` for guest checkout; legacy `POST /orders` is authenticated and disabled. `SecurityConfig.java:89` permits `/orders/checkout/guest`, while `SecurityConfig.java:132` adds `rateLimitFilter`. `RateLimitService.java:342-344` returns `new EndpointLimit("POST", "checkout:guest", config.guestCheckoutPerHour, 3600)` for that path, with the configured default in `application.properties:51`.
- **Regression guard**: `RateLimitServiceTest.guestCheckoutEndpointHasDedicatedHourlyPerClientLimit` verifies the active route allows requests up to `traffic.rate-limit.guest-checkout-per-hour`, rejects the next request with the dedicated limit, and records `checkout:guest` hot-bucket telemetry.
- **Verification**: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=RateLimitServiceTest test` passed.
- **Severity**: MEDIUM
- **Dimension**: Security / Availability

### F2797: MEDIUM — CSRF disabled globally with CORS credentials enabled
- **Status**: WONTFIX / CURRENT_SOURCE_NON_ISSUE / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `SecurityConfig.java:49-54`, `SecurityConfig.java:142-164`, `JwtAuthenticationFilter.java:35-40`, `frontend/src/api/index.ts:1425-1460`, `SecurityConfigCorsTest.java:93-115`
- **Impact**: The reported cookie-backed CSRF threat model does not match current source. `csrf().disable()` is intentional because the API is stateless and authenticates unsafe requests with explicit JWT bearer headers, not ambient browser cookies or server sessions. `SecurityConfig` uses `SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS`, `JwtAuthenticationFilter` reads only the `Authorization: Bearer ...` header, and the frontend client injects `Authorization` without `withCredentials`. Credentialed CORS remains narrowed by the F2794 production-origin filtering guard.
- **Evidence**: `SecurityConfig.java:49-54` documents the stateless bearer-token contract and disables CSRF only with `SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS`. `JwtAuthenticationFilter.java:35-40` ignores requests without a bearer Authorization header. `frontend/src/api/index.ts:1425-1460` attaches stored JWTs as `Authorization: Bearer ...`; no `withCredentials` mode is configured. `SecurityConfig.java:142-164` uses explicit allowed headers including `Authorization`, not wildcard headers, and F2794 guards production CORS origin filtering.
- **Regression guard**: `SecurityConfigCorsTest.csrfDisabledContractRequiresStatelessBearerAuth` verifies CSRF disablement stays paired with stateless sessions, source documentation, bearer-header JWT authentication, absence of cookie-based JWT extraction, and a frontend client that uses Authorization headers without `withCredentials`.
- **Verification**: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=SecurityConfigCorsTest test` passed.
- **Severity**: MEDIUM
- **Dimension**: Security

### F2798: MEDIUM — AppConfig endpoint leaks runtime mode and build info
- **Status**: FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `AppConfigController.java:23-29`, `AppConfigResponse.java:10-13`, `frontend/src/types.ts:1176-1180`, `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:523`, `AppConfigControllerTest.java:20-44`
- **Impact**: The report's `GET /config`, mobile version, `appId`, and `buildTime` fields are stale against current source, but the active anonymous `GET /app/config` response still exposed `runtimeMode` and `paymentSimulationEnabled`. That let unauthenticated clients infer deployment mode and whether payment simulation was enabled. The public app config now returns only storefront-safe settings needed before login: email-code availability, default shipping fee, and free-shipping threshold.
- **Evidence**: `AppConfigController.java:23-29` now constructs `AppConfigResponse` without reading `app.runtime-mode` or payment simulation settings. `AppConfigResponse.java:10-13` contains only `emailCodeEnabled`, `defaultShippingFee`, and `freeShippingThreshold`. `frontend/src/types.ts:1176-1180` mirrors the sanitized public contract, and `Checkout.tsx:523` no longer derives the simulation payment UI from anonymous app config.
- **Regression guard**: `AppConfigControllerTest.publicAppConfigOmitsRuntimeBuildAndSimulationState` serializes the response and verifies it includes the safe storefront fields while excluding `runtimeMode`, `paymentSimulationEnabled`, `buildTime`, `mobileVersionCode`, `mobileVersionName`, and `appId`, and verifies the controller does not read runtime mode or payment simulation config keys.
- **Verification**: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=AppConfigControllerTest test` passed; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed; `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/Checkout.test.tsx src/api/index.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` passed with existing React/AntD warning noise.
- **Severity**: MEDIUM
- **Dimension**: Security

### F2799: MEDIUM — Stock restoration uses non-atomic increment (race condition)
- **Status**: FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `OrderService.java:1594-1609`, `ProductRepository.java:102-105`, `OrderStockReservationServiceTest.java:59-115`
- **Impact**: Simple-product stock restoration no longer uses a Java read-modify-write entity save. `restoreStock(...)` now validates the order item, and when no variant specs are present it calls the atomic repository update path and returns. The SQL update increments stock in one database statement, including `coalesce(stock, 0)`, so concurrent cancellations/refunds cannot overwrite each other's simple-stock restorations. Variant JSON restorations still use `findByIdForUpdate(...)` before mutating and saving because the variant stock lives inside the locked product row.
- **Evidence**: `OrderService.java:1598-1609` calls `productRepository.increaseStock(productId, quantity)` for simple restocks and logs if no row was updated. `ProductRepository.java:102-105` performs `update products set stock = coalesce(stock, 0) + ?2 ... where id = ?1`. `OrderService.java:1610-1628` keeps the pessimistic-lock entity path only for selected-spec/variant restorations.
- **Regression guard**: `OrderStockReservationServiceTest.restoresSimpleProductStockWithAtomicRepositoryIncrement` verifies simple restocks call `increaseStock(...)` and never load/save the product entity; `restoresVariantOnlyStockWithoutCreatingProductStock` continues to guard the locked variant JSON path; `restoreStockLogsMissingProductInsteadOfSilentlySkipping` verifies zero-row atomic updates are logged without a silent skip.
- **Verification**: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=OrderStockReservationServiceTest test` passed.
- **Severity**: MEDIUM
- **Dimension**: Data Integrity / Concurrency

### F2800: MEDIUM — Error handler discards custom exception messages
- **Status**: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `GlobalApiExceptionHandler.java:52-64`, `GlobalApiExceptionHandler.java:120-142`, `GlobalApiExceptionHandlerTest.java:82-99`
- **Impact**: The reported `GlobalExceptionHandler.java` and `handleRuntimeException()` path are stale. Current source uses `GlobalApiExceptionHandler`, and business `IllegalArgumentException`/`IllegalStateException` failures are handled as HTTP 400 with the exception's sanitized message. Only unexpected `Exception` falls through to the generic internal-server-error response to avoid leaking internals.
- **Evidence**: `GlobalApiExceptionHandler.java:52-64` handles `IllegalArgumentException` and `IllegalStateException` through `handleBadRequest(...)`. `GlobalApiExceptionHandler.java:120-142` preserves validation-specific messages and otherwise returns `exception.getMessage()` or `"Bad request"`. `GlobalApiExceptionHandler.java:99-105` keeps generic `"Internal server error"` only for unexpected exceptions.
- **Regression guard**: `GlobalApiExceptionHandlerTest.businessIllegalStateMessagesAreReturnedAsBadRequest` verifies `IllegalStateException("Insufficient stock")` returns HTTP 400 with `"Insufficient stock"` and source-guards against reintroducing `handleRuntimeException` or `"Server error, please try again later"`.
- **Verification**: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=GlobalApiExceptionHandlerTest test` passed.
- **Severity**: MEDIUM
- **Dimension**: UX / API Design

### F2801: LOW — SecurityAuditLogManagement has hardcoded strings not internationalized
- **Status**: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `SecurityAuditLogManagement.tsx`, `SecurityAuditLogManagement.test.ts:35-39`
- **Impact**: The reported hardcoded audit-trace headings are stale against current source. `SecurityAuditLogManagement.tsx` no longer contains `"Full link trace"`, `"Top 10 slow requests"`, `"完整链路追踪"`, or `"前 10 个慢请求"`; those strings only remain as negative assertions in the focused regression guard.
- **Evidence**: Static search found no matching stale headings in `SecurityAuditLogManagement.tsx`. `SecurityAuditLogManagement.test.ts` now asserts the page source does not contain the stale English or Chinese hardcoded headings while preserving the existing i18n label tests.
- **Verification**: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/SecurityAuditLogManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` passed.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: Internationalization

### F2802: LOW — ProductManagement action buttons missing aria labels
- **Status**: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `ProductManagement.tsx:1794-1887`, `ProductManagement.test.ts:9-24`
- **Impact**: The reported icon-only missing-label issue is stale against current source. Product row actions now build product-specific accessible names for feature, edit, duplicate, approve, reject, review, and delete actions, and apply those names through `aria-label` and `title` props. The edit action also keeps visible localized text.
- **Evidence**: `ProductManagement.tsx` defines `featureActionLabel`, `editActionLabel`, `duplicateActionLabel`, `approveActionLabel`, `rejectActionLabel`, `reviewActionLabel`, and `deleteActionLabel` from the current product name, then passes them to the relevant row action buttons and confirmation buttons.
- **Regression guard**: `ProductManagement.test.ts` now checks the row action renderer keeps product-specific accessible names on icon-only and text buttons.
- **Verification**: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/ProductManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` passed.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: Accessibility

### F2803: LOW — SystemMonitor uses hardcoded `t()` calls instead of translation keys
- **Status**: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `SystemMonitor.tsx:222,239`, `SystemMonitor.test.ts:12-25`
- **Impact**: The reported raw-key calls are stale against current source. `SystemMonitor.tsx` no longer calls `t('common.loading')`, `t('system.cpu')`, or `t('system.memory')`; runtime metric labels use page-scoped `pages.systemMonitor.*` keys.
- **Evidence**: Current source renders CPU cores with `t('pages.systemMonitor.cpuCores')` and JVM memory with `t('pages.systemMonitor.jvmMemory')`, plus page-scoped `used` and `free` labels. English, Chinese, and Spanish locale files all define those keys.
- **Regression guard**: `SystemMonitor.test.ts` now rejects the stale raw-key calls and asserts the current metric keys exist in all supported locale files.
- **Verification**: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/SystemMonitor.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` passed.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: Internationalization

### F2804: LOW — Error boundary uses hardcoded Chinese `data-testid` attributes
- **Status**: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `ErrorBoundary.tsx:93-96`, `ErrorBoundary.test.ts:10-25`
- **Impact**: The reported hardcoded fallback is stale against current source. `ErrorBoundary.tsx` no longer renders `data-testid="error-fallback"` or hardcoded visible `"Error"` fallback text; fallback copy is injected from localized `errorBoundary.*` keys.
- **Evidence**: `ErrorBoundary.tsx` calls `useLanguage()` and maps title, subtitle, retry, and back-home text through `t('errorBoundary.title')`, `t('errorBoundary.subtitle')`, `t('errorBoundary.retry')`, and `t('errorBoundary.backHome')`. English, Chinese, and Spanish locale files all define those keys.
- **Regression guard**: `ErrorBoundary.test.ts` rejects the stale test id and hardcoded visible `"Error"` fallback while asserting the locale keys exist in all supported locales.
- **Verification**: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` passed.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: Internationalization

### F2805: LOW — AddressManager `data-testid` attributes use Chinese strings
- **Status**: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `Profile.tsx:1174,1395,1947`, `Profile.test.ts:9-22`
- **Impact**: The reported `AddressManager.tsx` component and hardcoded `"添加新地址"` button text are stale against current source. There is no `AddressManager`, no `data-testid="add-address-button"`, and no hardcoded `"添加新地址"` literal under `frontend/src`.
- **Evidence**: Current address management is in `Profile.tsx`, where the add-address button uses `t('pages.profile.addAddress')` and the address modal title uses `t('pages.profile.addAddressTitle')`. English, Chinese, and Spanish locale files define those keys.
- **Regression guard**: `Profile.test.ts` now rejects the stale component/test-id/Chinese literal and asserts current profile address locale keys exist across supported locales.
- **Verification**: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/Profile.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` passed.

### F2806: LOW — ReturnManagement hardcoded return-request string

- **Impact**: The reported `ReturnManagement.tsx` file and `"Return request for order"` hardcoded string are stale against current source. Active customer return request UI lives in `Profile.tsx` and `OrderTracking.tsx`, where modal titles and reason placeholders use localized Profile return keys.

Static search found no `ReturnManagement` component or `"Return request for order"` literal in active frontend source.

### F2807: LOW — SecurityAuditLogManagement hardcoded admin description string
- **Status**: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `SecurityAuditLogManagement.tsx:1063,1726`, `SecurityAuditLogManagement.test.ts:35-42`
- **Impact**: The reported `'Description: by Admin'` hardcoded string is stale against current source. Current `SecurityAuditLogManagement.tsx` does not contain that literal; audit messages/descriptions render through the localized `messageLabel(...)` path.
- **Evidence**: `SecurityAuditLogManagement.tsx` defines `messageLabel` with `localizedMapValue(auditMessageLabels, language, value)` and renders audit message values as `<span>{messageLabel(value)}</span>`.
- **Regression guard**: `SecurityAuditLogManagement.test.ts` now rejects the stale hardcoded admin-description literal and asserts the localized message-label render path remains present.
