- **Dimension**: Internationalization

### F2808: LOW — RefreshToken entity uses eager fetch for user relationship
- **Status**: WONTFIX / CURRENT_SOURCE_NON_ISSUE / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `TokenBlacklistService.java:56-79`, `LoginController.java:293-317`, `TokenBlacklistServiceTest.java:24-38`
- **Impact**: The reported `RefreshToken.java` JPA entity is absent from current source, so there is no eager `@ManyToOne` user fetch on refresh-token lookup. Current refresh tokens are Redis-backed `refresh:<token>` entries containing only the username.
- **Evidence**: `LoginController.refreshToken(...)` consumes a refresh token via `tokenBlacklistService.consumeRefreshToken(...)`, then loads the current user by username only after token rotation validation. `TokenBlacklistService.storeRefreshToken(...)` writes the username string to Redis, and `consumeRefreshToken(...)` reads/deletes that string without any JPA entity access.
- **Regression guard**: `TokenBlacklistServiceTest.refreshTokensStoreAndConsumeUsernameWithoutJpaRefreshTokenEntity` verifies username-only Redis storage/consume behavior and asserts the old `RefreshToken.java` entity and `RefreshTokenService.java` files are absent.
- **Verification**: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=TokenBlacklistServiceTest test` passed with existing intentional Redis-failure warning noise.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: Performance

### F2809: LOW — User entity lastLoginAt/lastLoginIp never updated on login
- **Status**: WONTFIX / CURRENT_SOURCE_NON_ISSUE / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **File**: `User.java`, `UserMapper.xml`, `schema.sql`, `V1__init.sql`, `UserMapperContractTest.java:15-33`
- **Impact**: The reported `lastLoginAt` and `lastLoginIp` fields are stale against current source. Current `User.java`, `UserMapper.xml`, `schema.sql`, and the initial migration do not define `lastLoginAt`, `lastLoginIp`, `last_login_at`, or `last_login_ip`, so the login flow has no current persisted login timestamp/IP fields to update.
- **Evidence**: Static source/schema search found no current last-login fields or columns. The admin user contract is based on current user fields such as username, email, phone, role/roleCode, status, created/updated timestamps, and password-change timestamp.
- **Regression guard**: `UserMapperContractTest.currentUserContractDoesNotDefineLastLoginFields` asserts the entity, mapper, schema, and initial migration remain free of the stale last-login field/column names.
- **Verification**: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=UserMapperContractTest test` passed.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: Data Integrity

### F2760: HIGH — Admin infrastructure pages overflow and clip primary controls on mobile/landscape

- Area: Frontend / Admin infrastructure operations responsive layouts
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190`, authenticated `SUPER_ADMIN`, current-source CRA dev server, viewports `390x844`, `740x360`, and `768x1024`
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: Several admin infrastructure pages size their hero/action/stat sections wider than the visible admin workspace, then clip the overflow. On mobile Logs, the `Download logs` button is cut at the right edge; on mobile Traffic Control, the `Refresh`, `Clear rate limit`, and `Reset all circuits` actions are rendered as 562px-wide rows inside a 390px viewport; on Alerts landscape/tablet, the hero is 1030px wide and the self-check action is pushed outside the viewport; on IP Blacklist landscape/tablet, the `Manual block` action and right-side filters/cards are offscreen; on Service Registry landscape/tablet, the hero, status cards, and metadata tables extend beyond the visible content area.
- Status: FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1730-admin-current-codex/admin-logs-mobile-viewport.png`; `admin-traffic-control-mobile-viewport.png`; `admin-alerts-landscape-viewport.png`; `admin-ip-blacklist-landscape-full.png`; `admin-registry-landscape-full.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1730-admin-current-codex/report.json` records `.log-management__hero` at `x=15 w=526` in a 390px viewport, Traffic Control actions at `x=30 w=562`, `.alert-management__hero` at `x=99 w=1030` in a 740px viewport, IP Blacklist `Manual block` at `x=1300 w=139`, and `.registry-management__hero` / `.registry-management__stats` at `w=1030`.
- Source fix: `LogManagement.css:430`, `TrafficControl.css:245`, `AlertManagement.css:438`, `IpBlacklistManagement.css:531`, and `RegistryManagement.css:506` now add F2760 landscape/tablet containment blocks. The blocks normalize page/hero/card/filter/table wrapper widths to `min-width: 0` and `max-width: 100%`, convert hero/action groups to grid tracks, show stats as visible two-column grids instead of hidden rails, and keep table/description overflow inside the relevant wrapper rather than stretching or clipping the page shell.
- Regression guard: `AdminInfrastructureResponsive.test.ts` verifies all five infrastructure CSS files include the F2760 `max-width: 900px` / `max-height: 640px` containment block, page overflow containment, hero grid layout, visible stats grid, action grid where applicable, and wrapper-scoped table overflow.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/AdminInfrastructureResponsive.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` passed.

### F2761: MEDIUM — Admin summary/status rails hide critical operational state by default

- Area: Frontend / Admin dashboard, orders, products, support, users, and alerts summary rails
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190`, authenticated `SUPER_ADMIN`, current-source CRA dev server, viewports `390x844` and `740x360`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: Multiple admin pages use horizontally scrolling summary rails with hidden scrollbars or clipped edges, so important status counts are not visible in the default viewport. Orders only shows `Needs action` and `Overdue` fully while `Due soon` is clipped and `Missing tracking`, returns, refunding, and refunded cards are offscreen; Products hides most listing readiness metrics after `Ready`; Support hides `0 over 30m`, `Score 28`, and `3 closed`; Users landscape clips `Missing phones`; Alerts mobile hides `Resolved` and `Critical/Error`; the mobile dashboard readiness rail similarly cuts off later risk categories.
- Status: FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1730-admin-current-codex/admin-orders-mobile-viewport.png`; `admin-products-mobile-full.png`; `admin-support-mobile-full.png`; `admin-users-landscape-full.png`; `admin-alerts-mobile-full.png`; `admin-mobile-menu-mobile-full.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1730-admin-current-codex/report.json` records `.order-management-page__summaryGrid` with `scrollWidth=1468 clientWidth=360`, `.product-listing-quality__metrics` with `scrollWidth=1066 clientWidth=334`, `.support-management__insightStats` with `scrollWidth=569 clientWidth=334`, `.user-management-page__healthItem.is-risk` at `x=754` in a 740px viewport, and `.alert-management__stats` with `scrollWidth=608 clientWidth=376`.
- Source fix: `OrderManagement.css:1383`, `ProductManagement.css:1788`, `SupportManagement.css:1927`, `UserManagement.css:607`, `AlertManagement.css:525`, and `AdminDashboard.css:1394` now add final F2761 responsive overrides. The affected order summary, product listing-quality metrics, support insight/summary stats, user health metrics, alert stats, and dashboard readiness/action/payment/SLA metrics use visible wrapping grids at `max-width: 900px` / `max-height: 640px`; hidden scrollbar/fade-mask/scroll-snap rail behavior is disabled; fixed rail card bases are reset; and the User health plus Dashboard readiness shells collapse to one content column before tablet or phone-landscape clipping.
- Regression guard: `AdminSummaryRailsResponsive.test.ts` verifies all affected CSS files include the F2761 narrow/short viewport block, visible `repeat(auto-fit, minmax(..., 1fr))` metric grids, no fade-mask/scroll-snap rail behavior in the final override, reset card flex bases, and the User/Dashboard shell collapse contracts.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/AdminSummaryRailsResponsive.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` passed. Adjacent F2760 guard `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/AdminInfrastructureResponsive.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` also passed after the Alert stats cascade change.

### F2762: HIGH — Alert Center table compresses alert titles into vertical text

- Area: Frontend / Admin Alert Center table
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190/admin/alerts`, authenticated `SUPER_ADMIN`, current-source CRA dev server, viewports `390x844`, `740x360`, and `768x1024`
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: Alert titles in the Alert Center table are squeezed into a very narrow first column, so operationally important text is rendered letter-by-letter down the cell. The current alert title `JVM memory usage is high` becomes a 33px-wide vertical block; nearby alert copy such as `IOException detected` also breaks into short fragments. This makes the alert list hard to scan and undermines triage from mobile/tablet admin views.
- Status: FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1730-admin-current-codex/admin-alerts-mobile-full.png`; `admin-alerts-landscape-full.png`; `admin-alerts-tablet-full.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1730-admin-current-codex/report.json` records the `strong` alert title `JVM memory usage is high` at `x=160 y=3991 w=33 h=126` on mobile, `x=267 y=3811 w=33 h=126` on landscape, and `x=289 y=3802 w=33 h=126` on tablet.
- Source fix: `AlertManagement.tsx:293` gives the alert title column a `320px` width and `alert-management__alertColumn` class; `AlertManagement.tsx:639` raises the table scroll contract to `1180` and scopes the table as `alert-management__table`, so fixed severity/status/category/count/date/action columns no longer squeeze the title column. `AlertManagement.css:556` adds final F2762 rules that keep `.alert-management__titleCell` at readable widths on mobile/tablet shells and override global `overflow-wrap: anywhere` with `break-word`/normal word-breaking for primary alert title/message text.
- Regression guard: `AlertManagement.test.ts` verifies the title-column width/class, scoped table class, `scroll={{ x: 1180 }}`, and F2762 CSS contract for readable title widths plus word-based wrapping.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/AlertManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` passed. Adjacent guards `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/AdminInfrastructureResponsive.test.ts src/pages/AdminSummaryRailsResponsive.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` and `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` also passed.

### F2763: MEDIUM — Bug Management mobile filters and row labels distort

- Area: Frontend / Admin Bug Management mobile layout
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190/admin/bugs`, authenticated `SUPER_ADMIN`, current-source CRA dev server, viewport `390x844`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: The Bug Management mobile filter panel renders the status, severity, and module selects as tall blank boxes, with each chevron pushed near the bottom of the control instead of a normal 44px select row. This consumes a large part of the page before the bug list begins. In the mobile card list, generated field labels are also too narrow: repeated `Scan` and `Status` labels are clipped to 24px and 26px, making row metadata harder to understand.
- Status: FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1730-admin-current-codex/admin-bugs-mobile-full.png`; `admin-bugs-create-mobile-full.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1730-admin-current-codex/report.json` records repeated clipped row labels, including `Scan` at `x=208 w=24 scrollWidth=33` and `Status` at `x=266 w=26 scrollWidth=43` across multiple mobile bug cards.
- Source fix: `BugManagement.css:569` adds final F2763 mobile rules that make the toolbar a single-column grid, pin toolbar Select shells to `44px`, align selection internals to `42px`, and keep selection text centered without creating tall blank boxes. The same block changes mobile table-card `td[data-label]` rows to a grid label/value layout with a `minmax(104px, 34%)` label track and stacks labels at `max-width: 380px`, so `Severity`, `Status`, `Scan / update`, and `Actions` remain readable at 390px and 360px widths.
- Regression guard: `BugManagement.test.ts` now verifies the three filter Selects, the mobile `data-label` source contract, compact Select CSS, grid-based label/value layout, static visible pseudo labels, and the very-narrow stacked-label override.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/BugManagement.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` passed.

### F2757: HIGH — Pet Gallery landscape social-proof section collapses into vertical unreadable text

- Area: Frontend / Pet Gallery responsive content cards
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190/pet-gallery`, current-source CRA dev server, viewport `740x360` mobile landscape
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: In mobile landscape, the Pet Gallery social-proof/action area breaks below the first action cards. The `Social proof path`, `Shop what real pet parents make believable`, and supporting paragraph collapse into a 14-18px-wide column, rendering letter-by-letter down the page. This makes an entire conversion section unreadable and creates a very tall blank-looking gap before the gallery grid.
- Status: FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1712-responsive-codex/pet-gallery-landscape-full.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1712-responsive-codex/pet-gallery-landscape-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1712-responsive-codex/report.json` records vertical text at `x=29 y=1220 w=14 h=1854`, including `.pet-gallery-insights__eyebrow` at `w=18 h=151`, the heading at `w=18 h=725`, and the paragraph at `w=18 h=946`.
- Source fix: `PetGallery.css:1736` adds a F2757 short-landscape override for `max-width: 900px` and `max-height: 480px`. It stacks `.pet-gallery-insights`, `.pet-gallery-actions`, and `.pet-gallery-conversion` to a single readable column, gives insights/action/conversion copy blocks a readable `min(100%, 280px)` width, forces headings/body copy to horizontal writing mode with normal word-breaking, removes hidden masks from insight/signal cards, and keeps action buttons in stable two-column grids at `740x360` and `844x390`.
- Regression guard: `PetGallery.test.ts` verifies the insight/action/conversion source sections and the F2757 landscape CSS contract for one-column stacking, readable copy widths, horizontal writing mode, unmasked signal cards, and stable action grids.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/PetGallery.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` passed.

### F2758: HIGH — Pet Finder budget controls are covered by the fixed bottom navigation on small and landscape screens

- Area: Frontend / Pet Finder first-step form
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190/pet-finder`, current-source CRA dev server, viewports `320x568` and `740x360`
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: The Pet Finder first-screen form does not reserve enough clearance for the fixed bottom navigation. At `320x568`, the budget slider handle and budget range text sit underneath the global bottom nav (`Home`, `Products`, `Coupons`, `Cart`, `App`, `Account`), so the customer cannot reliably adjust the budget without hitting navigation. In `740x360` landscape, the same fixed nav consumes the lower part of the first viewport and covers the transition from the intro/search area into the form.
- Status: FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1712-responsive-codex/pet-finder-small-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1712-responsive-codex/pet-finder-small-full.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1712-responsive-codex/pet-finder-landscape-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1712-responsive-codex/pet-finder-landscape-full.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1712-responsive-codex/report.json` records the small-screen `.shop-nav__bottomBar` at `x=8 y=496 w=304 h=62 zIndex=1250`, overlapping the budget slider area inside the first Pet Finder card.
- Source fix: `PetFinder.tsx:231` marks the first form card as `pet-finder-page__finderCard`; `PetFinder.tsx:243` marks the budget column as `pet-finder-page__budgetControl`. `PetFinder.css:1357` adds a final F2758 responsive block for `max-width: 780px` and `max-height: 480px` that reserves page bottom padding/scroll padding for the fixed nav, adds finder-card margin and scroll margin, gives the budget slider/range text extra bottom clearance, and keeps `.shop-nav__bottomBar` bounded to safe-area viewport edges.
- Regression guard: `PetFinder.test.ts` verifies the first-card/budget-control source classes, budget slider accessibility handles, F2758 page/finder-card/budget-control clearance CSS, slider minimum height and margin, and fixed bottom-nav safe-area contract.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/PetFinder.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` and `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed.

### F2759: LOW — Forgot Password reset-guide labels are clipped at 320px

- Area: Frontend / Forgot Password mobile reset form
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190/forgot-password`, current-source CRA dev server, viewport `320x568`
- Severity: LOW
- Description: The password-reset guide chips at the top of the form are too short for their labels on a 320px screen. `Match account email` renders as `Match account...` and `Protect your account` renders as `Protect your...`, even though these labels explain the reset flow immediately above the form.
- Status: FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1712-responsive-codex/forgot-password-small-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1712-responsive-codex/forgot-empty-submit-small-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1712-responsive-codex/report.json` records clipped reset-guide spans at `x=25 y=278 w=81 h=35` and `x=120 y=278 w=81 h=35`, each with `scrollHeight: 53` and `clientHeight: 35`.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/ForgotPassword.tsx` renders `.shopee-login-reset-guide__item` labels for the reset flow; `frontend/src/pages/Login.css` has multiple mobile auth overrides for `.shopee-login-reset-guide__item`, including small-screen padding/min-height rules and later font/touch target guards, but the 320px layout still constrains the label height before two-line copy can fit.
- Source fix: `Login.css:2531` adds a final F2759 reset-card override that lets `.shopee-login-reset-guide__item` grow vertically, removes hidden overflow from reset guide text, clears the old `-webkit-line-clamp`, and switches the guide to a one-column label layout below `380px` while preserving full-width wrapping through `430px`.
- Regression guard: `ForgotPassword.test.ts` verifies the reset guide label source keys and the F2759 CSS contract for unclamped labels and one-column stacking on 320px-class phones.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/ForgotPassword.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` passed.

### F2754: MEDIUM — Product quick preview modal leaves fixed mobile rails visible behind the modal layer

- Area: Frontend / Product list quick preview modal
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190/products?q=feeder&sort=price_desc`, current-source CRA dev server, viewports `390x844` and `360x740`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: Opening `Quick preview` does not fully isolate the product-list page underneath. The fixed global bottom navigation and product-list mobile conversion bar remain mounted and visually leak behind the modal layer; at `390x844` the bottom nav is still visible below the modal card even though the transparent `.ant-modal-wrap` intercepts taps, and at `360x740` the preview action stack sits directly over the same fixed rails. This creates an interactive-looking background behind a blocking modal and makes the modal state feel visually unresolved.
- Status: FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1652-interactions-codex/products-quick-preview-mobile.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1652-interactions-codex/products-quick-preview-compact.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1652-interactions-codex/quick-preview-compact-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1652-interactions-codex/report.json` records `.ant-modal-mask` at `zIndex: 1000`, `.product-list__mobileConversionBar` at `zIndex: 1241`, `.shop-nav__bottomBar` at `zIndex: 1250`, and `.ant-modal-wrap` at `zIndex: 1500`, leaving the app rails above the dimming mask while the modal wrap catches pointer events.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/ProductList.tsx` renders the fixed `.product-list__mobileConversionBar` and the `.product-list__previewModal`; `frontend/src/pages/ProductList.css` and `frontend/src/mobile-app.css` add repeated mobile fixed-position rules for the conversion bar and preview modal; `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css` positions the global `.shop-nav__bottomBar` as a fixed mobile rail.
- Source fix: `ProductList.tsx:1907` adds `product-list--previewOpen` while the preview modal is open, and `ProductList.tsx:2627` scopes the quick-preview modal root as `product-list__previewModalRoot`. `ProductList.css:7037` adds the final F2754 mobile overlay contract: hide the product-list mobile conversion rail while preview is open and raise the preview mask/wrap/modal above storefront/native rails with a stronger mask.
- Regression guard: `ProductList.test.tsx` verifies the preview-open page class, scoped preview modal root, hidden conversion rail, and preview modal z-index contract.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/ProductList.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` passed with existing React/Router warning noise.

### F2755: HIGH — Product filter drawer is covered by the product conversion bar and global bottom navigation

- Area: Frontend / Product list mobile filter drawer
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190/products?q=feeder&sort=price_desc`, current-source CRA dev server, viewports `390x844` and `360x740`
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: Opening the mobile `Filters` drawer does not clear the page-level fixed rails. The product-list conversion bar (`Filters`, `Best deals`, `Add to cart`) and the global bottom navigation stay above the drawer and overlap its footer area; on the compact viewport they sit directly over the drawer's lower content and footer, making `Reset` / `Apply filters` compete with unrelated product and navigation controls.
- Status: FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1652-interactions-codex/filter-drawer-compact-viewport.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1652-interactions-codex/products-filter-drawer-mobile.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1652-interactions-codex/products-filter-drawer-compact.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1652-interactions-codex/report.json` records the compact drawer at `zIndex: 1000`, `.product-list__mobileConversionBar` at `y=596..654` / `zIndex: 1241`, `.shop-nav__bottomBar` at `y=668..730` / `zIndex: 1250`, and `.product-list__drawerFooter` at `y=674..740`, confirming the footer sits in the bottom-nav collision zone.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/ProductList.tsx` renders both `.product-list__mobileConversionBar` and the filter `Drawer` with `.product-list__drawerFooter`; `frontend/src/pages/ProductList.css` contains multiple mobile overrides for `.product-list__mobileConversionBar`, `.product-list__mobileDrawer`, and `.product-list__drawerFooter`; `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css` keeps `.shop-nav__bottomBar` fixed above the app shell.
- Source fix: `ProductList.tsx:1907` adds `product-list--filterDrawerOpen` while the filter drawer is active, and `ProductList.tsx:2526` scopes the Drawer root as `product-list__filterDrawerRoot`. `ProductList.css:7073` adds the final F2755 mobile overlay contract: hide the product-list conversion rail during drawer interaction, raise the drawer root/mask/content above storefront/native rails, and keep the drawer footer at the visible safe-area bottom.
- Regression guard: `ProductList.test.tsx` verifies the filter-drawer-open page class, scoped Drawer root, hidden conversion rail, drawer mask/content z-index contract, and native App z-index override.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/ProductList.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` passed with existing React/Router warning noise; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed.

### F2756: MEDIUM — Empty cart drawer trust strip collapses while the global bottom nav overlays the drawer

The result makes the checkout reassurance area look broken and leaves app navigation visually competing with the cart drawer.

- Source fix: `CartDrawer.tsx:565` scopes the Drawer root as `cart-drawer__root`. `CartDrawer.css:1224` adds the final F2756 compact overlay/trust-row contract: raise the drawer root/mask/content above storefront/native bottom rails and stack the compact trust labels into full-width rows with normal word wrapping instead of the previous narrow rail/fade-mask layout.
- Regression guard: `CartDrawer.test.ts` verifies the scoped Drawer root, overlay z-index contract, native App z-index override, and compact trust-row stacking/wrapping rules.

### F2720: HIGH — Mobile product-detail buy bar covers the price and product summary

- Area: Frontend / Product detail mobile purchase flow
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190/products/1`, current-source CRA dev server, viewports `390x844` and `360x740`
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: The mobile product-detail purchase bar is fixed at the bottom of the viewport, but on the initial product-detail screen it sits directly over the product title, price, discount, delivery promise, favorite/compare controls, and shipping badges. At `360x740` the buy bar expands to three rows and covers even more of the product summary, so the customer cannot read the price context while deciding whether to add to cart or buy now.
- Status: FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1638-current-codex/product-detail-1-mobile.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1638-current-codex/product-detail-1-compact.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1638-current-codex/report.json` records `.product-mobile-buybar` at `y=746..839` in the `390x844` viewport overlapping `.product-price-panel` at `y=655..877`, and at `y=575..734` in the `360x740` viewport overlapping `.product-title-block` at `y=543..608`.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx` renders the fixed `.product-mobile-buybar`; `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.css` positions it fixed at the bottom and changes it to a taller three-row grid under `max-width: 380px`, while the page padding-bottom only protects the end of the document, not the initial viewport content behind the fixed bar.
- Source fix: `ProductDetail.css:5909` adds a final F2720 normal-mobile-web guard after the App WebView fixed-buybar restore. At `max-width: 780px`, `body:not(.shop-mobile-app) .product-detail-page .product-summary-card .product-mobile-buybar` is forced to `position: static !important`, clears inset/z-index/fixed width/transform, stays full width inside the summary card, and keeps the meta/action grid in flow below the price panel. The App WebView fixed buy bar remains separately scoped under `body.shop-mobile-app`.
- Regression guard: `ProductDetail.test.tsx` verifies the buy bar source order after `.product-price-panel`, pins the final F2720 CSS after the App WebView restore, confirms the normal-web selector clears fixed positioning, and ensures the guard does not target `body.shop-mobile-app`.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/ProductDetail.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` passed with existing React/Router/Suspense warning noise; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed.

### F2721: HIGH — Mobile coupon sticky rails overlap each other and the bottom navigation

- Area: Frontend / Coupon center mobile sticky actions
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190/coupons`, current-source CRA dev server, viewports `390x844` and `360x740`
- Severity: HIGH
- Description: The coupon page has multiple sticky mobile elements competing for the bottom of the viewport. The quick navigation rail (`Next best savings action`, `Claim coupons`, `My coupons`) and the mobile action bar (`Log in to keep this coupon`, `Log in`, `Shop now`) overlap each other; on the shorter `360x740` viewport they also overlap the global bottom navigation. This makes the primary coupon action area visually stacked and partially untappable.
- Status: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1638-current-codex/coupons-mobile.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1638-current-codex/coupons-compact.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1638-current-codex/report.json` records `.coupon-center-page__quickNav` at `y=574..719` and `.coupon-center-page__mobileActionBar` at `y=660..760` on `390x844`, and on `360x740` records bottom nav `y=668..730`, quick nav `y=574..719`, and action bar `y=560..660`.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/CouponCenter.css` applies sticky positioning to both `.coupon-center-page__quickNav` and `.coupon-center-page__mobileActionBar`, with the mobile action bar offset from the global bottom nav but without a coordinated stack height for the separate quick nav.
- Source fix: `CouponCenter.css:5181` adds the final F2721 mobile flow guard. At `max-width: 560px`, `.coupon-center-page__quickNav` and `.coupon-center-page__mobileActionBar` are forced to `position: static !important`, their inset/z-index/width/transform competition is cleared, the quick nav becomes an in-flow two-column grid, and the page keeps bottom-nav-aware padding so coupon actions no longer occupy the same sticky layer as the global bottom navigation.
- Regression guard: `CouponCenter.test.ts` verifies the coupon quick nav/mobile action source markup, pins the F2721 CSS after the earlier bottom-aware mobile rule, and fails if later CSS reintroduces sticky/fixed coupon rails or bottom-positioned mobile action bars.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/CouponCenter.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` and `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed.

### F2722: MEDIUM — Short mobile 404 page hides the primary Back to Home CTA under the bottom nav

- Area: Frontend / Not Found mobile layout
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190/definitely-not-a-route`, current-source CRA dev server, viewport `360x740`
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Description: On the short mobile viewport, the 404 page's primary `Back to Home` button lands directly underneath the fixed bottom navigation. The secondary `Search Products` button remains visible below the nav, but the primary recovery path is visually covered and hard to tap.
- Status: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1638-current-codex/not-found-compact.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1638-current-codex/report.json` records `.ant-result-extra` at `y=682..780`, the `Back to Home` button at `y=682..726`, and the fixed `.shop-nav__bottomBar` at `y=668..730`, with 44px of vertical overlap on the primary button.
- Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/NotFound.css` stacks the result buttons on mobile but gives `.not-found-page` only `padding: 48px 12px`; it does not reserve the fixed bottom-nav clearance before the recovery buttons.
- Source fix: `NotFound.css:7` reserves mobile bottom-nav clearance with `min-height: calc(100svh - var(--shop-mobile-bottom-nav-height, 76px))` and bottom padding, stacks `.ant-result-extra` into a one-column grid, and keeps recovery buttons full width with 44px touch targets. `NotFound.css:45` trims top/result/icon/title/subtitle spacing on `max-height: 760px` phones so the primary `Back to Home` CTA stays clear of the fixed bottom navigation.
- Regression guard: `NotFound.test.ts` verifies the 404 source recovery actions, mobile bottom-nav clearance, full-width 44px button grid, and short-viewport spacing contract.
- Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/NotFound.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` and `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed.

### F2723: LOW — Mobile checkout empty-state trust label breaks mid-word at 390px

- Area: Frontend / Checkout empty-state mobile layout
- Environment: `http://127.0.0.1:4190/checkout`, current-source CRA dev server, viewport `390x844`
- Severity: LOW
- Description: The empty checkout hero renders the trust signal `Secure payment` as a broken two-line label with the final character separated (`Secure paymen` / `t`) at 390px. This makes a checkout-critical reassurance chip look unfinished, even though the compact 360px breakpoint switches to a cleaner one-column layout.
- Status: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- Evidence: `app-ui-audit-20260607T1638-current-codex/checkout-mobile.png`; `app-ui-audit-20260607T1638-current-codex/checkout-compact-rerun.png` confirms the spinner in the first compact capture was transient and the compact layout itself renders. Source evidence: `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.css` sets `.checkout-page__emptySignals span { flex: 1 1 142px; padding: 6px 8px; font-size: 11px; }` at `max-width: 640px`, but only changes signals to full-width at `max-width: 360px`, leaving the common 390px viewport in a cramped two-column state.
- Source fix: `Checkout.css:3619` adds the final F2723 common-phone guard. At `max-width: 420px`, `.checkout-page__emptySignals` becomes a one-column grid with a `min(100%, 360px)` width, and each trust chip resets flex sizing, left-aligns content, and uses normal word breaking/white-space so labels have full-row width at `390x844`, `375x812`, and `360x740`.
