### F2100: HIGH — Admin Bootstrap Endpoint Publicly Accessible (Account Creation)
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/SecurityConfig.java:101`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/UserController.java:136-163`
**Description:** The `POST /users/create-admin` endpoint is `permitAll()` in the security config. While it requires an `X-Bootstrap-Token` header validated via `RuntimeConfigService`, this token is fetched from runtime configuration (`admin.bootstrap-token`). If the bootstrap token is weak, guessable, or accidentally set to a known value, any unauthenticated user can create ADMIN accounts. The endpoint is never disabled after the first admin is created.
**Impact:** Any unauthenticated user with the bootstrap token can create admin accounts.
**Fix:** After the first admin is created, automatically disable the bootstrap endpoint. Consider requiring an existing admin session for subsequent admin creation. Log and alert on every successful bootstrap call.
**Severity:** HIGH | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-20

### F2101: MEDIUM — Password Validation Regex Allows Weak Passwords
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/AuthController.java:208-210`
**Description:** The registration password validation uses `@Pattern(regexp = ".*\\p{L}.*")` which matches any Unicode letter including single CJK characters. A password like "aaaaaaaa1" (all same character) passes. There is no requirement for special characters or character diversity. The `UserService.assertStrongPassword` method only checks for letters AND digits.
**Impact:** Users can set weak, easily guessable passwords.
**Fix:** Enforce stronger password policies: require mixed case, special characters, and disallow common passwords or passwords with excessive character repetition.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-20

### F2102: MEDIUM — JWT Secret Validation Allows Short Secrets at Runtime
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/security/JwtService.java:106-114`
**Description:** The `ensureJwtSecretConfigured()` method checks `secret.length() < 32` but the secret is read from `RuntimeConfigService` which can be changed at runtime via Nacos config center. If the config center pushes a short or known secret, all existing JWT tokens become vulnerable. The check only runs during token generation/parsing, not at startup.
**Impact:** Runtime config change could weaken JWT security for all tokens.
**Fix:** Validate the JWT secret at application startup and refuse to start if the secret is below minimum length. Subscribe to config changes and reject short secrets.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-20

### F2103: MEDIUM — CORS Default Allows Private Network Origins
**File:** `src/main/resources/application.properties:36`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/CorsOriginProperties.java:15-18`
**Description:** The default CORS configuration includes `http://10.*:*,http://172.*:*,http://192.168.*:*` which allows any device on the local network to make cross-origin requests to the API. In non-production mode (the default if `APP_RUNTIME_MODE` is unset), this could allow network-adjacent attackers to interact with the API.
**Impact:** Network-adjacent attackers can make cross-origin API requests in non-production mode.
**Fix:** Make the production-safe filter the default and require explicit opt-in for development origins. Document that `APP_RUNTIME_MODE=production` must be set in all non-local deployments.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** SOURCE_FIXED / TARGETED_MAVEN_PASS | **Date:** 2026-06-20
**Fix evidence:** Covered by F9312 on 2026-06-08 07:39 UTC. Base/dev defaults and non-production fallback now exclude private-LAN wildcards, with explicit environment override required for temporary device testing.

### F2104: MEDIUM — Payment Simulation Endpoints Accessible Without Admin Filter Chain
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/PaymentController.java:89-111`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/SecurityConfig.java:93-94`
**Description:** The `POST /payments/{id}/simulate-paid` and `POST /payments/{id}/simulate-callback` endpoints are under `/payments` which is `permitAll()` for POST in SecurityConfig. The controller calls `assertAdminPaymentSimulation()` which checks admin auth, but the security filter chain permits the request before the controller-level check runs. If `payment.simulation-enabled` is true, any admin can mark arbitrary payments as paid.
**Impact:** Simulation endpoints bypass the filter chain security layer.
**Fix:** Move simulation endpoints under `/admin/**` path prefix so they are protected by the `hasRole("ADMIN")` filter chain rule.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-20

### F2105: MEDIUM — JWT Token Stored in localStorage (XSS Vulnerability)
**File:** `frontend/src/api/index.ts:1274-1305`
**Description:** The JWT token and refresh token are stored in `localStorage` via `setStoredItem('token', token)`. localStorage is accessible to any JavaScript running on the page, making it vulnerable to XSS attacks that could steal authentication tokens.
**Impact:** Any XSS vulnerability could lead to token theft.
**Fix:** Consider using httpOnly cookies for token storage, or implement strict Content Security Policy (CSP) headers and ensure all XSS vectors are thoroughly mitigated.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-20

### F2106: MEDIUM — dangerouslySetInnerHTML with Custom Sanitizer (XSS Risk)
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Notifications.tsx:110`, `frontend/src/pages/NotificationManagement.tsx:233`, `frontend/src/utils/sanitizeHtml.ts`
**Description:** Two components render HTML using `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` with a custom `stripUnsafeHtml` sanitizer. The sanitizer uses DOM API to parse and strip dangerous tags/attributes but does NOT use a battle-tested library like DOMPurify. Custom sanitizers are prone to bypass via edge cases in browser HTML parsing. The sanitizer allows `http:` URLs in `href`/`src` attributes which could be used for data exfiltration.
**Impact:** Potential XSS bypass via mutation XSS or encoding tricks.
**Fix:** Replace the custom sanitizer with DOMPurify. Restrict allowed URL schemes to `https:` and relative paths only.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-20

### F2107: MEDIUM — Guest Order Access Weak Authentication
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/OrderController.java:144-149`, `src/main/resources/mapper/OrderMapper.xml:366-381`
**Description:** Guest order access (`GET /orders/guest/{id}`) relies on `guestEmail` and `orderNo` as query parameters without JWT protection. The `findByOrderNoAndEmail` mapper does a fuzzy match on `shipping_address` containing the email, which expands the attack surface.
**Impact:** Attacker who knows or guesses an order number and email can view order details including shipping addresses.
**Fix:** Add rate limiting specifically to guest order lookup endpoints. Consider requiring an email verification code for guest order access.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-20

### F2108: MEDIUM — Unbounded SELECT Queries Without LIMIT
**File:** `src/main/resources/mapper/ProductMapper.xml:33-40`, `src/main/resources/mapper/OrderMapper.xml:75-77`, `src/main/resources/mapper/NotificationMapper.xml:16`
**Description:** Several mapper queries use `SELECT * FROM table` without any `LIMIT` clause. The `ProductMapper.findAll`, `OrderMapper.findAll`, and `NotificationMapper` queries could return unbounded result sets if called without care.
**Impact:** Potential OOM or excessive memory usage with large datasets.
**Fix:** Add `LIMIT` clauses to all `findAll` queries, or replace them with paginated alternatives as a safety net.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-20
**Partial fix evidence:** 2026-06-08 09:00 UTC: ProductMapper portion is fixed by removing the unused `ProductMapper.findAll()` API and matching XML statement, with `ProductMapperContractTest` coverage. This F2108 entry remains OPEN for the other mapper statements named in the issue.

### F2109: LOW — Token Blacklist Not Checked on Refresh
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/security/JwtAuthenticationFilter.java:47-51`
**Description:** The blacklist check only runs on the access token's JTI. The refresh token endpoint (`/auth/refresh`) does not validate whether the refresh token itself has been revoked.
**Impact:** Revoked refresh tokens could still be used to obtain new access tokens.
**Fix:** Ensure the refresh token endpoint validates the refresh token against the blacklist before issuing a new access token.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-20

### F2110: LOW — Frontend User Type Includes password Field
**File:** `frontend/src/types.ts:9`
**Description:** The `User` interface includes `password?: string`. While this is likely never populated in API responses, having the field in the TypeScript type means any accidental API response including a password field would be silently accepted.
**Impact:** Accidental password exposure would not trigger type errors.
**Fix:** Remove `password` from the frontend `User` type. Use a separate `RegisterRequest` or `LoginRequest` type for password fields.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-20

### F2111: LOW — Missing AbortController Cleanup in CustomerSupportWidget
**File:** `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.tsx:313-318`
**Description:** The `supportApi.getUnreadCount()` call has no abort signal and no cleanup. If the component unmounts before the promise resolves, the `.then()` callback will attempt to call `setUnread` on an unmounted component.
**Impact:** Wasted state update after component unmount.
**Fix:** Use an `AbortController` or a `disposed` flag pattern for this effect.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-20

### F2112: LOW — Stale Closure Risk in SearchBar useEffect
**File:** `frontend/src/components/SearchBar.tsx:17-22`
**Description:** The `useEffect` captures `onSearch` and `value` in its closure and runs a debounced timeout. If `onSearch` changes identity between renders, the stale closure will call the old `onSearch`. The cleanup correctly clears the timeout, so this is mitigated.
**Impact:** Search could call stale callback if parent doesn't memoize `onSearch`.
**Fix:** Use `useCallback` for `onSearch` in the parent, or use a ref to store the latest callback.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-20

### F2113: LOW — No Development-Mode Warning for Missing i18n Keys
**File:** `frontend/src/i18n.tsx`
**Description:** If a new key is added to the code but not yet added to the locale files, there is no visible fallback mechanism. The `t()` function returns the key string itself without warning.
**Impact:** Missing translations silently fall back to raw key strings.
**Fix:** Add a development-mode warning when a translation key is not found.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-20

### F2114: HIGH — SecurityAuditLogManagement.tsx Inline Translations Bypass Global i18n
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/admin/SecurityAuditLogManagement.tsx`
**Description:** The component contains ~250 lines of inline translated strings (both Chinese and English) that duplicate global i18n keys (`securityAuditLog.title`, `securityAuditLog.filter.action`, `securityAuditLog.table.timestamp`, etc.) instead of using the `useTranslation()` hook's `t()` function. This bypasses the global i18n system and creates a maintenance burden.
**Impact:** If the admin panel language selection changes, this component won't reflect those changes. Duplicate keys may diverge over time.
**Fix:** Remove the inline `zh/en` objects and `useState`-based translation system. Use `useTranslation()` with the global locale keys that already exist in `zh.json` and `en.json`.
**Severity:** HIGH | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2115: MEDIUM — CartController /cart/add Quantity Has No Bounds Checking
**File:** `backend/src/main/java/com/shop/controller/CartController.java:38`
**Description:** `POST /cart/add` accepts any integer quantity from 0 to `Integer.MAX_VALUE`. Unlike `POST /cart/add-one` (validated with `@Min(1)` and stock checking), the `/cart/add` endpoint doesn't validate quantity bounds.
**Impact:** A client can add 0 items to cart (effectively a no-op) or an absurdly large quantity that could cause issues in downstream calculations.
**Fix:** Add `@Min(1) @Max(99999)` or similar bounds to the quantity parameter.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2116: MEDIUM — ProductDetail.tsx Uses Raw Chinese Strings for Animal Keyword Matching
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx:181-197`
**Description:** The `isAnimalSupplement` function checks if the product name contains keywords like `'宠物'`, `'猫'`, `'狗'`, `'犬'`, `'鱼'` etc. These are hardcoded Chinese strings. When the product name is in English, the keyword check will fail.
**Impact:** The pet-friendly safety info box will not appear for English product names.
**Fix:** Use the i18n system to provide both Chinese and English keywords, or use product category instead of name string matching.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2117: LOW — Admin PUT /admin/users/{id} Enables Mass-Assignment
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/AdminController.java`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/dto/AdminUserUpdateRequest.java`
**Description:** `PUT /admin/users/{id}` directly applies request body fields via `BeanUtils.copyProperties()` from admin user update request to the User entity. An admin could potentially set arbitrary fields not intended for modification.
**Impact:** Potential mass-assignment of fields not intended for admin modification.
**Fix:** Use a dedicated DTO with only the allowed fields for admin user updates.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** FIXED / DUPLICATE_COVERED_BY_F2812 | **Date:** 2026-06-23
**Fix evidence:** 2026-06-09 04:57 UTC: current source no longer binds the admin user update route to a broad user request/entity. `AdminUserUpdateRequest` exposes only status/address and rejects unsupported fields; see F2812 for verification.

### F2118: LOW — Duplicate Console.warn for Logout Token Revoke in Navbar + useAuth
**File:** `frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx:69`, `frontend/src/hooks/useAuth.tsx:112`
**Description:** Both `Navbar.tsx` and `useAuth.tsx` hook have identical `catch` blocks that log a warning when the token revoke API fails during logout. Since the Navbar's logout handler calls `logout()` from `useAuth`, the warning is logged twice.
**Impact:** Minor — double console.warn on logout token revoke failure.
**Fix:** Remove the try/catch from the Navbar's token revoke call since `useAuth.logout()` already handles it.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2119: HIGH — Cart.tsx fetchCartItems Has No Race Guard on Async Response
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Cart.tsx:157-185`
**Description:** `fetchCartItems` makes an API call but has no mechanism to abort a stale in-flight request if called again (e.g. when `language` changes). If language changes rapidly, the second fetch may resolve before the first, and the stale response overwrites cart items.
**Impact:** Stale cart data displayed after language switch; quantity/price may revert.
**Fix:** Use an `AbortController` per call and pass its signal to the API, or add a request version counter (similar to `quantityRequestVersionRef`).
**Severity:** HIGH | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2120: HIGH — Cart.tsx selectedTotal Floating-Point Precision Risk in Shipping Threshold
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Cart.tsx:565-567`
**Description:** `selectedTotal` is a raw floating-point sum. The `freeShippingThreshold` comparison uses this imprecise value. If the threshold is 100 and the total is 99.99999999999999, the remaining shows 0.00000000000001 instead of 0, and the progress bar shows 99% instead of 100%.
**Impact:** Free shipping progress bar shows incorrect percentage at the threshold boundary.
**Fix:** Round `selectedTotal` to 2 decimal places before using it in threshold comparisons.
**Severity:** HIGH | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2121: HIGH — Checkout.tsx addSuggestedProduct Has Race Condition and No Error Handling
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:1285-1306`
**Description:** `addSuggestedProduct` has no concurrency guard (no `submittingRef`-style check). Rapid clicks fire multiple concurrent `cartApi.addItem` calls. Additionally, non-auth errors are re-thrown without showing a user-facing message, causing unhandled promise rejections.
**Impact:** Duplicate items added to cart; no error feedback to user on failure.
**Fix:** Add a `useRef<boolean>` guard and wrap errors with `message.error` instead of re-throwing.
**Severity:** HIGH | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2122: HIGH — Checkout.tsx retryCreatePayment Has No Idempotency Key
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:1430-1449`
**Description:** After `orderApi.checkout` succeeds, the idempotency key is cleared (line 1367). If `paymentApi.create` fails and the user retries, `retryCreatePayment` does NOT use an idempotency key. Rapid retries could create multiple payment records for the same order.
**Impact:** Potential duplicate payment records for a single order.
**Fix:** Generate and attach an idempotency key to `retryCreatePayment` calls.
**Severity:** HIGH | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2123: HIGH — Checkout.tsx Cart Items Cleared Before Payment Is Confirmed
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:1367-1375`
**Description:** After `orderApi.checkout` succeeds but before `paymentApi.create` is called, cart items are cleared from storage. If the component crashes between order creation and payment creation, the cart items are lost.
**Impact:** Users lose their cart contents if payment creation fails after order creation.
**Fix:** Defer cart cleanup until after payment creation succeeds, or persist cart snapshot to sessionStorage.
**Severity:** HIGH | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2124: HIGH — Backend Payment Create and Sync Endpoints Are permitAll
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/SecurityConfig.java:93-98`
**Description:** `POST /payments`, `POST /payment`, `POST /payment/*/sync`, and `POST /payments/*/sync` are all `permitAll`. While the controller verifies order ownership, the endpoints are accessible to unauthenticated users who can guess order IDs. Sync endpoints trigger payment provider API calls before authorization is verified.
**Impact:** Unauthenticated users can trigger payment operations and enumerate order existence.
**Fix:** Require authentication for payment endpoints, or ensure authorization happens before any side effects.
**Severity:** HIGH | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2125: HIGH — Backend OrderController.getMyOrders Returns All Orders Without Pagination
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/OrderController.java:117-119`
**Description:** `getMyOrders` calls `orderService.getOrdersByUserId(userId)` which returns ALL orders for the user with no pagination. For users with many orders, this returns an unbounded result set.
**Impact:** Memory exhaustion and slow responses for users with large order histories.
**Fix:** Add pagination parameters (`page`, `size`) to the endpoint and service method.
**Severity:** HIGH | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2126: HIGH — Backend CartService.calculateTotal Uses double for Monetary Calculations
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/CartService.java:146-151`
**Description:** `calculateTotal` uses `item.getPrice().doubleValue()` with `mapToDouble`, producing a `double` sum. Floating-point arithmetic is inherently imprecise for monetary values (e.g. `0.1 + 0.2 != 0.3`). The `OrderService` correctly uses `BigDecimal` throughout.
**Impact:** Incorrect cart total calculations due to floating-point precision errors.
**Fix:** Change to `BigDecimal` arithmetic: `item.getPrice().multiply(BigDecimal.valueOf(item.getQuantity()))`.
**Severity:** HIGH | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2127: HIGH — Backend Stock Restoration Does Not Use Pessimistic Locking
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/OrderService.java:1487-1500`
**Description:** `restoreStock` reads the product without `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` and increments stock. If multiple cancellations happen concurrently for the same product, stock values could be incorrect. Compare with `prepareCheckoutItems` which correctly uses `findAllByIdForUpdate`.
**Impact:** Stock inconsistency under concurrent order cancellations.
**Fix:** Use `findByIdForUpdate` in `restoreStock` to prevent concurrent stock modifications.
**Severity:** HIGH | **Status:** FIXED | **Date:** 2026-06-23
**Fixed:** 2026-06-08 07:15 UTC
**Fix evidence:** `OrderService.restoreStock(...)` now calls `productRepository.findByIdForUpdate(item.getProductId())` before updating product/variant stock and logs a warning if the product cannot be found. `OrderStockReservationServiceTest.restoresVariantOnlyStockWithoutCreatingProductStock` verifies the lock lookup is used; `restoreStockLogsMissingProductInsteadOfSilentlySkipping` verifies missing products are observable. Targeted Maven: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=OrderStockReservationServiceTest test` ✅; combined slice `./mvnw -q -Dtest=PaymentFlowServiceTest,OrderInputNormalizationServiceTest,OrderStockReservationServiceTest test` ✅.

### F2128: HIGH — Frontend JWT and Refresh Token Stored in localStorage (XSS Token Theft)
**File:** `frontend/src/api/index.ts:1291-1292`
**Description:** Both JWT access token and refresh token are stored in localStorage, which is accessible to any JavaScript on the page including XSS payloads. This is a well-known security risk.
**Impact:** XSS attack can steal authentication tokens and impersonate users.
**Fix:** Move refresh token to HttpOnly/Secure/SameSite cookie; keep access token in memory only.
**Severity:** HIGH | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2129: HIGH — Frontend No Client-Side Auth Guards on Protected Routes
**File:** `frontend/src/App.tsx:834-840`
**Description:** Routes like `/profile`, `/wishlist`, `/history`, `/stock-alerts`, `/notifications`, and `/checkout` are rendered without any auth guard. Any unauthenticated user can navigate directly to these pages.
**Impact:** Unauthenticated users can access protected storefront pages.
**Fix:** Add a `RequireAuth` wrapper component that checks for a valid token and redirects to `/login`.
**Severity:** HIGH | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2130: MEDIUM — Cart.tsx Debounced Timer + fetchCartItems Re-entry Can Flicker Quantity
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Cart.tsx:280-318`
**Description:** If the component re-renders and `fetchCartItems` is called while a debounced quantity update is still pending, the fetched data may overwrite the optimistic local quantity without canceling the pending timer, causing a brief UI flicker.
**Impact:** Quantity display briefly shows wrong value during language switch.
**Fix:** Cancel all pending quantity timers when `fetchCartItems` is about to execute.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2131: MEDIUM — Cart.tsx flushPendingQuantityUpdates Timer/Version Race
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Cart.tsx:587-631`
**Description:** Timer clearing and version bumping happen in separate loops. Between `clearQuantityTimer` and the version bump, a pending timer callback could fire with stale data.
**Impact:** Potential stale quantity update applied after flush.
**Fix:** Move the version bump into the same loop as the timer clearing.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2132: MEDIUM — Cart.tsx moveSavedItemToCart Uses Generic Error Message
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Cart.tsx:437-469`
**Description:** The catch block uses `t('messages.operationFailed')` without consulting the actual error. A 401, network error, or server 500 all show the same unhelpful message. Compare with `saveForLater` which uses `getApiErrorMessage`.
**Impact:** Users cannot diagnose why moving an item back to cart failed.
**Fix:** Use `getApiErrorMessage(err, t('messages.operationFailed'), language)`.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2133: MEDIUM — Cart.tsx moveSavedItemsToCart Does Not Revert State on Failure
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Cart.tsx:471-523`
**Description:** Unlike `saveForLater` which reverts state on failure, the bulk `moveSavedItemsToCart` catches errors but does not restore saved-items state. If `cartApi.getItems` fails after some items have already been removed from saved-for-later, those items are lost.
**Impact:** Saved-for-later items can be lost on partial failure.
**Fix:** Save a snapshot of saved items before the operation and restore it in the catch block.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2134: MEDIUM — Cart.tsx updateQuantity Stale Closure Risk for fetchCartItems
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Cart.tsx:280-318`
**Description:** `updateQuantity` is not wrapped in `useCallback`. The debounced `setTimeout` closure captures `fetchCartItems` from the render when it was called. If `language` changes between click and debounce firing, the stale `fetchCartItems` fetches with the old language.
**Impact:** Quantity update may trigger cart refresh with wrong language setting.
**Fix:** Use a ref for `fetchCartItems` or wrap `updateQuantity` in `useCallback`.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2135: MEDIUM — Checkout.tsx retryCreatePayment and simulatePayment Lack Double-Submit Guard
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:1430-1449, 1467-1484`
**Description:** Both functions rely solely on React state (`paying`, `simulatingPayment`) to disable buttons. Between click and state update, a rapid double-click can dispatch two API calls. `handleSubmit` correctly uses a synchronous `submittingRef.current` guard but these do not.
**Impact:** Duplicate payment creation or simulation calls.
**Fix:** Add synchronous ref guards identical to the `submittingRef` pattern.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2136: MEDIUM — Checkout.tsx Payment Channel Load Failure Is Silent
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:609-614`
**Description:** When `paymentApi.getChannels()` fails, the catch block sets channels to empty but shows no error message. The submit button is permanently disabled with no retry mechanism.
**Impact:** User stuck with no way to proceed or retry loading payment channels.
**Fix:** Show `message.warning` and/or provide a retry button.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2137: MEDIUM — Checkout.tsx addSuggestedProduct Has No Loading State
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:1285-1306`
**Description:** No spinner, disabled state, or visual feedback while the add-to-cart operation is in progress. The user can click multiple times.
**Impact:** No feedback during add operation; enables duplicate clicks.
**Fix:** Add a local loading state per product and show a spinner/disable the button.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2138: MEDIUM — Checkout.tsx No Postal Code Format Validation
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:2326`
**Description:** The postal code field only has a `required` rule. No format validation (US 5-digit, UK alphanumeric, CN 6-digit). Invalid postal codes are accepted and sent to the backend.
**Impact:** Invalid addresses submitted; potential shipping failures.
**Fix:** Add a regex validator or custom validator that checks format based on region.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** SOURCE_FIXED / TARGETED_JEST_AND_TSC_PASS — Covered by F2675. New-address checkout postal validation is now region-aware for CN/MX/US/GB plus fallback regions, localized validation copy was added, and focused checkout regressions cover invalid and valid country-specific formats. | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2139: MEDIUM — Checkout.tsx No Minimum Length on Address or Recipient Name
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:2293, 2323`
**Description:** Both fields only check `required`. A single character passes validation, resulting in incomplete addresses being submitted.
**Impact:** Incomplete shipping addresses accepted.
**Fix:** Add minimum length rules (e.g. 2 characters for name, 5 for address).
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2140: MEDIUM — Checkout.tsx Coupon Quote Failure Permanently Disables Submit
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:1225-1231`
**Description:** If the coupon quote fails (`couponQuoteStatus === 'error'`), `shippingQuoteReady` stays false, permanently disabling submission. The user has no way to proceed without a page refresh.
**Impact:** User stuck after coupon API failure with no recovery path.
**Fix:** Allow submission when coupon quote fails, falling back to no-coupon pricing.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2141: MEDIUM — Checkout.tsx Saved Addresses Skip Region and Postal Code Validation
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:1014-1021`
**Description:** `selectedAddressReady` for saved addresses checks name, phone, and address but does NOT verify that `region` or `postalCode` are present. Old addresses created before these were required would pass validation.
**Impact:** Backend shipping calculation may fail for incomplete saved addresses.
**Fix:** Validate all required fields for saved addresses.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN — Not closed by F2675. F2675 validates the new-address form path, but current saved address data still arrives as a combined `address` string without independent saved-address `region` and `postalCode` fields to validate reliably. | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2142: MEDIUM — Checkout.tsx Payment Polling Has No Visual Indicator
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:1575-1658`
**Description:** The 30-minute polling loop runs silently with no spinner, progress bar, or text indicating that the system is actively checking payment status.
**Impact:** User may think the page is frozen during payment confirmation.
**Fix:** Show a subtle polling indicator while the interval is active.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2143: MEDIUM — Backend CartController Missing @Min/@Max on Quantity Parameters
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/CartController.java:35-38, 78-79`
**Description:** `addToCart` and `updateQuantity` accept `@RequestParam Integer quantity` without `@Min`/`@Max` constraints. The service layer rejects invalid values, but Spring won't produce clean 400 validation errors.
**Impact:** Non-ideal error responses for invalid quantity values.
**Fix:** Add `@Min(1) @Max(999)` annotations or use `@Validated` on the controller.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2144: MEDIUM — Backend CartAddRequest DTO Has No Validation Annotations
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/dto/CartAddRequest.java:6-11`
**Description:** `CartAddRequest` has no `@NotNull`, `@Min`, `@Max`, or `@Size` annotations. A null `productId` or `quantity` causes a NullPointerException instead of a clean 400 response.
**Impact:** Raw 500 error instead of 400 for invalid input.
**Fix:** Add `@NotNull` on `productId` and `quantity`, `@Min(1) @Max(999)` on `quantity`.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2145: MEDIUM — Backend ProductController No Upper Bound on Pagination Size
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/ProductController.java:61-62`
**Description:** The `size` query parameter is passed directly without capping. A user can pass `?size=100000` causing a massive database query.
**Impact:** Potential memory exhaustion and slow responses.
**Fix:** Cap the `size` parameter, e.g. `Math.min(size, 100)`.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2146: MEDIUM — Backend Cart Operations Have No Endpoint-Specific Rate Limits
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/RateLimitService.java:289-333`
**Description:** Cart mutation endpoints (`POST /cart/add`, `POST /cart`, `PUT /cart/update`, `DELETE /cart/*`) have no specific rate limits beyond the global limit (300/min for authenticated users).
**Impact:** Authenticated users can add items to cart 300 times per minute.
**Fix:** Add endpoint-specific rate limits for cart mutation operations.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2147: MEDIUM — Backend Development CORS Allows Broad Private Network Origins
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/CorsOriginProperties.java:15-17`
**Description:** In non-production mode, default allowed origins include `http://10.*:*`, `http://172.*:*`, `http://192.168.*:*` with wildcard ports. Any device on the LAN can make credentialed cross-origin requests.
**Impact:** CORS bypass if app accidentally runs in non-production mode on a network.
**Fix:** Restrict development origins to specific ports (e.g. `localhost:3000`).
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** SOURCE_FIXED / TARGETED_MAVEN_PASS | **Date:** 2026-06-23
**Fix evidence:** Covered by F9312 on 2026-06-08 07:39 UTC. The non-production fallback now contains only loopback wildcard origins, and the dev profile no longer includes private subnet wildcards.

### F2148: MEDIUM — Backend Guest Checkout Creates Users with Potentially Plaintext Passwords
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/OrderService.java:536-557`
**Description:** `getOrCreateGuestUser` creates a user with `user.setPassword(UUID.randomUUID().toString())`. It's unclear whether this password is hashed before saving. `UserService.registerAdmin` hashes passwords, but this direct `userRepository.save` path may not.
**Impact:** Guest user passwords may be stored in plaintext.
**Fix:** Ensure the password is hashed before saving, or use a dedicated service method.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2149: MEDIUM — Backend Inconsistent Error Response Formats Across Controllers
**File:** Multiple controllers
**Description:** `CartController` returns `ResponseEntity.badRequest().body(Map.of(...))` while others throw `ResponseStatusException`. The raw `Map` responses bypass the `GlobalApiExceptionHandler`.
**Impact:** Inconsistent API error shape confuses frontend error handling.
**Fix:** Standardize on throwing `ResponseStatusException` or use a consistent error DTO.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2150: MEDIUM — Backend CartController Has Duplicate Add-to-Cart Endpoints
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/CartController.java:34-75`
**Description:** Three add-to-cart endpoints exist: `POST /cart/add` (RequestParam), `POST /cart` (RequestBody), `POST /cart/me/add` (RequestParam). Different input formats but similar behavior increases maintenance burden.
**Impact:** API confusion; potential for inconsistent behavior between endpoints.
**Fix:** Consolidate to a single `POST /cart` endpoint with `@RequestBody CartAddRequest`.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2151: MEDIUM — Backend Account Enumeration via Registration Error Messages
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/AuthController.java:88-93`
**Description:** Registration returns distinct messages for "Email already registered", "Username already registered", and "Phone already registered". An attacker can enumerate which accounts exist.
**Impact:** Account enumeration aiding targeted attacks.
**Fix:** Return a generic "Registration failed" message for duplicate field errors.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2152: MEDIUM — Backend Token Blacklist Fails Open When Redis Is Down
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/TokenBlacklistService.java:117-128`
**Description:** If Redis is unavailable and the token is not in the local in-memory blacklist, `isAccessTokenBlacklisted()` returns `false`. Recently blacklisted (logged out) tokens on other instances may still be accepted.
**Impact:** Logged-out tokens remain valid during Redis outages.
**Fix:** Consider returning `true` (fail-closed) when Redis is unavailable.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2153: MEDIUM — Backend CheckoutRequest Has Weak Validation
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/dto/CheckoutRequest.java:9-26`
**Description:** `shippingAddress` has no `@Size(max)` constraint. `recipientName`, `recipientPhone`, `contactEmail` have no validation annotations. `paymentMethod` has no size limit.
**Impact:** Oversized or malformed input accepted without early rejection.
**Fix:** Add `@Size` constraints and `@Email` on `contactEmail`.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2154: MEDIUM — Frontend Role Stored in localStorage Can Be Tampered With
**File:** `frontend/src/hooks/useAuth.ts:33, 66`
**Description:** The user's role is stored in localStorage. The `isAdminRole()` check in the Navbar reads this directly. While AdminLayout re-verifies via API, the Navbar admin link visibility is based on the localStorage value.
**Impact:** Attacker can modify localStorage to see admin link (access is still server-verified).
**Fix:** Navbar admin link should be hidden by default and only shown after server-verified check.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2155: MEDIUM — Frontend Proactive Token Refresh Race Condition
**File:** `frontend/src/api/index.ts:1307-1319`
**Description:** If multiple requests are in-flight and the refresh fails, all queued requests get `null` and proceed without a token, then get 401s. The refresh failure should immediately reject queued requests.
**Impact:** Multiple requests fail with 401 instead of a single clean re-auth flow.
**Fix:** If `refreshAuthToken()` returns null, immediately reject queued requests.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2156: MEDIUM — Frontend isAdminRole() Accepts Any Non-USER Role
**File:** `frontend/src/utils/roles.ts:12-13`
**Description:** `isAdminRole()` returns true for any role that is not literally "USER". Arbitrary strings like "MODERATOR", "GUEST", "VIEWER" would be treated as admin roles.
**Impact:** Overly permissive role check could grant admin UI access to non-admin roles.
**Fix:** Use an explicit allowlist: `ADMIN_ROLES.includes(normalizeRole(role))`.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2157: MEDIUM — Frontend Custom HTML Sanitizer Bypass Risk
**File:** `frontend/src/utils/sanitizeHtml.ts:1-58`
**Description:** Custom DOM-based sanitizer using `innerHTML` parsing. Edge cases may exist with nested encoding, null bytes, or mutation XSS. Missing blocked tags include `<details>`, `<audio>`, `<video>`, `<noscript>`.
**Impact:** Potential XSS bypass in notification messages rendered with `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`.
**Fix:** Use a battle-tested library like DOMPurify instead of a custom sanitizer.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2158: MEDIUM — Frontend No CSP Headers Configured
**File:** `frontend/public/index.html`
**Description:** No Content-Security-Policy headers are set. The `runtime-config.js` is loaded via a `<script>` tag which would benefit from CSP nonce/hash protection.
**Impact:** No defense-in-depth against injected scripts.
**Fix:** Configure CSP headers at the server/CDN level.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2159: MEDIUM — Frontend AuthProvider Defined but Never Mounted
**File:** `frontend/src/hooks/useAuth.ts:18-84`
**Description:** `AuthProvider` is exported but never used in `index.tsx` or `App.tsx`. The app relies on raw localStorage reads scattered throughout components.
**Impact:** Dead code; inconsistent auth state management.
**Fix:** Either mount `AuthProvider` in the tree and use `useAuth()` throughout, or remove the dead code.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2160: MEDIUM — Frontend PWA Manifest Without Service Worker
**File:** `frontend/public/manifest.json`
**Description:** `manifest.json` is present but no service worker is registered anywhere. The PWA install prompt may appear but the app won't work offline or cache assets.
**Impact:** Misleading install prompt; no offline support.
**Fix:** Implement a service worker or remove the manifest.
**Severity:** MEDIUM | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2161: LOW — Cart.tsx Guest Cart Quantity Update Has No Debounce
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Cart.tsx:284-286`
**Description:** When not authenticated, `updateQuantity` immediately writes to localStorage with no debounce. Rapid changes trigger multiple writes and DOM events.
**Impact:** Minor performance concern for guest users with rapid quantity changes.
**Fix:** Apply the same 350ms debounce used for authenticated users.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2162: LOW — Cart.tsx Skeleton Omits Saved-for-Later Section
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Cart.tsx:951-976`
**Description:** Loading skeleton shows hero, stats, and product cards but no skeleton for the saved-for-later section.
**Impact:** Visual gap during loading could be confusing.
**Fix:** Add saved-for-later skeleton to the loading state.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2163: LOW — Cart.tsx cartNextAction IIFE Recomputed Every Render
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Cart.tsx:685-740`
**Description:** `cartNextAction` is computed via an IIFE on every render. For large carts this is wasted computation.
**Impact:** Minor performance overhead on each render.
**Fix:** Wrap in `useMemo`.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2164: LOW — Cart.tsx Saved-Items Grid Missing role="list" / role="listitem"
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Cart.tsx:1473-1521`
**Description:** The mobile saved items grid uses `<div>` elements without `role` attributes, unlike the cart items section which correctly uses `role="list"` and `role="listitem"`.
**Impact:** Screen readers may not properly announce the saved items list structure.
**Fix:** Add `role="list"` to the container and `role="listitem"` to each item div.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2165: LOW — Cart.tsx Desktop Table Rendered and Hidden on Mobile
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Cart.tsx:1304`
**Description:** Both desktop `<Table>` and mobile card list are always in the DOM; CSS media queries hide one. On mobile, the Table is hidden but still rendered and its data processed.
**Impact:** Wasted rendering work on mobile for large carts.
**Fix:** Conditionally render based on a media query hook.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2166: LOW — Checkout.tsx buildAddress Truncates Silently at 500 Characters
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:1269`
**Description:** The concatenated address string is truncated to 500 characters. If region + postal code + detail exceeds this, the address is silently shortened.
**Impact:** Critical address information may be lost without warning.
**Fix:** Validate total length before submission and warn the user.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2167: LOW — Checkout.tsx Client-Side Coupon Discount Estimate May Diverge from Backend
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:111-125, 2347-2355`
**Description:** `estimateCouponDiscount` calculates discounts client-side. If rounding logic differs from the backend, displayed savings could be misleading.
**Impact:** Minor discrepancy between displayed and actual discount amounts.
**Fix:** Use backend-quoted discount amounts when available.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2168: LOW — Checkout.tsx Auth Expiry During Submit Does Not Clear Created Order
**File:** `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:1406-1418`
**Description:** If auth expires during the submit flow, the catch block resets addresses, coupons, and cart items but does not reset `createdOrder` or `payment`. This leaves the component in an inconsistent state.
**Impact:** Component state suggests clean slate but order exists server-side.
**Fix:** Also call `setCreatedOrder(null)` and `setPayment(null)` in the auth expiry handler.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2169: LOW — Backend LIKE Wildcard Characters Not Escaped in User Search
**File:** `src/main/resources/mapper/UserMapper.xml:147-149`
**Description:** Admin user search does not escape `%` and `_` LIKE wildcard characters. Users searching for these characters get unexpected results. Compare with `PetGalleryService.normalizeAdminKeyword()` which strips them.
**Impact:** Unexpected search behavior for special characters.
**Fix:** Apply the same `%` and `_` escaping in UserService search normalization.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2170: LOW — Backend Rate Limit Skip Prefixes Include /uploads/
**File:** `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/RateLimitService.java:162`
**Description:** The skip prefixes include `/uploads/` which exempts all uploaded file serving from rate limiting. An attacker could enumerate and download all uploaded files without restriction.
**Impact:** Unrestricted file enumeration/download.
**Fix:** Apply a separate, more generous rate limit to `/uploads/` paths.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

### F2171: LOW — Frontend Top-Level Error Boundary Lacks "Clear Data" Option
**File:** `frontend/src/App.tsx:824`
**Description:** If the top-level ErrorBoundary catches an error, the user sees a generic error but has no mechanism to reset state or clear corrupted localStorage that might be causing the error.
**Impact:** Users stuck in error loop if localStorage is corrupted.
**Fix:** Add a "Clear data and retry" option that clears localStorage auth tokens and reloads.
**Severity:** LOW | **Status:** OPEN | **Date:** 2026-06-23

## Regression Summary

- 2026-06-23 21:30 UTC: **Deep Review #100 (Cart, Checkout, Backend Multi-Axis Audit)**: **53 new issues found (F2119–F2171).** Breakdown: 11 HIGH, 25 MEDIUM, 17 LOW. Scope: Cart.tsx, Checkout.tsx, CartController, CartService, OrderService, ProductController, SecurityConfig, AuthController, TokenBlacklistService, mapper XML files, sanitizeHtml.ts, useAuth.ts, roles.ts, App.tsx. Key findings: (1) **HIGH: Cart.tsx fetchCartItems has no race guard** — stale responses overwrite data on language switch (F2119). (2) **HIGH: Cart.tsx floating-point precision risk** in shipping threshold comparison (F2120). (3) **HIGH: Checkout.tsx addSuggestedProduct no double-submit guard** (F2121). (4) **HIGH: Checkout.tsx retryCreatePayment no idempotency key** (F2122). (5) **HIGH: Checkout.tsx cart cleared before payment confirmed** (F2123). (6) **HIGH: Backend payment endpoints permitAll** — unauthenticated access (F2124). (7) **HIGH: Backend getMyOrders returns all orders without pagination** (F2125). (8) **HIGH: Backend CartService.calculateTotal uses double** for monetary values (F2126). (9) **HIGH: Backend stock restoration lacks pessimistic locking** (F2127). (10) **HIGH: JWT + refresh token in localStorage** (F2128). (11) **HIGH: No client-side auth guards** on protected routes (F2129). Current totals: 2171 issues, 2009 FIXED, 15 WONTFIX, 147 OPEN.

(2) **MEDIUM: /cart/add has no quantity bounds checking** unlike /cart/add-one (F2115). (3) **MEDIUM: ProductDetail.tsx isAnimalSupplement uses hardcoded Chinese** keywords (F2116). (4) **LOW: Admin PUT /admin/users/{id} mass-assignment via BeanUtils.copyProperties** (F2117). (5) **LOW: Duplicate console.warn for logout token revoke** (F2118). **Positive findings**: Backend security audit across JwtService, SecurityConfig, AuthController, PaymentController, OrderController, MediaStorageServiceImpl, and mapper XML files — SQL injection CLEAN (MyBatis `#{}`), path traversal CLEAN (filename sanitization), rate limiting COMPREHENSIVE. Frontend audit across api/index.ts, types.ts, sanitizeHtml.ts, Notifications.tsx, CustomerSupportWidget.tsx, SearchBar.tsx, and i18n.tsx. Key findings: (1) **HIGH: Admin bootstrap endpoint publicly accessible** (F2100) — `POST /users/create-admin` is `permitAll()` and never auto-disables. (2) **MEDIUM: Password validation allows weak passwords** (F2101). (3) **MEDIUM: JWT secret runtime validation allows short secrets** (F2102). (4) **MEDIUM: CORS defaults to private network origins** (F2103). (5) **MEDIUM: Payment simulation bypasses filter chain** (F2104). (6) **MEDIUM: JWT stored in localStorage** (F2105). (7) **MEDIUM: Custom HTML sanitizer instead of DOMPurify** (F2106). (8) **MEDIUM: Guest order weak auth** (F2107). (9) **MEDIUM: Unbounded SELECT queries** (F2108). **Positive findings**: SQL injection CLEAN (MyBatis #{})`, path traversal CLEAN (filename sanitization), rate limiting COMPREHENSIVE (per-IP + per-user + login + password endpoints). Current totals: 2113 issues, 1999 FIXED, 12 WONTFIX, 102 OPEN.

Frontend Build ✅. **24 new multi-dimensional issues found (F2048–F2071).** Key findings: (1) **HIGH: 7 React unmount guard violations** across AdminDashboard, OrderManagement, StockAlerts, Notifications, Wishlist, BrowsingHistory, PetGallery — state updates after unmount causing potential memory leaks and stale renders. (2) **HIGH: Profile.tsx dateLocale ternary inverted** — `language === 'en'` should be `language === 'es'`, causing swapped date formatting between English and Spanish.
