- **Impact**: The product detail page has an image carousel with no `aria-label` on the carousel container, no `role="region"` or `aria-roledescription="carousel"`, and no `aria-label` on individual slides. Screen readers cannot navigate the image gallery.

### F2833: LOW — Login.tsx missing aria-label on OAuth buttons
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `Login.tsx`
- **Impact**: OAuth login buttons (Google, GitHub) have no `aria-label` describing what they do. The icons alone are not accessible to screen readers.
- **Severity**: LOW

### F2834: LOW — ProductUrlImportService.createImageRecord sets hardcoded MIME type image/jpeg
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `ProductUrlImportService.java:479-494`
- **Impact**: `createImageRecord()` always sets `mimeType = "image/jpeg"` regardless of the actual image type. PNG, WebP, and GIF images will all be stored with incorrect MIME types. The `mediaType` field is set correctly from the Content-Type header, but `mimeType` is hardcoded.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: Data Integrity

### F2835: LOW — i18n hardcoded strings in frontend code not in locale files
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `Profile.tsx:184, 371, 539`, `Product.tsx:436`, `ProductList.tsx:132, 341`, `BrowsingHistory.tsx:34, 38, 39`, `ProductImport.tsx:196`, `ProductImportExport.tsx:418, 486`, `AdminDashboard.tsx:913, 1000`, `OrderDetail.tsx:835`
- **Impact**: Multiple pages contain Chinese text not in locale files: "宠物品种" (pet breed), "品种" (breed), "商品名称不能为空" (product name required), "未知分类" (unknown category), "默认分类" (default category), "暂无浏览记录" (no browsing history), "加载中..." (loading...), "个商品" (items), "暂无相关商品" (no related products), "仅管理员可用" (admin only), "流量统计" (traffic stats), "添加商品" (add product), "编辑商品" (edit product), "商品管理" (product management), "库存" (stock), "导出商品" (export), "导出成功" (export success), "导出失败" (export failed), "正在导出" (exporting).
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: i18n

### F2836: LOW — i18n hardcoded strings in ConfigCenter.tsx and Navbar.tsx
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `ConfigCenter.tsx:348, 443`, `Navbar.tsx:177, 178`, `MobileUpdate.tsx:19`
- **Impact**: Additional hardcoded Chinese strings: "内容不能为空" (content required), "确认清除当前设备的IP封禁状态?" (confirm clear IP ban?), "已清除封禁" (ban cleared), "操作" (action), "添加" (add), "新版本可用" (new version available), "正在更新" (updating), "重新启动" (restart), "忽略此版本" (ignore version), "立即更新" (update now).

### F2837: LOW — i18n phone placeholder uses Chinese parentheses in English locale
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `en.json:187`
- **Impact**: The phone placeholder uses Chinese parentheses `（）` instead of English parentheses `()`: `"Please enter your phone number（For login credentials recovery）"`. This is inconsistent with the English locale.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: i18n

### F2838: LOW — No code splitting for page components — all pages loaded in single bundle
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `App.tsx`
- **Impact**: All page components (ProductList, Product, Cart, Checkout, Profile, NotFound, AdminDashboard, etc.) are imported directly without `React.lazy()`. This means the entire application is loaded in a single JavaScript bundle, increasing initial load time. Pages that are rarely visited (like AdminDashboard for non-admin users) are still downloaded.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: Performance

### F2839: LOW — Login page CSS has no responsive media queries
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `Login.css`
- **Impact**: The login page has no `@media` queries for responsive design. The login form width is fixed at 400px. On mobile devices (< 480px), the form may overflow horizontally or have excessive padding. On very large screens (> 1440px), the form may appear too narrow.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: UI/UX

### F2840: LOW — Login page CSS uses hardcoded color values instead of CSS variables
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `Login.css`
- **Impact**: The login page uses hardcoded colors like `#1890ff`, `#52c41a`, `#722ed1`, `#fff`, `#f0f2f5` instead of CSS custom properties. If a dark mode or theme system is added, these colors will need to be updated manually. Other pages use CSS variables (via antd theming).
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: UI/UX / Maintainability

### F2841: LOW — Multiple CSS files have no responsive media queries
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `AppDownload.css`, `Coupons.css`, `Home.css`, `Orders.css`, `PetGallery.css`, `PetMissing.css`, `Support.css`
- **Impact**: These pages have no `@media` queries for responsive design. They will not adapt to different screen sizes, potentially causing layout issues on mobile devices.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: UI/UX

### F2842: LOW — Coupons.css has 7 hardcoded !important CSS overrides
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `Coupons.css:13-21`
- **Impact**: The coupons page uses 7 `!important` overrides to force antd component styling. This creates specificity conflicts and makes future style changes difficult. The overrides should use more specific selectors or CSS modules.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: CSS Quality

### F2843: LOW — Orders.css has 11+ hardcoded CSS variable values
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `Orders.css:60-181`
- **Impact**: The orders page defines CSS custom properties (`--card-shadow`, `--background`, `--text-primary`, `--text-secondary`, `--border`, `--text-tertiary`) in the component stylesheet instead of using the global theme. This creates duplicate definitions and maintenance burden.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: CSS Quality

### F2844: LOW — Coupons.css overrides antd pagination font size with !important
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `Coupons.css:273`
- **Impact**: The coupons page overrides `.ant-pagination` font-size with `!important`, which affects all pagination components on the page and may conflict with future antd theme customizations.

- **Impact**: The login page has a `::after` pseudo-element with content "或使用以下方式登录" (or login with the following methods) that is not in the i18n system. This text will always appear in Chinese regardless of the selected language.

### F2860: HIGH — orderApi.pay sends no body; backend expects transactionId
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:1776`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/OrderController.java:256-277`
- **Impact**: Frontend `orderApi.pay` calls `api.put('/orders/{id}/pay')` with no request body. Backend `payOrder` reads `body.get("transactionId")` from `Map<String, String> body`. While `transactionId` is nullable, the payment confirmation record will always have a null transaction ID, making it impossible to reconcile payments with external payment providers.
- **Severity**: HIGH
- **Dimension**: API Contract / Data Integrity

### F2861: HIGH — orderApi.ship sends no body; backend expects trackingNumber and trackingCarrierCode
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:1777`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/OrderController.java:279-303`
- **Impact**: Frontend `orderApi.ship` calls `api.put('/orders/{id}/ship')` with no request body. Backend `shipOrder` reads `trackingNumber` and `trackingCarrierCode` from the request body. Both will be null when called from the frontend, resulting in shipped orders with no tracking information. Customers cannot track shipments.
- **Severity**: HIGH
- **Dimension**: API Contract / Business Logic

### F2862: HIGH — selectedSpecs max length mismatch: frontend 2000 vs backend 1000
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:21` (MAX_SELECTED_SPECS_LENGTH=2000), `src/main/java/com/example/shop/dto/CartAddRequest.java:23` (@Size(max=1000))
- **Impact**: Frontend normalizes `selectedSpecs` to 2000 characters, but backend CartAddRequest DTO limits to 1000. Specs between 1001-2000 chars pass frontend validation but fail backend with a 400 error. Affects `cartApi.addItem`.
- **Severity**: HIGH
- **Dimension**: API Contract / Validation

### F2863: MEDIUM — Missing Content-Security-Policy header
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/SecurityConfig.java`
- **Impact**: No CSP header is configured. A CSP header would provide defense-in-depth against XSS by restricting which scripts, styles, and resources the browser is allowed to load. Without it, any XSS vulnerability (including the custom sanitizer bypass risk in F2865) has full impact.
- **Severity**: MEDIUM
- **Dimension**: Security

### F2864: MEDIUM — JWT/refresh tokens stored in localStorage, vulnerable to XSS theft
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:1291-1302`
- **Impact**: JWT and refresh tokens are stored in `localStorage`, making them accessible to any JavaScript running on the page. If any XSS vector exists (including custom sanitizer bypasses), an attacker can steal tokens. The more secure alternative is `httpOnly` cookies which are inaccessible to JavaScript.
- **Severity**: MEDIUM
- **Dimension**: Security

### F2865: MEDIUM — Custom HTML sanitizer instead of DOMPurify for dangerouslySetInnerHTML
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `frontend/src/utils/sanitizeHtml.ts`, used in `Notifications.tsx:133`, `NotificationManagement.tsx:233`
- **Impact**: The custom `stripUnsafeHtml` sanitizer uses a blocklist approach (removing disallowed tags) rather than an allowlist. The blocked tag list does not include `details`, `video`, `audio`, `source`, `picture`, `noscript`, or `xmp`. Custom sanitizers are prone to bypass vectors. DOMPurify is the industry standard and should be used instead.
- **Severity**: MEDIUM
- **Dimension**: Security

### F2866: MEDIUM — Phone number returned in login/refresh token responses (PII exposure)
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/LoginController.java:401`
- **Impact**: The login and token-refresh responses include the user's phone number alongside the JWT. Phone numbers are sensitive PII and should not be returned by default in auth responses. An attacker who intercepts the login response (e.g., via XSS or network MITM) gets both the token and the phone.
- **Severity**: MEDIUM
- **Dimension**: Security / Privacy

### F2867: MEDIUM — Frontend checks for `code` field in error responses; backend never returns it
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `frontend/src/utils/apiError.ts:73-80`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/ApiErrorResponseFactory.java:28-37`
- **Impact**: Frontend `getApiErrorMessage` checks `response.data.code` for `LOGIN_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE` and `RATE_LIMITED`. Backend `ApiErrorResponseFactory.buildPayload()` never includes a `code` field. The `LOGIN_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE` check will never match. The `RATE_LIMITED` check has a fallback to HTTP 429 status which works, but the code-based check is dead logic.
- **Severity**: MEDIUM
- **Dimension**: API Contract / Error Handling

### F2868: MEDIUM — Admin coupon create/update sends raw Partial<Coupon> without normalization or validation
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:2391-2393`
- **Impact**: `adminApi.createCoupon` and `updateCoupon` send the Coupon object as-is with no normalization. Frontend `Coupon` type includes fields the backend does not accept (`id`, `remainingQuantity`, `claimedQuantity`) which are silently ignored. More critically, there is no frontend validation of required fields (`name`, `couponType`), so partial data produces confusing backend 400 errors.
- **Severity**: MEDIUM
- **Dimension**: API Contract / UX

### F2869: MEDIUM — Backend controllers return inconsistent error response shapes
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/CartController.java:46-47`, `CouponController.java:47-48`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/ApiErrorResponseFactory.java:28-37`
- **Impact**: Some controllers return `Map.of("error", e.getMessage())` for validation errors, while `GlobalApiExceptionHandler` returns `{ error, message, status, statusText, path, requestId, timestamp }`. The frontend reads both `data.error` and `data.message` so messages display correctly, but the response shape inconsistency could cause issues if frontend ever depends on `data.status` or other fields.
- **Severity**: MEDIUM
- **Dimension**: API Contract / Consistency

### F2870: LOW — Dead code: orderApi.create/update/delete hit disabled backend endpoints
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:1722,1754,1755`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/OrderController.java:52-59,152-159,161-165`
- **Impact**: Frontend exposes `orderApi.create`, `orderApi.update`, and `orderApi.delete` which POST/PUT/DELETE to `/orders` and `/orders/{id}`. Backend explicitly disables these endpoints, throwing `ResponseStatusException(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)`. These frontend functions are dead code that will always fail if called.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: Code Quality

### F2871: LOW — Admin order list returns raw Order entity (more fields than typed)
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:1684`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/OrderController.java:167-184`
- **Impact**: For admin users, backend returns raw `List<Order>` entity objects, but frontend types the response as `OrderCustomer[]`. The `Order` entity has many more fields (userId, customerUsername, customerEmail, customerPhone, customerType, orderItems, etc.). Not a runtime error but a type safety gap that could lead to accidental data exposure.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: API Contract / Type Safety

### F2872: LOW — CouponQuoteResponse shippingFee always hardcoded to zero
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/dto/CouponQuoteResponse.java:22`
- **Impact**: Backend `CouponQuoteResponse` constructor hardcodes `this.shippingFee = BigDecimal.ZERO` and does not accept `shippingFee` as a constructor parameter. Frontend `CouponQuote` type includes `shippingFee?: number` but will always receive 0. The field is meaningless in the API contract.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: API Contract / Dead Field

### F2873: LOW — Admin bootstrap token security depends entirely on runtime configuration
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/UserController.java:173-180`
- **Impact**: `POST /users/create-admin` is `permitAll()` but protected by a bootstrap token passed via `X-Bootstrap-Token` header. If the `admin.bootstrap-token` runtime config is empty, the endpoint throws FORBIDDEN. But a weak token would allow arbitrary admin account creation. Should add startup-time validation to ensure this is always set to a strong value.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: Security / Configuration

### F2874: LOW — adminApi.updateUser clears permission caches on roleCode change without sending roleCode
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:2122-2131`
- **Impact**: `adminApi.updateUser` calls `normalizeAdminUserUpdatePayload` which only sends `email, phone, address, role, status` — NOT `roleCode`. But the cache-clearing logic at line 2125 checks `user.roleCode` and clears permission caches when it changes, even though `roleCode` was not actually sent to the server. Overly broad cache invalidation.
- **Severity**: LOW
- **Dimension**: Code Quality / Performance

### F2875: MEDIUM — 32 of 38 frontend pages have zero test coverage
- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: Various pages in `frontend/src/pages/`
- **Impact**: Core user flows — Home, ProductList, ProductDetail, Cart, Register, ForgotPassword, OrderTracking, Profile, Wishlist — and all admin management pages have no test coverage. Only Login, Checkout, CartCheckoutFlow, CouponManagement, SupportManagement, and SecurityAuditLogManagement have tests. This means regressions in critical flows will not be caught by CI.

- **Dimension**: Test Coverage

- **Impact**: Components without tests include ErrorBoundary, CartDrawer, CustomerSupportWidget, ProductReview, AdminLayout, AddOnAssistant, PetPersonalizedAssistant, ProductRichDetailEditor, SkeletonLoader, and SocialProofToast. ErrorBoundary in particular is critical for error recovery.

- **Impact**: CartService, PaymentService (only partial via PaymentFlowServiceTest), RefundService, OrderService (only partial), CheckoutIdempotencyService, RuntimeConfigService, AdminRoleService, LogisticsCarrierService, PaymentChannelAvailabilityService, PetBirthdayCouponService, and ReviewImageService have no dedicated tests. CartService and PaymentService are critical for checkout flows.

- **Status**: OPEN
- **File**: Various controllers in `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/`
- **Impact**: Most admin controllers and key user-facing controllers (CartController, CategoryController, CouponController, ReviewController, WishlistController, NotificationController) have no test. Only OrderController, UserController, LoginController, ProductController, PaymentController, and a few admin controllers have tests.

### F3359: HIGH — Frontend review comment max length (2000) allows 2x what backend accepts (1000)
- **Files**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:1893`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/dto/ReviewCreateRequest.java:24`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/entity/Review.java:43`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: The frontend `reviewApi.create()` normalizes the comment to 2000 characters (`normalizeTextParam(comment, 2000)`), but the backend `ReviewCreateRequest` DTO has `@Size(max = 1000)` on the `comment` field. Reviews with comments between 1001-2000 characters will be accepted by the frontend UI but rejected by the backend with a 400 validation error. Users who write lengthy review comments will experience a silent submission failure — the frontend does not warn them.
- **Fix**: Reduce frontend normalization limit from 2000 to 1000, or increase backend entity/DTO limit to 2000.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 07:15 UTC)**: `reviewApi.create()` now normalizes comments with `MAX_REVIEW_COMMENT_LENGTH = 1000`. Current `ProductReview.tsx` already caps and counts the textarea at the same 1000 limit and warns before submit; new `ProductReview.test.tsx` guards this UI contract. `api/index.test.ts` now proves an oversized review comment is sent as 1000 characters. Targeted Jest and `tsc` passed.

### F3360: HIGH — Frontend product description max length (5000) allows 5x what backend accepts (1000)
- **Files**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:326`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/entity/Product.java:43`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: The frontend `normalizeProductPayload()` normalizes `description` to 5000 characters, but the Product entity annotation restricts it to 1000 characters. Admin product descriptions between 1001-5000 characters will fail server-side validation. The product edit UI does not enforce the 1000-character limit.
- **Fix**: Reduce frontend normalization limit from 5000 to 1000, or increase backend entity limit to 5000.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 07:06 UTC)**: `normalizeProductPayload()` now uses `PRODUCT_DESCRIPTION_MAX_LENGTH = 1000`, the Product Management description textarea has `maxLength`, `showCount`, and a Form max rule with localized `pages.productAdmin.descriptionMaxLength` copy, and `api/index.test.ts` guards admin create payload truncation to 1000. Targeted Jest and `tsc` passed.

### F3361: HIGH — Frontend product status field length (40) allows 2x what backend accepts (20)
- **Files**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:332`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/entity/Product.java:105-107`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: The frontend `normalizeProductPayload()` normalizes the `status` field to 40 characters (`normalizeTextParam(value, 40).toUpperCase()`), but the Product entity limits status to 20 characters. If any status value longer than 20 characters is sent, the backend will reject it. While standard statuses ("ACTIVE", "INACTIVE") fit, custom or malformed status strings up to 40 chars will cause server-side validation failures.
- **Fix**: Reduce frontend normalization limit from 40 to 20.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 07:15 UTC)**: Product create/update payload normalization and admin product status update/batch-status requests now use `MAX_PRODUCT_STATUS_LENGTH = 20`; admin product list status filter is also bounded to the product status limit. `api/index.test.ts` guards create, single-status update, and batch-status payloads. Targeted Jest and `tsc` passed.

### F3362: HIGH — Mobile "Add to Cart" button ignores required product option selections
- **Files**: `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx:864`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: The mobile purchase button's disabled state (`mobilePurchaseBlocked`) only checks `!isOutOfStock && purchaseSubmitting !== null`. It does NOT check `purchaseSelectionBlocked` (whether required option groups have been selected). The desktop "Add to Cart" calls `validateOptions()` inside `handleAddToCart()`, but `mobilePurchaseBlocked` does not incorporate this check. Mobile users see an enabled "Add to Cart" button for products with unselected options, click it, see a flash of loading state, then get a warning message. Confusing UX.
- **Fix**: Add `purchaseSelectionBlocked` to the `mobilePurchaseBlocked` condition.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 07:06 UTC)**: Product Detail now defines `addToCartBlocked` and `mobileAddToCartBlocked` using `purchaseSelectionBlocked`; desktop Add to cart uses `disabled={addToCartBlocked}`, mobile Add to cart uses `disabled={mobileAddToCartBlocked}`, and out-of-stock mobile `Notify me` remains enabled because the mobile blocker excludes the out-of-stock path. `ProductDetail.test.tsx` source guards cover all three purchase blockers. Targeted Jest and `tsc` passed.

### F3363: HIGH — Missing Content-Security-Policy (CSP) header
- **Files**: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/SecurityConfig.java:59-67`
- **Status**: OPEN
- **Description**: The security headers configuration sets `X-Content-Type-Options`, `X-Frame-Options`, `HSTS`, and `Referrer-Policy`, but there is no `Content-Security-Policy` header configured anywhere in the codebase. Without CSP, if an attacker manages to inject malicious script via stored XSS (e.g., in user-generated content), the browser has no policy to block execution. The `sanitizeHtml.ts` utility exists on the frontend but CSP provides defense-in-depth at the browser level.
- **Fix**: Add a CSP header to the security configuration.

### F3364: HIGH — JWT Token stored in localStorage — vulnerable to XSS token theft
- **Files**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:1291-1292`, `frontend/src/utils/safeStorage.ts`
- **Status**: OPEN
- **Description**: Both the JWT access token and refresh token are stored in `window.localStorage`. localStorage is accessible to any JavaScript running on the same origin, meaning any XSS vulnerability can steal both tokens. If a bypass is found in the custom `sanitizeHtml.ts` sanitizer (used with `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` in notifications), an attacker can exfiltrate both tokens and maintain persistent access.
- **Fix**: Store tokens in httpOnly cookies (set by server), or keep access token in memory with refresh token in httpOnly cookie.

### F3365: HIGH — Admin bootstrap endpoint exposed without authentication
- **Files**: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/SecurityConfig.java:102`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/UserController.java:136-163`
- **Status**: OPEN
- **Description**: The endpoint `POST /users/create-admin` is listed as `.permitAll()`. While it requires an `X-Bootstrap-Token` header validated via `constantTimeEquals`, this is a single shared secret with no rate limiting specific to this endpoint. If the bootstrap token is leaked (e.g., via logs, CI/CD, or environment variable exposure), any unauthenticated attacker can create admin accounts.
- **Fix**: Restrict endpoint to only work when no admin exists, or require network-level restrictions (localhost only), or add dedicated rate limiting.

### F3366: MEDIUM — Cart selectedSpecs frontend limit (2000) allows 2x what backend accepts (1000)
- **Files**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:20-21,1668`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/dto/CartAddRequest.java:23`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: The frontend allows `selectedSpecs` up to 2000 characters (`MAX_SELECTED_SPECS_LENGTH = 2000`), but the backend `CartAddRequest` DTO limits it to 1000. Cart items with specs between 1001-2000 chars will fail at the backend. Users selecting products with many or complex specifications will get cart add failures.
- **Fix**: Reduce frontend constant from 2000 to 1000.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 07:15 UTC)**: `MAX_SELECTED_SPECS_LENGTH` is now 1000 and is used by both `cartApi.addItem()` and guest checkout item normalization. `api/index.test.ts` proves oversized cart selected specs and guest checkout selected specs are sent as 1000 characters. Targeted Jest and `tsc` passed.

### F3367: MEDIUM — Guest checkout item frontend limit (100) exceeds backend limit (80)
- **Files**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:164`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/dto/GuestCheckoutRequest.java:31`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: The frontend allows up to 100 guest checkout items (`normalizeGuestCheckoutItems` slices to 100), but the backend `GuestCheckoutRequest` DTO limits items to `@Size(max = 80)`. Checkouts with 81-100 items will be rejected by the backend with a validation error.
- **Fix**: Reduce frontend slice limit from 100 to 80.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 07:15 UTC)**: Guest checkout item normalization now slices to `MAX_GUEST_CHECKOUT_ITEMS = 80`. `api/index.test.ts` proves a 90-line guest checkout payload is reduced to 80 before submission. Targeted Jest and `tsc` passed.

### F3368: MEDIUM — CheckoutRequest missing backend validation on recipientName and recipientPhone
- **Files**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:1727-1728`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/dto/CheckoutRequest.java:18-19`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: The backend `CheckoutRequest` DTO has no `@Size` validation on `recipientName` and `recipientPhone`. While the frontend limits these to 120 and 60 chars respectively, the backend will accept arbitrarily long strings, potentially leading to database overflow or unexpected behavior if the underlying columns have limits.
- **Fix**: Add `@Size(max = 120)` on `recipientName` and `@Size(max = 60)` on `recipientPhone` in `CheckoutRequest.java`.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 07:29 UTC)**: `CheckoutRequest` now annotates `recipientName` with `@Size(max = 120)` and `recipientPhone` with `@Size(max = 60)`. `OrderInputNormalizationServiceTest` now validates that 121/61 character values are rejected while 120/60 character values pass. Targeted Maven test passed.

### F3369: MEDIUM — Admin coupon create/update sends raw entity without normalization
- **Files**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:2391-2392`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/dto/CouponUpsertRequest.java`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: Unlike other admin endpoints where the frontend normalizes payloads through dedicated `normalize*Payload()` functions, the coupon create/update endpoints send `Partial<Coupon>` directly. The backend expects `CouponUpsertRequest` with `@NotBlank name`, `@NotBlank couponType`. If the frontend Coupon type includes fields not in CouponUpsertRequest (e.g., `id`, `createdAt`), these will be sent unnecessarily or cause deserialization issues.
- **Fix**: Add `normalizeCouponPayload()` function and use it before sending to backend.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 07:29 UTC)**: Added `normalizeCouponPayload()` and wired `adminApi.createCoupon/updateCoupon` through it. The frontend now sends only `CouponUpsertRequest` fields, normalizes text/case/numeric bounds, and drops entity-only fields such as `id`, `claimedQuantity`, `remainingQuantity`, and `createdAt`. `api/index.test.ts` covers create and update payloads.

### F3370: MEDIUM — Coupon quote auto-select causes unnecessary double API call
- **Files**: `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:705-755`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: The coupon quote `useEffect` depends on `selectedUserCouponId`. On initial load, when `autoSelectBestCoupon` is enabled and no coupon is selected, the first API call returns available coupons and auto-selects the best one by calling `setSelectedUserCouponId(nextCouponId)`. This state change triggers the same `useEffect` to fire again, making a second identical API call. Every authenticated checkout page load makes 2 coupon quote API calls instead of 1.
- **Fix**: Either don't depend on `selectedUserCouponId` when auto-selecting, or don't re-fetch when only the auto-selected coupon changes.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 07:15 UTC)**: Checkout now records the cart key and coupon id when the backend quote response already includes the auto-selected `selectedUserCouponId`, then skips the immediate next effect for that same cart/coupon pair. Cart changes and manual coupon changes still clear the auto-selection ref and re-quote. `Checkout.test.tsx` guards the source contract. Targeted Jest and `tsc` passed.

### F3371: MEDIUM — Gift threshold only configured for MXN currency
- **Files**: `frontend/src/utils/cartBenefits.ts:14-19`, `frontend/src/utils/conversionConfig.ts:12`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: The `getGiftThreshold` function returns 0 for any currency that is not MXN. The `conversionConfig` only defines `thresholdMxn: 1299` with no thresholds for USD, CAD, EUR, or GBP. The Cart and Checkout pages still render gift-related UI gated on `conversionConfig.giftAtCheckout.enabled`, but since `getGiftThreshold` returns 0 for non-MXN currencies, the gift UI is effectively hidden for those markets. The `giftAtCheckout.enabled: true` flag is misleading.
- **Fix**: Either add gift thresholds for all supported currencies, or add a per-currency enable flag.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 16:08 UTC)**: `conversionConfig.giftAtCheckout` now exposes `thresholdsByCurrency` for all supported shopper currencies (`MXN`, `USD`, `CAD`, `EUR`, `GBP`) and `getGiftThreshold()` looks up only known currency codes through `isCurrencyCode(...)`. The thresholds use the same base cart-total unit as existing cart/checkout calculations, while display conversion remains handled by `formatMoney(...)`. `cartBenefits.test.ts` now proves every supported currency has a positive threshold, unsupported currencies return `0`, and NaN inputs do not leak into benefit targets. Checkout's existing gift eligibility guard remains based on `giftThreshold > 0`.

### F3372: MEDIUM — Checkout "restore submitted cart items" uses stale price from cart snapshot for guest users
- **Files**: `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:1412-1436`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: When a user cancels a pending payment, `restoreSubmittedCartItems` re-adds items to the cart. For authenticated users, the server applies the current price. But for guest users, the price comes from the checkout page's cart snapshot (`item.price`), not the current product price. If a price change occurred between checkout load and payment cancellation, the restored cart has incorrect prices.
- **Fix**: Guest cart restoration should either re-fetch current prices or use the server-provided price.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 07:29 UTC)**: Guest restore now fetches current products with `productApi.getByIds(productIds, { bypassCache: true })` before writing guest cart items back. `resolveGuestRestorePrice()` prefers current bundle price, selected variant SKU/options price, or current effective product price, and falls back to the original cart snapshot only if the refresh is unavailable. `Checkout.test.tsx` guards the source contract.

### F3373: MEDIUM — Cart item availability check is less strict than checkout's check
- **Files**: `frontend/src/utils/cartUi.ts:8-17`, `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:71-72`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: In `cartUi.ts`, `isCartItemAvailable` only checks that stock > 0. In Checkout.tsx, `isPurchasable` checks that stock >= quantity. This means a cart item with stock=2 and quantity=3 will appear as "available" in the cart (green, selectable), but will be filtered out as unpurchasable in checkout. The cart should show the item as having insufficient stock.
- **Fix**: Change `isCartItemAvailable` to also check `stock >= quantity` when quantity is known.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 07:29 UTC)**: `cartUi.isCartItemAvailable()` now requires finite stock to cover the requested line quantity when stock is known, and `canCartItemCheckout()` delegates to that shared contract. `cartUi.test.ts` covers `stock=2, quantity=3` as unavailable and `stock=3, quantity=3` as available.

### F3374: MEDIUM — Category repository keyword query vulnerable to LIKE injection
- **Files**: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/repository/CategoryRepository.java:19-21`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: The `findIdsByKeyword` query uses LIKE with unescaped metacharacters. While JPA parameter binding prevents SQL injection, LIKE special characters (`%`, `_`) in user-supplied keyword are not escaped. A user searching for `%` would match all records, and `_` acts as a single-character wildcard.
- **Fix**: Escape LIKE metacharacters before passing to the query, or use a different matching approach.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 07:39 UTC)**: `CategoryRepository.findIdsByKeyword()` and public keyword product repository windows now use `LIKE ... ESCAPE '!'`. `ProductServiceImpl` escapes `%`, `_`, and `!` before category lookup, finder candidate windows, and CriteriaBuilder product keyword predicates. `ProductSearchServiceTest` guards `escapeLikeTerm("100%_!promo") -> "100!%!_!!promo"` and source-checks the repository escape contract.

### F3375: MEDIUM — ResponseStatusException reason messages may leak internal details
- **Files**: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/GlobalApiExceptionHandler.java:37-49,61-63,141`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: The `handleResponseStatus` method passes `exception.getReason()` directly as the error message to the client. The `handleBadRequest` method for `IllegalArgumentException` falls through to `exception.getMessage()`, potentially exposing internal state. Attackers can use error message content to map internal data structures.
- **Fix**: Return generic error messages to clients; log internal messages only server-side.
- **Triage note (2026-06-09 08:05 UTC)**: Kept OPEN. `ApiErrorResponseFactory.sanitizeMessage()` already strips control characters and caps response messages at 240 characters, and existing tests intentionally protect user-facing validation/business errors such as `Insufficient stock`. Closing this safely needs a policy/allowlist pass for which exception reasons remain customer-safe, not a broad generic replacement that would regress checkout/auth/admin validation UX.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 16:08 UTC)**: `GlobalApiExceptionHandler` now resolves `ResponseStatusException`, `IllegalArgumentException`, and `IllegalStateException` messages through a client-safe policy. 5xx reasons always return `Internal server error`; unsafe 4xx/internal-service details such as secrets, JDBC/SQL/database/Redis/Nacos/configuration/path/URL details are filtered to `Bad request` and logged server-side with path/requestId. Safe customer-facing validation/business messages remain visible, including `Insufficient stock` and `Current password is incorrect`. `GlobalApiExceptionHandlerTest` covers sensitive reason filtering, 5xx reason suppression, sensitive bad-request filtering, and preserved safe validation messages.

### F3376: MEDIUM — Password not sanitized before sending to backend (DoS risk)
- **Files**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:1414,1412,1437`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: The frontend sends the raw password value with no length check. The backend `RegisterRequest` has `@Size(min = 8, max = 128)` but `updatePassword` may not have the same constraint. An attacker could send extremely long password strings (e.g., 1MB) to the login/register/password-change endpoints, causing excessive CPU consumption during bcrypt hashing.
- **Fix**: Enforce a maximum password length (e.g., 128 chars) on the frontend before sending.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 07:39 UTC)**: Added frontend `normalizePasswordParam()` with a 128-character ceiling for register, login, forgot-password, and password-update calls; overlong passwords throw before Axios requests are made. Backend `UpdatePasswordRequest.oldPassword` now has `@Size(max = 128)`, and `UserService.updatePassword()` rejects overlong current passwords before `passwordEncoder.matches(...)`. Guards added in `api/index.test.ts` and `UserServiceTest`.

### F3377: MEDIUM — XSS risk in DOM-based HTML sanitizer
- **Files**: `frontend/src/utils/sanitizeHtml.ts`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: The `stripUnsafeHtml` function creates a DOM `<template>` element and sets `innerHTML` on it. While it removes blocked tags and dangerous attributes, the approach has known limitations: mutation-based XSS vectors may be possible during parsing, and the blocklist approach is vulnerable to novel vectors. Used with `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` in `NotificationManagement.tsx` and `Notifications.tsx`.
- **Fix**: Use a well-audited HTML sanitization library like DOMPurify instead of a custom implementation.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 08:05 UTC)**: Added `dompurify` and changed `stripUnsafeHtml()` to call `DOMPurify.sanitize(...)` with the current rich-text allowlist, while preserving stricter project URL/attribute policy through DOMPurify hooks. `sanitizeHtml.test.ts` now guards the DOMPurify dependency and rejects a return to `document.createElement('template')`; existing malicious URL/container/attribute cases still pass.

### F3378: MEDIUM — Guest order operations lack rate limiting
- **Files**: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/SecurityConfig.java:89-93`
- **Status**: WONTFIX / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_CONFIRMED
- **Description**: Multiple sensitive guest order endpoints are `.permitAll()` (cancel, confirm, return, return-shipment, checkout). These require `guestEmail` + `orderNo` as credential, but the security depends entirely on the secrecy of the order number. Order numbers may be sequential or predictable. No endpoint-specific rate limiting for credential guessing.
- **Fix**: Add aggressive rate limiting (per IP and per order ID), and ensure order numbers are cryptographically random.
- **Resolution evidence (2026-06-09 08:05 UTC)**: Current source already applies `RateLimitFilter` globally and `RateLimitService.endpointLimitFor(...)` defines dedicated per-client buckets for `checkout:guest`, `orders:guest-lookup`, and `orders:guest-mutation`. `RateLimitServiceTest.guestCheckoutEndpointHasDedicatedHourlyPerClientLimit()` and `guestOrderMutationEndpointsShareDedicatedPerClientLimitAcrossIds()` guard the sensitive guest checkout/mutation paths; `orderNumbersAndLongTokensAreCollapsedInBucketPath()` avoids high-cardinality order-number buckets.

### F3379: LOW — Product imageUrl frontend limit (2048) slightly exceeds backend limit (2000)
- **Files**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:331`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/entity/Product.java:51`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: The frontend normalizes product `imageUrl` to 2048 characters, but the entity limits it to 2000. URLs between 2001-2048 characters will be rejected by the backend. Extremely rare in practice since most image URLs are under 2000 chars.
- **Fix**: Reduce frontend normalization limit from 2048 to 2000.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 07:39 UTC)**: Added `MAX_PRODUCT_IMAGE_URL_LENGTH = 2000` and wired product payload `imageUrl` normalization through it. `api/index.test.ts` proves an oversized valid product image URL is reduced to 2000 characters before admin product create.
- **Additional fix evidence (2026-06-09 08:05 UTC)**: Test engineer sub-agent `Einstein` found the backend service/import image URL precheck still used 2048. `ProductServiceImpl.MAX_IMPORT_IMAGE_URL_LENGTH` now aligns to 2000, and `ProductSaveServiceTest` / `ProductImportServiceTest` reject 2001-character product image URLs before repository persistence.

### F3380: LOW — Login response email/phone not stored in frontend
- **Files**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:1274-1305`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/LoginController.java:394-405`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: The backend login response includes `email` and `phone` fields, but `persistAuthSession()` in the frontend does not store them. The `AuthSessionResponse` type only defines `token`, `refreshToken`, `id`, `username`, `role`, `roleCode`. Components that need the user's email must make an additional API call to `/users/profile`, adding unnecessary latency.
- **Fix**: Store email/phone from login response in local storage.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 08:05 UTC)**: `AuthSessionResponse` now includes `email` and `phone`; `persistAuthSession()` stores normalized values from login/refresh responses; `AUTH_SESSION_STORAGE_KEYS` clears them on logout/session expiry; and `useAuth` profile hydration keeps them synchronized. Guards added in `api/index.test.ts` and `useAuth.test.tsx`.

### F3381: LOW — Missing Permissions-Policy header
- **Files**: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/SecurityConfig.java:59-67`
- **Status**: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: No `Permissions-Policy` header is configured. This header restricts which browser features (camera, microphone, geolocation, etc.) can be used by the page and embedded content. In combination with missing CSP, any XSS exploit would have full access to browser APIs.
- **Fix**: Add `Permissions-Policy` header to security configuration.
- **Fix evidence (2026-06-09 08:05 UTC)**: `SecurityConfig` now emits `Permissions-Policy` denying camera, microphone, geolocation, accelerometer, gyroscope, magnetometer, USB, serial, Bluetooth, and browsing topics. `SecurityConfigCorsTest.securityHeadersIncludeRestrictivePermissionsPolicy()` guards the header contract.

### F3382: LOW — Native SQL queries use positional parameters (maintenance risk)
- **Files**: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/repository/ProductRepository.java:93-100`, `CouponRepository.java:134-138`, `PetGalleryPhotoRepository.java:60-63`
- **Status**: OPEN
- **Description**: Several native SQL queries use positional parameters (`?1`, `?2`) instead of named parameters (`:paramName`). While safe from SQL injection, they are more error-prone during maintenance — swapping parameter positions would not be caught at compile time.
- **Fix**: Convert to named parameters for clarity and maintainability.

### F3383: LOW — Actuator health endpoint fully public
- **Files**: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/SecurityConfig.java:70`
- **Status**: OPEN
- **Description**: `GET /actuator/health` and `GET /actuator/health/**` are `permitAll()`. The `/**` wildcard means any sub-path is accessible. Depending on Spring Boot configuration, health details (disk space, database connectivity) may be exposed.
- **Fix**: Ensure `management.endpoint.health.show-details` is set to `never` or `when-authorized`.

### F3384: LOW — Dead code: orderApi.create/update/delete/addItem call disabled backend endpoints
- **Files**: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:1722,1754,1755,1804`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/OrderController.java:53-59,152-165,204-211`
- **Status**: OPEN
- **Description**: The frontend exposes `orderApi.create()`, `orderApi.update()`, `orderApi.delete()`, and `orderApi.addItem()` which all hit backend endpoints that always return 403 Forbidden. These are dead code paths that could confuse developers or cause unexpected failures if accidentally used.
- **Fix**: Remove dead API methods from frontend, or add `@Deprecated` annotations with clear warnings.

### F3385: LOW — Confusing `discountPercent` field name semantics
- **Files**: `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:109-123`, `frontend/src/utils/couponCenter.ts:27-32`
- **Status**: OPEN
- **Description**: The field `coupon.discountPercent` represents the **payable percentage** (what the customer pays), not the discount percentage. The code consistently calculates the discount as `(100 - discountPercent)`. For example, `discountPercent: 90` means the customer pays 90% (10% off). The field name is misleading and creates high risk of future maintenance bugs.
- **Fix**: Rename to `payablePercent` or add clear documentation.

### F3386: LOW — `couponUiText.ts` uses inline translations instead of main locale files
- **Files**: `frontend/src/utils/couponUiText.ts`
- **Status**: OPEN
- **Description**: This file defines 28 translation strings in a standalone `Record<'en' | 'es' | 'zh', CouponUiText>` object rather than using the main `locales/*.json` files and the `t()` function. These strings are completely separate from the main i18n system and won't appear in translation management tooling.
- **Fix**: Move strings into `en.json`, `es.json`, `zh.json` and use `t()` throughout.

### F3387: LOW — `mountedRef` pattern used inconsistently in Cart.tsx
- **Files**: `frontend/src/pages/Cart.tsx:143,157-185,191-196`
- **Status**: OPEN
- **Description**: The `fetchCartItems` callback does NOT check `mountedRef.current` before calling `setCartItems` and `setSelectedIds` in the error path. Other callbacks in the same file DO check `mountedRef`. While React handles this gracefully in modern versions, the inconsistency could cause confusion during debugging.
- **Fix**: Add `mountedRef.current` check to all async state-updating callbacks consistently.

### F3388: LOW — `productRecommendationsCache` grows unbounded across product navigations
- **Files**: `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx:50`
- **Status**: SOURCE_FIXED / FRONTEND_TSC_PASS / TARGETED_JEST_PASS
- **Fixed**: 2026-06-08 12:24 UTC
- **Description**: The `productRecommendationsCache` is a module-level `Map` that caches recommendations per product/language combination. It is cleared on auth session change but otherwise never evicts entries. In long browsing sessions with many unique products, memory usage grows without bound.
- **Fix**: Add LRU eviction policy (e.g., max 50 entries) similar to `recentProductsCache` in Cart.tsx.
- **Fix evidence**: `ProductDetail.tsx` now caps the module-level recommendation cache with `PRODUCT_RECOMMENDATIONS_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES = 50`, prunes expired entries before reads/writes, refreshes insertion order on cache hits, and evicts the oldest entry when adding beyond the cap. `ProductDetail.test.tsx` covers the bounded LRU behavior by filling the cache to the cap, reading an old entry to make it recent, inserting one more entry, and verifying the true least-recently-used entry is evicted while the recently-read entry remains. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅; `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/ProductDetail.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅ (passes with existing React/React Router/Suspense warning noise).

### F3389: LOW — Cart table "Select All" checkbox has no accessible name
- **Files**: `frontend/src/pages/Cart.tsx:920-928`, `frontend/src/pages/CartCheckoutFlow.test.tsx`
- **Status**: SOURCE_FIXED / FRONTEND_TSC_PASS / TARGETED_JEST_PASS
- **Fixed**: 2026-06-08 12:35 UTC
- **Description**: The "Select All" checkbox in the table header renders inside an Ant Design `Checkbox` with visible text, but the column `title` is a React element and Ant Design's `Table` may not associate it programmatically as a table header label. Screen reader users may hear "checkbox" without context.
- **Fix**: Add `aria-label` to the header checkbox.
- **Fix evidence**: The Cart table header checkbox now sets an explicit `aria-label` using `pages.cart.selectAll`, while keeping the visible label for sighted users. `CartCheckoutFlow.test.tsx` asserts the authenticated cart table exposes a checkbox with accessible name `Select all`. Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/CartCheckoutFlow.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅.

### F3390: LOW — Product image gallery thumbnails use `<img>` with `role="button"` instead of `<button>` wrapping
- **Files**: `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx:1520-1558`, `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.css:1099-1119,2876-2896`, `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.test.tsx`
- **Status**: SOURCE_FIXED / FRONTEND_TSC_PASS / TARGETED_JEST_PASS
- **Fixed**: 2026-06-08 12:35 UTC
- **Description**: Desktop gallery thumbnails use `<img>` elements with `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`, and `onKeyDown` handlers. Best practice is to wrap the `<img>` in a `<button>` element. Mobile gallery thumbnails correctly use `<button>` elements.
- **Fix**: Use `<button>` wrapping for desktop thumbnails, matching the mobile pattern.
- **Fix evidence**: Desktop carousel thumbnails now render as real `<button type="button">` controls with `aria-pressed`, `aria-label`, click handling, and gallery arrow-key handling on the button. The nested thumbnail `<img>` is presentational (`alt=""`) and no longer carries `role`, `tabIndex`, click handlers, or keyboard handlers. CSS moved active/focus/hover styling from the image to `.product-detail-thumbs__button`. `ProductDetail.test.tsx` asserts the gallery thumbnail is a `BUTTON`, the nested image has no role, and arrow-key navigation still changes the hero image. Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/ProductDetail.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅.

### F3391: LOW — Checkout address Radio components lack individual `aria-label` attributes
- **Files**: `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:2452-2480`, `frontend/src/pages/CartCheckoutFlow.test.tsx`
- **Status**: SOURCE_FIXED / FRONTEND_TSC_PASS / TARGETED_JEST_PASS
- **Fixed**: 2026-06-08 12:35 UTC
- **Description**: The address radio buttons contain nested `<Text>` elements with address details. Screen readers may announce the raw text content without structure (e.g., "John 555-1234 123 Main St" as a single label).
- **Fix**: Add `aria-label` to each `<Radio>` with structured content like `"John, 555-1234, 123 Main St, Default address"`.
- **Fix evidence**: Saved-address radios now build a structured label from recipient, normalized phone, address text, and the localized default-address marker when present. The "Use new address" radio also has an explicit `aria-label` and title. `CartCheckoutFlow.test.tsx` verifies an authenticated checkout exposes the saved default address radio as `Member Buyer, 5552223333, 1 Member Way, Default address` and still exposes the new-address radio. Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/CartCheckoutFlow.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅.

### F3392: LOW — Coupon/Wallet filter buttons lack `role="group"` for container
- **Files**: `frontend/src/pages/CouponCenter.tsx:929,1207`
- **Status**: SOURCE_FIXED / FRONTEND_TSC_PASS / STATIC_SOURCE_CHECK
- **Fixed**: 2026-06-08 12:35 UTC
- **Description**: The coupon claim filter buttons and wallet filter buttons are in a container with `aria-label` but no `role` attribute. Screen readers may not announce the filter buttons as a related group.
- **Fix**: Add `role="group"` to the filter button container divs.
- **Fix evidence**: `coupon-claim-section__filters` and `coupon-wallet__filters` now both render with `role="group"` while preserving their existing localized `aria-label` values. Verification: static source check confirmed both containers have `role="group"`; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅.

### F3394: HIGH — storeId cross-store data isolation missing in checkout, cart, order, and admin APIs

- **Files**: `backend/src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/CheckoutController.java:59-91`, `backend/src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/CartController.java:89-96`, `backend/src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/OrderController.java:128-132`, `backend/src/main/java/com/example/shop/entity/CartItem.java:12-47`
- **Status**: WONTFIX / CURRENT_SOURCE_NON_ISSUE
- **Description**: Although `Cart` and `CartItem` entities declare a `storeId` field (defaulting to `"default"`), no backend controller actually filters by `storeId` when performing operations. `CartController.getCart()` queries only by `userId`, `addItem()` and `updateItem()` find carts/items only by `userId`/`itemId`, and `CheckoutController.checkout()` loads the user's cart without any store scope. In a multi-tenant scenario, Store A's products could appear in Store B's cart, orders could be placed against cross-store inventory, and admin product operations lack store-level scoping. This is a medium-to-high severity data isolation and business logic defect.
- **Fix**: (1) Accept `storeId` from request headers/params and propagate it to all cart/checkout/order repository queries. (2) Ensure `CartItem` lookups filter on both `cart.storeId` and `itemId`. (3) Scope admin product/inventory endpoints by store. (4) Add integration tests verifying cross-store isolation.
- **Triage evidence**: Stale report against current source. Current backend has no `CheckoutController`, `AdminOrderController`, `AdminProductController`, `StoreMemberRepository`, or `Cart` entity, and static search found no `storeId`/`store_id` product/cart/order field or request DTO property in current checkout/cart APIs. Current `CartController` resolves the effective user through `SecurityUtils.requireUser(...)` / `assertSelf(...)`; `OrderService.prepareCheckoutItems(...)` loads selected cart item IDs and rejects any item whose `userId` does not match the checkout user before locking/reserving stock. No source change required unless a future marketplace/store-membership model is introduced.

### F3395: HIGH — Price manipulation: `product.price` used from client-supplied cart items instead of authoritative DB price

- **Files**: `backend/src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/OrderService.java:251-257`, `backend/src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/CheckoutController.java:155-162`, `backend/src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/CartController.java:89-96`
- **Status**: CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- **Description**: When placing an order via `POST /orders`, the `OrderItem` is built using `ci.getUnitPrice()` which comes from the `OrderItem` JSON payload sent by the frontend — not from the product table. A malicious client could submit a modified price (e.g., $0.01 instead of $99.99) and the backend would accept it. The same pattern exists in `CartItemResponse` which reads `ci.getProduct().getPrice()` from the product entity, but the `CartItem.productId` → product relationship must be verified. The frontend does not re-validate prices server-side before checkout. This is a medium-to-high severity financial/business logic defect.
- **Fix**: In `OrderService.createOrder()`, look up each product by ID and use `product.getPrice()` from the database as the authoritative price, ignoring any client-supplied `unitPrice`. Add a price-verification step at checkout confirmation time.
- **Fix evidence**: Current source has no `unitPrice` field in checkout/cart request DTOs and direct `createOrder(Order)` throws `IllegalStateException("Direct order creation is disabled; use checkout flows")`. Active authenticated checkout runs through `OrderService.prepareCheckoutItems(...)`, which reloads products with `productRepository.findAllByIdForUpdate(...)`, validates active product/variant/stock, and overwrites each cart item price with `ProductVariantService.resolvePrice(...)` before coupon quote, total calculation, and `insertCheckoutOrderItems(...)`. Guest checkout builds synthetic cart items from product IDs and authoritative product/variant prices only. Added `PaymentFlowServiceTest.checkoutUsesAuthoritativeProductPriceInsteadOfCartSnapshotPrice()` proving a forged/stale cart snapshot price of `0.01` is ignored and the persisted order total/order-item price use the current product price of `50.00`. Verification: targeted Maven test passed.

### F3396: MEDIUM — `OrderService.createOrder()` has unused `entityManager` field and redundant flush logic

- **Files**: `backend/src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/OrderService.java:65-69,249-264,338`
- **Status**: WONTFIX / CURRENT_SOURCE_NON_ISSUE
- **Triaged**: 2026-06-08 12:44 UTC
- **Description**: `OrderService` declares `private final EntityManager entityManager` and passes it via constructor, but it is never directly used (the service operates via JPA repository methods). Additionally, `createOrder()` calls `orderRepository.flush()` at line 264 immediately after `save()`, which is redundant within the same transaction. The `entityManager` field is dead code and should be removed. The `flush()` call may cause unnecessary database round-trips in the common path.
- **Fix**: Remove the unused `entityManager` field and its constructor parameter. Evaluate whether `flush()` is needed (likely not in the normal path; it's only useful if you need the generated ID before the transaction commits).
- **Triage evidence**: Stale report. Current `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/OrderService.java` has no `EntityManager` import, field, constructor parameter, or `flush()` call. Static source search `rg -n "EntityManager|entityManager|flush\\(" src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/OrderService.java src/test/java/com/example/shop/service` returned no matches. The current direct `createOrder(Order)` method rejects use of that legacy path with `IllegalStateException("Direct order creation is disabled; use checkout flows")`, while active checkout flows persist orders through mapper/repository insert/update paths.

### F3397: MEDIUM — Frontend product options stored in localStorage without validation; stale data causes stale UI

- **Files**: `frontend/src/utils/productOptions.ts:61-64`, `frontend/src/utils/productOptions.test.ts:24-29`
- **Status**: WONTFIX / CURRENT_SOURCE_NON_ISSUE (duplicate covered by F3427)
- **Triaged**: 2026-06-08 12:26 UTC
- **Description**: `productOptions.ts` caches fetched product option data into `localStorage` under `__product_options_v1` with no TTL, size limit, or validation. If the backend adds/removes/renames options, the frontend continues rendering stale cached data until the user clears localStorage manually. The cache can also grow unbounded if many products are viewed. This is a medium-severity UX/data freshness defect.
- **Fix**: Add a TTL (e.g., 24 hours), a max entry count, and/or a cache version check that invalidates when the API schema changes. Consider using `sessionStorage` instead for ephemeral caching.
- **Triage evidence**: Duplicate of F3427. Current `productOptions.ts` has no localStorage access and no `__product_options_v1` cache; it derives option groups and variants from current product payload fields. Static search for persistent option-cache keys and option-related safeStorage access finds no current-source cache to expire. See F3427 for full evidence and E2E handoff.

### F3398: MEDIUM — Mobile update test uses hardcoded version values that drift with each release

- **Files**: `frontend/src/utils/mobileUpdate.test.ts:69-76`, `frontend/src/generated/mobileRelease.ts:1-17`
- **Status**: SOURCE_FIXED / TARGETED_JEST_PASS (duplicate covered by F3428)
- **Fixed**: 2026-06-08 12:26 UTC
- **Description**: The `mobileUpdate.test.ts` file at line 69 asserts `currentMobileVersionCode()` returns `10023` and `currentMobileVersionName()` returns `'1.0.23'`, but the generated `mobileRelease.ts` constant has already been bumped to `10024`/`'1.0.24'`. When the runtime config window variable is set to the old test value, the function returns the newer generated constant instead, causing a test failure. This is a low-severity test maintenance defect that makes the test flaky on every mobile release.
- **Fix**: Update the test to read the expected values from `CURRENT_MOBILE_RELEASE` constants (e.g., `CURRENT_MOBILE_RELEASE.versionCode` and `CURRENT_MOBILE_RELEASE.versionName`) instead of hardcoding them, or mock the generated constant entirely.
- **Fix evidence**: Duplicate of F3428. Current `mobileUpdate.test.ts` imports `currentMobileRelease` and asserts generated-release fallback against `currentMobileRelease.versionCode` / `currentMobileRelease.versionName`, while the `10023` / `1.0.23` literals remain only as explicit runtime-config override fixtures. Verification: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/utils/mobileUpdate.test.ts --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅.

### F3399: LOW — `productRecommendationsCache` grows unbounded across product navigations

- **Files**: `frontend/src/components/ProductRecommendations.tsx` (cache map), `frontend/src/utils/productOptions.ts:61-64`
- **Status**: SOURCE_FIXED / FRONTEND_TSC_PASS / TARGETED_JEST_PASS (duplicate current-source cache fixed by F3388)
- **Fixed**: 2026-06-08 12:24 UTC
- **Description**: The product recommendations component caches fetched data in a module-level `Map` that never evicts entries. Navigating through hundreds of products accumulates stale entries in memory. While not a correctness issue, it is a resource leak that could degrade performance on low-memory mobile devices during long browsing sessions.
- **Fix**: Add LRU eviction (e.g., cap at 50 entries) or use `sessionStorage` with TTL.
- **Fix evidence**: Current source does not have `frontend/src/components/ProductRecommendations.tsx`, but the real current module-level product recommendation cache is `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx` and is fixed under F3388. It now has TTL pruning, LRU hit refresh, and a 50-entry cap with targeted ProductDetail Jest coverage. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` ✅; `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/pages/ProductDetail.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅.

### F3400: LOW — `Coupons.tsx` hero image has hardcoded Unsplash URL; fails offline and leaks external requests

- **Files**: `frontend/src/pages/Coupons.tsx:358-363`
- **Status**: WONTFIX / CURRENT_SOURCE_NON_ISSUE
- **Triaged**: 2026-06-08 12:35 UTC
- **Description**: The coupons page hero image uses `https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1607082349566-187342175e2f?w=1200&h=300&fit=crop` as a hardcoded external URL. In offline or restricted network environments, this produces a broken image. It also makes a third-party request without user consent, which is a minor privacy concern. The rest of the app uses local/public assets.
- **Fix**: Replace with a local asset in `frontend/public/images/` or an API-driven banner URL.
- **Triage evidence**: Stale report. Current source has no `frontend/src/pages/Coupons.tsx`; the active route uses `frontend/src/pages/CouponCenter.tsx`. The current coupon hero at `CouponCenter.tsx:494-570` renders structured copy, buttons, badges, stat cards, and plan content, while `CouponCenter.css:46-66` supplies CSS gradients only. Static search for the reported URL and source image usage returns no matches: `rg -n "photo-1607082349566|187342175e2f|https://images\\.unsplash\\.com/photo-160708|<img|background-image|url\\('https://images\\.unsplash|url\\(\\\"https://images\\.unsplash" frontend/src/pages/CouponCenter.tsx frontend/src/pages/CouponCenter.css frontend/src/App.tsx` ✅ no output. No production code change required.

### F3401: LOW — Backend `GlobalApiExceptionHandler` logs `requestId` as empty string for non-request-bound failures

- **Files**: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/ApiErrorResponseFactory.java`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/GlobalApiExceptionHandler.java`, `src/test/java/com/example/shop/config/GlobalApiExceptionHandlerTest.java`
- **Status**: SOURCE_FIXED / TARGETED_MAVEN_PASS
- **Fixed**: 2026-06-08 12:42 UTC
- **Description**: `GlobalApiExceptionHandler` reads the request ID from MDC (`MDC.get("requestId")`) and includes it in the error response body. When the exception occurs outside a request context (e.g., during async processing or scheduled tasks), `MDC.get("requestId")` returns `null` and the response includes `"requestId": ""`. This makes it impossible to correlate errors in logs with the originating context. The `REQUEST_ID_ATTRIBUTE` constant in `RequestIdFilter` is also never actually used (the filter reads `X-Request-Id` directly from the header).
- **Fix**: (1) Set a fallback request ID (e.g., `"system"` or a generated UUID) when MDC is empty. (2) Remove or use the unused `REQUEST_ID_ATTRIBUTE` constant. (3) Consider propagating request IDs to async contexts via `TaskDecorator`.
- **Fix evidence**: Current source already uses `RequestCorrelationFilter` instead of the stale `RequestIdFilter` name, and its `REQUEST_ID_ATTRIBUTE` is actively used by the filter and error factory. The real current defect was centralized in `ApiErrorResponseFactory.resolveRequestId(...)`, which returned `""` when the request was null or had no correlation attribute/header. It now returns `ApiErrorResponseFactory.FALLBACK_REQUEST_ID` (`"unavailable"`) for null or uncorrelated requests and sanitizes attribute/header request-id values separately from user-facing error messages. `GlobalApiExceptionHandlerTest` now verifies unexpected exception payloads use the fallback request id and direct payload creation with a null request also emits the fallback while keeping an empty path. Verification: `./mvnw -q -Dtest=GlobalApiExceptionHandlerTest test` ✅; handled-exception test logs now show `requestId=unavailable`.

### F3402: MEDIUM — MUI CSS build fails with duplicate @keyframes animation1
- **Files**: `frontend/src/index.tsx`
- **Status**: WONTFIX / CURRENT_SOURCE_NON_ISSUE / FRONTEND_BUILD_PASS

- **Fix**: Wrap the app root in `<StyledEngineProvider injectFirst>` in `index.tsx`, or ensure the MUI CSS baseline (`@mui/material/CssBaseline`) is imported before any custom CSS.
- **Triage evidence**: Stale/current-source non-issue. Current `frontend/package.json` has no `@mui/*` dependencies, and static search finds no `StyledEngineProvider`, `CssBaseline`, or `@mui` runtime imports in `frontend/src`. Current production build as the app user succeeds: `runuser -u guhao -- npm run build` ✅. The build output reaches `Compiled successfully` and emits only existing Browserslist stale-data and bundle-size advisories; no `Duplicate local keyframes are not supported: animation1` diagnostic appears.

### F3403: HIGH — @adobe/css-tools SyntaxError breaks all 42 Jest test suites
- **Files**: `frontend/package.json`, `frontend/src/setupTests.ts`
- **Status**: SOURCE_FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / TARGETED_JEST_PASS
- **Fixed**: 2026-06-08 12:50 UTC
- **Description**: All 42 Jest test suites fail with `SyntaxError: Unexpected token '.'` at line 206 of `@adobe/css-tools/dist/index.cjs`. The file uses optional chaining (`?.`) which the default Jest transform does not handle. This is a transitive dependency of `@testing-library/jest-dom` or similar.
- **Fix**: Add `@adobe/css-tools` to Jest's `transformIgnorePatterns` exception list so it gets transpiled, or pin a compatible version that doesn't use optional chaining.
- **Fix evidence**: Current `frontend/package.json` pins `@adobe/css-tools` through `overrides` and includes it in Jest `transformIgnorePatterns` exceptions: `node_modules/(?!(@adobe/css-tools|@testing-library)/)`. A representative Jest suite that loads the normal test setup and `@testing-library/jest-dom` now passes: `npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/components/SearchBar.test.tsx --watchAll=false --runInBand` ✅. The test run has only the existing React act deprecation warning and no `@adobe/css-tools` `Unexpected token '.'` failure.
